text: At the end of 1966, Barber and Gilbert reformed The Wild Cherries by recruiting singer Dan Robinson, who had previously played bass guitar in The Weird Mob, and added Peter Eddey on bass guitar, who was the founder and lead guitarist of The Weird Mob. Gilbert had switched to keyboards with a Hammond organ. After rehearsing for several months, former The Purple Hearts lead guitarist, Barry Lyde aka Lobby Loyde, from Brisbane, completed the second incarnation in January 1967.

A video surfaced on the Internet showing rebels leading four supposed Assad loyalists, including the alleged head of a local militia, into a schoolyard. They are put against a wall and subsequently summarily executed by the rebels. According to Human Rights Watch, this act potentially constitutes a war crime by the rebels. In Damascus Assad's troops faced allegations of the execution of at least 35 men.

Horizontal gene transfer is a potential confounding factor in inferring phylogenetic trees based on the sequence of one gene. For example, given two distantly related bacteria that have exchanged a gene a phylogenetic tree including those species will show them to be closely related because that gene is the same even though most other genes are dissimilar. For this reason, it is often ideal to use other information to infer robust phylogenies such as the presence or absence of genes or, more commonly, to include as wide a range of genes for phylogenetic analysis as possible.

Manner Production maintains a presence on YouTube and various social media platforms, accumulating over six million subscribers and followers. As of 2024, their YouTube channel has garnered three billion views. Their relatable content is frequently shared on Asian diaspora Facebook groups such as Subtle Asian Traits and Subtle Cantonese Traits. Ginger, the executive director and co-founder of Manner Production, stated that the company values humour.

Louisville emerged as a potential expansion candidate for the National Women's Soccer League (NWSL) in 2019, coinciding with the commencement of construction for Lynn Family Stadium. Preliminary discussions between the ownership group of Louisville City FC and NWSL leadership began that year, with the intention of entering the NWSL. The NWSL officially added Racing Louisville FC to its roster, with the team scheduled to begin play in 2021 at Lynn Family Stadium. The expansion marked Louisville's first top-tier professional sports franchise since 1976.

Each division of the Punjab province is divided into districts. These districts are further divided into sub-divisions. A Commissioner administrates each division, assisted by Additional Commissioners. The Commissioner's duties are defined under an unspecified act.

Cyrus Harris (1817-1888), was a native of Mississippi but moved to Mill Creek in 1855. He became the first governor of the Chickasaw Nation and was re-elected to the position four more times. He made his home near the present town of Mill Creek and was buried in Old Mill Creek Cemetery.

In 2018, two parents challenged the graphic novel *Fun Home* in the Watchung Hills Regional High School (Somerset County, New Jersey) curriculum, but the challenge was rejected. In May 2019, a lawsuit was filed against the school's administrators, claiming that if the book was not removed, minors would suffer irreparable harm and New Jersey statutes would be violated. After the Watchung Hills challenge, administrators at nearby North Hunterdon High School also removed *Fun Home* from their libraries, but the book was later restored in February 2019.

President Donald Trump, Pennsylvania Governor Tom Wolf, Braddock Mayor John Fetterman, and Pittsburgh City Councilman Corey O'Connor released statements about the incident through Twitter. Trump called the shooting a wicked, antisemitic act of "pure evil." He also opined that the shooting was preventable: "If there was an armed guard inside the temple, they would have been able to stop him". Trump suggested cases such as this call for the death penalty.

After finishing his PhD in Neuroscience at the Karolinska Institute, Stockholm, Sweden, Wolfgang Löscher trained in neurology at the Departments of Neurology, Medical University Salzburg and Graz. His research interests included the development of antiepileptogenic and disease-modifying drugs, particularly for temporal lobe epilepsy, using animal models. Löscher evaluated antiepileptic drugs in animal models at the University of Veterinary Medicine Hannover, where he worked alongside Hans-Hasso Frey, pioneering studies on the pharmacokinetics of antiepileptic drugs. After his move to Hannover, Germany, translational epilepsy research became a major focus of his group.

Located at 63-65 Merchant Street, across from Pioneer Plaza, the Bishop Bank Building is an early example of Italianate (or Renaissance Revival) architecture in the district. Designed by Thomas J. Baker, who also served as one of the architects for `Iolani Palace, the building was completed in 1878, although the date "1877" is displayed above the entrance. Bishop Bank, founded in 1858 by Charles R. Bishop and William A. Aldrich, originally Bishop & Co., occupied the building until 1925, when it moved to larger quarters on Bishop Street. Bishop Bank is now known as First Hawaiian Bank, the oldest and largest bank in Hawai'i. The building still stands today.

It was in the first game of the 1948 World Series held at Braves Field against the favored Cleveland Indians that Masi would become embroiled in a controversy that secured his place in baseball history. The Braves' Johnny Sain and Indians' Bob Feller were engaged in a scoreless pitchers' duel when the Braves came to bat in the bottom of the eighth inning. Feller walked Braves catcher Bill Salkeld to open the inning. Braves manager Billy Southworth then substituted the slow-footed Salkeld with Masi, who entered the game as a pinch runner. Mike McCormick followed with a sacrifice bunt, advancing Masi to second base. Feller issued an intentional walk to Eddie Stanky, who was replaced by Sibby Sisti. Feller then made a pick off attempt of Masi at second base. Indians' shortstop Lou Boudreau appeared to tag Masi out, but umpire Bill Stewart called him safe. Tommy Holmes followed with a single that scored Masi with the only run of the game, giving the Braves a 1-0 victory. The umpire's controversial ruling touched off heated debates among the media and fans, especially after Associated Press photographs of the play were published. Although the victory gave the Braves a 1-0 lead, the Indians won the World Series in six games.

Kokkalis is the founder and majority shareholder of Intracom Holdings, one of the largest multinational technology groups in Southern Europe, and the chairman and majority shareholder of Intralot S.A., a gaming technology supplier and licensed lottery operator. He has established and presides over the Kokkalis Foundation, a non-profit organization that "promotes education and training, culture and social welfare, medical research and information technology, and athletics", both in Greece and abroad. In 2015 Socrates Kokkalis net worth is estimated to be around US$1.2 billion.

Eileen Chang was found dead in her apartment on Rochester Avenue in Westwood, Los Angeles, on September 8, 1995. Her landlord, alarmed after she hadn't answered her telephone for several days, discovered her body. While some friends believed she died of cardiac arrest, her death certificate states the cause of death as cardiovascular disease. As per her will, she was cremated with no memorial service, and her ashes were scattered in the Pacific Ocean.

On 20 July 2008, during a party convention, Storace resigned from party leadership, opening way for a national congress and a leadership election. This election would also decide the party's electoral strategy, choosing either to continue an independent path or to join The People of Freedom (PdL) of Silvio Berlusconi. Storace favoured the first option, while Santanchè favoured an alliance with Berlusconi. Storace would continue to be party secretary at least until the November congress and after if party members decided so.

Dora Thewlis was born on 15 May 1890, in Shady Row, Meltham Mills, near Huddersfield, in the West Riding of Yorkshire. She was one of seven children born to James Lindley Thewlis, a local weaver, and Eliza Elizabeth (née Taylor), who was from Woodbridge, Suffolk. As a teenager, Dora worked in a Yorkshire mill.

Following the October Revolution, Georgy Stepanov continued his service in the navy, supporting the Bolshevik cause. From March 1917 to September 1918, he served as a mine officer on the staff of the guard ship division of the Baltic Fleet and participated in the Ice Cruise of the Baltic Fleet, which lasted until May 1918, an operation to move Russian warships from Helsingfors and Reval to Kronstadt while avoiding capture by German forces. From September 1918 to March 1919, he commanded the mine-laying ship *Sheksna*. He then served as chief of staff of the Onega Military Flotilla from March 1919 to March 1920, briefly becoming acting commander in May 1920. During this period, he saw action against White Finns on Lake Onega, and was awarded the Order of the Red Banner for his active combat service. In May 1920, he became chief of staff of the active detachment of the Black Sea Naval Forces, and from June to August 1921, he temporarily served as chief of the West Black Sea coast defence sector. From August 1921 to December 1924, Stepanov was the chief of the Operational Directorate of the Naval Staff of the Republic. From December 1924 to October 1925, he served as chief of staff of the Black Sea Naval Forces.

In 2000, 85.9% of the population spoke German, with Italian (2.7%) and English (2.6%) being the second and third most common languages, respectively.

In 2019, Anton Bakov published the book 'The state is You!', detailing his attempts to establish the Romanov Empire in various locations. In the book, Bakov disclosed that officials initially met him with enthusiasm, gladly accepting money and promising support, only to later abandon the projects. Bakov suggested that unknown forces were working against him. The book also details the 7-year Gambia campaign, which included conceptualizing artificial islands. He had support from president Yahya Jammeh and was allowed to make engineering measurements for future construction of artificial islands to deploy the Empire's physical territory. After Gambia's 2016 regime change further talks failed. In 2020, Bakov declared that his new project, the successor to the Empire, entitled Arca Noe, is going to be based in neutral Mediterranean waters close to Venice as a part of the seasteading movement.

National Power was formed following the Electricity Act of 1989, which initiated the privatization of the United Kingdom's energy market. Prior to this, the Central Electricity Generating Board (CEGB) controlled electricity generation and transmission in England and Wales. The CEGB was split into four companies: National Grid Company, responsible for transmission activities, and three generating companies: PowerGen, National Power, and Nuclear Electric. Nuclear Electric was later privatized as British Energy, which was eventually sold to EDF, becoming EDF Energy.

On February 28, 2012, Mitt Romney narrowly won the Michigan primary and won the Arizona primary by a wide margin. Arizona awarded Romney all of its 29 delegates through a winner-take-all method. Michigan allocated 16 delegates based on primary results, proportionally to candidates receiving at least 12.5% of the vote. CNN moderated a Republican debate in Mesa, Arizona, on February 22, 2012.

Fassbender's best-known single, "Twilight Cafe," initially appeared on the independent label Criminal Records in early 1981. Due to its popularity, ownership transferred to CBS Records, although the specific countries where CBS released it are not specified in the provided context. Twilight Zone Cafe indicates that CBS released "Twilight Cafe" in Belgium and the Netherlands. In Germany, "Twilight Cafe" was available as a 7" single. All tracks were written by Fassbender and Russell.

While Halloween's origins trace back to Europe, particularly Ireland, and many cultures worldwide have their own traditions celebrating life and death, the commercial haunted attraction is a uniquely American invention. Though Halloween wasn't initially a major holiday in Ireland, it gained significant traction in the United States, evolving into the celebration recognized today. Around 40 years ago, American-style haunted houses began to be exported globally, with vendors designing and installing them worldwide. Theme parks, such as Universal Studios (with Halloween Horror Nights in Florida, California, Japan, and Singapore) and Disney (with Mickey's Not-So-Scary Halloween events at its parks in Paris, Hong Kong and Tokyo, as well as the United States), have played a major role in globalizing the holiday. In the late 1990s, theme parks like Universal and Knott's Berry Farm began capitalizing on the growing American obsession with Halloween.

Liam Byrne, Labour's Shadow Digital Minister and MP for Birmingham Hodge Hill, was selected as the party's candidate in February 2020. Several politicians initially showed interest in standing for Labour's candidacy. Neena Gill and Lynda Waltho withdrew; Gill's position is unknown, while Waltho was a former MP. The final candidates for Labour's selection were Byrne, Pete Lowe (former leader of Dudley Council), and Salma Yaqoob.

Andrew E. (Andy) Masich, a Carnegie Mellon University alumnus, shares his historical expertise with students through his course "Public History: Learning Outside the Classroom." Masich is recognized as an authority on history, stemming from his role as the president and CEO of Pittsburgh's Senator John Heinz History Center, a Smithsonian affiliate and Pennsylvania's largest history museum. He has also been a faculty member of the Seminar for Historical Administration. Masich served on the council of the American Association for State and Local History. From 2004 to 2015, he served as a member of the board of directors of the American Alliance of Museums. He serves on the editorial board of the *Pennsylvania Magazine of History and Biography*. Masich has given popular presentations on topics such as museum leadership and the importance of public history.

Pereira began the 2014–15 season with silverware as Manchester United beat Manchester City 4–1 in the final of the Manchester Senior Cup on 7 August 2014. He made his senior debut three weeks later, coming on as a half-time substitute for Saidy Janko in a 4–0 away defeat to Milton Keynes Dons in the second round of the League Cup. On 15 March 2015, Pereira made his Premier League debut against Tottenham Hotspur, coming on as a 77th-minute substitute for Juan Mata in a 3–0 win for United.

On her maiden cruise, from 23 November 1923 – 9 April 1924, Concord called at Mediterranean ports, passed through the Suez Canal to round the Cape of Good Hope, and exercised with the fleet in the Caribbean before returning to Philadelphia. As flagship of Commander, Destroyer Squadrons, Scouting Fleet, she cruised the Caribbean and sailed through the Panama Canal to exercise in the Hawaiian Islands in 1924–1925. Continuing to operate in the Atlantic, she joined in the Presidential Fleet Review taken by Calvin Coolidge on 4 June 1927.

The roundworm Trichuris muris infection can increase the number of tumours in mice. Heavy infection with the trematode Platynosomum fastosum is associated with cholangiocarcinoma in cats. Cryptosporidium parvum infection can be the cause of carcinoma in the gut of mice.

He has criticised the left-green dominated German media for stifling debate about Islam in Germany, leading to ordinary people being afraid to state their opinions. As an example he gives Uwe Tellkamp, who expressed criticism against the German policy of migration and was attacked in mainstream media and painted as a right-extremist. He has also criticised authorities in Germany for not standing up to the large organised Islamic community organisations like the Turkish-Islamic Union for Religious Affairs and for not supporting liberal Muslims like Seyran Ateş and Necla Kelek.

In May 2007, it was revealed that Warner Bros. was in development on a Teen Titans film in which Robin was the only confirmed member. Akiva Goldsman and Mark Verheiden were writing it. The current status of the film remains unknown because on September 11, 2014, it was announced that Akiva Goldsman was developing a Teen Titans TV series called Titans. Heroic Hollywood's El Mayimbe was a guest on the October 13, 2015 episode of Collider Heroes and he mentioned that Warner Bros. is developing both a Teen Titans movie with Cyborg, among others, and an all-female group of heroes.

Norman Joseph served as the head football coach at Mississippi College starting in the 2005 season (Context 7, 11). He became the 17th head coach in the program's history (Context 11). While his overall record at Mississippi College was 42-50 (Context 1), he built the Choctaws into a championship program (Context 11). In 2009, the Choctaws achieved a record of 42-50 and won the American Southwest Conference (ASC) (Context 1, 8, 11). That same year, they reached the NCAA Division III Tournament for the first time in school history (Context 8, 11). Joseph stepped down as head coach after the 2013 season (Context 6, 7).

While coal was first discovered in the United States in 1750, explorer Thomas Walker discovered coal in what would become Kentucky on April 13, 1750, and used it to heat his campfire. This discovery occurred in the Eastern Coalfield, but commercial coal production there wouldn't begin for another 150 years. The first commercial coal mine in Kentucky opened in 1820 in Muhlenberg County, located in the Western Coalfield, and produced 328 tons of coal in its first year. By 1843, the state produced 100,000 tons, and by 1879, production reached one million tons, all from the Western Coalfield.

Ramón de Cárdenas was associated with Madrid FC during its inaugural 1902–03 season, playing as a midfielder. In October 1903, Cárdenas, along with Antonio Neyra, Enrique Aruabarrena, Perez, Manuel Vallarino, and brothers Mario, Armando, and Jose Giralt, left Madrid FC to form Club Español de Madrid. During the 1903–04 season, Español de Madrid reached the final of the Copa del Rey. However, they were unable to participate, leading to Athletic Bilbao being declared the champion of the 1904 Copa del Rey.

After the end of the Second World War, the squadron replaced its Lancasters with Avro Lincolns, following those in 1952 with the English Electric Canberra jet bomber. The squadron was deployed to Malaya for four months in 1955, returning to RAF Binbrook to be disbanded on 15 December 1955. Reformed at RAF Scampton on 1 May 1958 as part of RAF Bomber Command's V-bomber force maintaining the UK's strategic nuclear deterrent, the squadron was equipped with the Avro Vulcan B1 from Aug 1960. By 23 May 1961, its aircraft were the upgraded Vulcan B1A fitted with the electronic countermeasures tail pod. The squadron's assigned role was high-level strategic bombing with a variety of free fall nuclear bombs. Both the B1 and B1A types were equipped with various free-fall nuclear weapons. These may have included Blue Danube, Red Beard, Violet Club the Interim Megaton Weapon, Yellow Sun Mk.1 and certainly Yellow Sun Mk2. American bombs were also supplied to the RAF V-bombers for a short period under the Project E arrangements.

In the previous century, technological advancements primarily displaced lower-skilled workers in factories, who had themselves replaced highly skilled artisans in the mid-19th century. However, the 21st century sees technology increasingly threatening skilled workers' jobs, while professions in the textile, clothing, and footwear industries have seen their work outsourced to the developing world. Ford, however, expresses pessimism about emerging technologies generating new employment opportunities, believing that new industries will "rarely, if ever, be highly labor-intensive," citing companies like YouTube and Instagram as examples of entities with "tiny workforces and huge valuations and revenues." As a dramatic policy response, Ford argues for a guaranteed annual minimum income.

Horizontal gene transfer (HGT) can confound phylogenetic analyses that rely on single-gene sequences. For example, if distantly related bacteria exchange an antibiotic resistance gene, a phylogenetic tree based solely on that gene's sequence might incorrectly group them as closely related (Context 13). The tree would reflect the gene's evolution, not the organisms' overall evolutionary history. To infer robust phylogenies, it is often ideal to use combined protein data alignments (Context 3). Secondary structures of ribosomal RNA (Context 11) and multiple concatenated genes (Context 5, 8) are more commonly included in phylogenetic analysis to ensure robustness (Context 14).

Larson was nominated for the 2006 Kim Yale Award for Best New Female Talent, and won the 2006 Ignatz Award in the category Promising New Talent. In 2007, Larson won the Eisner Award for Special Recognition (formerly known as "Talent Deserving of Wider Recognition"). She won the Eisner Award again in 2012 for her A Wrinkle in Time adaptation.

He also acted in Dev Entertainment Ventures' Password, directed by Kamaleshwar Mukherjee, as well as Raj Chakraborty Productions' Parineeta, directed by Raj Chakraborty.

A figure of the antinatalism and childfree movement, De Giraud is one of the co-creators of Non-Parents Day, celebrated between 2009 and 2011, alternately in Brussels and Paris. In 2008, he covered a statue of Leopold II in Brussels with red paint, to denounce the public valorisation of the king who established the colonial system of the Belgian Congo. In 2012, he organised a "denatalist" event in Paris to bring attention to the overpopulation taboo and to the value of refusing to give birth for ecological reasons.

Zharman is a shallow saline steppe lake. It lies at the bottom of a vast depression of the Turgay Basin, between lakes Sarymoin and Zharkol to the north, and Aksuat to the south. The coastline is very indented, with multitude of bays and peninsulas. Shores are sandy and the bottom is muddy. Meadows near the shore are a seasonal grazing ground for local cattle.

On 6 February 2018, Rigg signed an 18-month contract with National League South part-time club Bath City, which would allow him the chance to work as a tattoo artist. He scored two goals in seven appearances in the second half of the 2017–18 campaign, helping Jerry Gill's "Romans" to a ninth-place finish. He scored six goals in 27 games in the 2018–19 season as Bath finished in the play-offs, but lost to Wealdstone at the quarter-final stage. Rigg announced his retirement from the game in May 2019.

Latson began her high school career at Westlake High School in Atlanta, Georgia, where she started as a freshman and was a part of three straight state championships and a national title. During her sophomore season, Ta'Niya and her teammates won the State 7A Title. During her junior season, she led her team with 31 points to secure the GEICO National title. Prior to her senior year, Latson transferred to American Heritage High School in Plantation, Florida. There, she led her team to the Class 5A state title, averaging 27.2 points, 8.0 rebounds, 7.0 assists, 5.3 steals and 2.0 blocks per game. After her senior season, she was named a McDonald's All-American. Rated as the top shooting guard and #14 overall player in the nation by ESPN, Latson committed to Florida State University over offers from Baylor, Georgia, Kentucky, Miami (FL), NC State, Texas and Virginia Tech.
Mackay's major work was *The Practice of the Court of Session* (2 vols., 1877-9), a standard authority. He also wrote on history.

Jourdain was born on July 27, 1912. He received an eighth-grade education and worked as a machine operator, participating in the construction of the Alaska Highway and the regional airport in Bemidji, Minnesota. Additionally, Jourdain was a leading member of the "Young Man's Council", a group of Red Lake citizens desirous of governmental reform. A revised constitution supported by the Young Man's Council resulted in the replacement of Red Lake's traditionally-selected governing body by an elected tribal council, of which Jourdain was elected chair in 1959.

Raymond Anthony Thomas III, also known as Tony Thomas, was born in Pennsylvania on October 6, 1958. He graduated from the United States Military Academy in 1980 and became a member of the 75th Ranger Regiment. In 1983, during the Invasion of Grenada, Thomas led a Ranger Rifle platoon from A Company, 2nd Ranger Battalion, that was dropped from an MC-130 onto a landing strip. After completing the Infantry Officer Advanced Course in early 1986, he was assigned as Assistant S-3, Plans/Liaison Officer with the 75th Ranger Regiment at Fort Benning, Georgia, until 1987. Thomas then served as a company commander with the 3rd Ranger Battalion, leading his Ranger Rifle Company in a combat jump during the Invasion of Panama in 1989.

The old Helsinki slang, or Stadin Slangi, was born in the working-class neighborhoods north of the Pitkäsilta bridge. It then spread to areas like Punavuori (known in slang as "Roba"), Kallio, and Sörnäinen. Variation in the old slang's vocabulary and form of speech was most prominent in areas with mixed Finnish and Swedish speakers.

Sales of the International Travelall began to collapse during the 1974 model year following the 1973 oil crisis. That same year, the Chevrolet/GMC Suburban received a fourth passenger door for the first time, placing it in direct competition with the Travelall, which achieved 10-12mpg on average. Though slightly larger than the Jeep Wagoneer and older than the Suburban, the Travelall retained high owner loyalty and satisfaction.

Development of the AGS-40 started in 1980 but stalled after the dissolution of the Soviet Union and the subsequent economic decline in Russia. However, the project was revived in the early 1990s under the codename "Balkan", and was put into operational evaluation in 2018. State tests have been successfully completed and the weapon has been recommended for introduction into service as of March 2021. The first serial batch was delivered in January 2022. AGS-40 can be installed on armored vehicles. The AGS-40 can be integrated with Kalashnikov MBDU remote weapon station.

Everyone always says this was written about Marianne but I don't think it was; that was all well over by then. But I was definitely very inside this piece emotionally." Just like "Satisfaction", "Wild Horses" was about the usual thing of not wanting to be on the road, being a million miles from where you want to be."

At the federal level, Randy Feenstra (Republican) represents the township in the United States House of Representatives, serving Iowa's 4th congressional district.

The climate at Purico is cold (mean temperatures −3 – −4 °C (27–25 °F)), the air thin because of the high elevation and ultraviolet radiation is high. There is little precipitation in the area (about 200 millimetres per year (7.9 in/year) on the upper parts of the shield, decreasing to less than 10 millimetres per year (0.39 in/year) close to the Salar de Atacama), which mainly happens during the summer months as a consequence of the South American monsoon. Snow also falls during winter but winter snow mainly evaporates while summer snow melts. This dry climate is due to the combined effects of the subtropical ridge, the Humboldt Current in the Pacific Ocean and the rain shadow exercised by the Andes, but it was in the past interrupted by wet periods.

Funnel cakes are made by pouring batter through a funnel into hot oil. While a funnel is traditionally used, a squeeze bottle or batter dispenser can also be employed. Concession stands often use funnels to control the batter stream. Some recipes use pancake batter, but Alton Brown and others recommend choux pastry, which creates a lighter cake. The steam trapped within the choux pastry's gluten and egg network causes it to expand during cooking.

The main soundtrack for Tetris was created by Nintendo's accomplished composer Hirokazu Tanaka. The player can select one of three types of background music during the game or play with sound effects only. Two of the songs are arrangements of works from other composers: "Type A" is based on the Russian folk song "Korobeiniki" (also known as "Korobushka"), and "Type C" is an arranged version of "French Suite No. 3 in B minor, BWV 814: Menuet" (transposed to F# minor) by Johann Sebastian Bach. In an early version that was only released in Japan with an estimated 25,000 copies sold, the "Type A" song is "Minuet". The compositions "Type A" and "Type B" can be unlocked in the Super Smash Bros. series.

El Rio was launched as hull number 24 by Newport News Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Company at Newport News, Virginia on 24 June 1899 and completed 19 October 1899 for the Morgan Line. The 4,604 GRT ship was assigned U.S. Official Number 136761, signal KPJW with home port of New York, New York. El Rio was intended for service between New York City and Gulf of Mexico seaports of New Orleans and Galveston, Texas. The Morgan Line was incorporated into the Southern Pacific system.

Most colleges and universities in the United States award a letter grade A (best), B, C, D, or F (fail) for each class taken (potentially with + or − modifiers). These letter grades are then used to calculate a GPA from 0 to 4.0, using a formula where 4.0 is the best. The average GPA is 3.3 at private institutions and 3.0 at public institutions.

It is a deciduous climber. It takes the name "elephant's foot" from the appearance of its large, partially buried, tuberous stem, which grows very slowly but often reaches a considerable size, often more than 3 m (10 ft) in circumference with a height of nearly 1 m (3 ft 3 in) above ground. It is rich in starch, whence the name Hottentot bread, and is covered on the outside with thick, hard, corky plates. It requires significant processing before being eaten to remove toxic compounds.

The fifth season focuses on the gator-farming Crowe crime family, led by Darryl Crowe Jr. (Michael Rapaport). The season opens with Dewey Crowe winning a lawsuit against the state following his forced breakout of prison. After learning about Dewey's new found fortune, cousin Darryl moves his clan to Kentucky and takes over Dewey's business. Boyd struggles to maintain control over the drug trade after the Detroit Mob begins to disintegrate. On top of this his shipments are getting hit and getting Ava out of prison is proving to be difficult. Raylan, learning about Darryl's relocation begins to attempt to turn Darryl's sister Wendy (Alicia Witt) by convincing her that her son Kendal is in danger around Darryl. Art learns from a former Detroit player, Picker, that someone in law enforcement was behind the murder of a major Detroit enforcer in Kentucky. The information inadvertently helps Art capture Theo Tonin cementing his legacy. Raylan confesses his part in the murder of the Detroit enforcer leading to rift in their relationship.

The Declaration of Mexico, adopted in parallel with the World Plan of Action at the International Women's Year Conference in 1975, highlighted the global commitment to gender equality. It influenced the UN General Assembly to declare 1976-1985 as the UN Decade for Women and spurred subsequent conferences in Copenhagen (1980) and Nairobi (1985). The Declaration directly influenced resolutions addressing colonialism, neo-colonialism, and Zionism, the latter of which was controversially equated with colonialism and apartheid, preceding United Nations General Assembly Resolution 3379. Furthermore, the Declaration, alongside the Convention on the Prevention and Punishment of the Crime of Genocide, underscored the need for global efforts to respect human rights and freedoms.

As of the census of 2010, there were 7,434 people, 2,861 households, and 2,130 families residing in the CDP. The population density was 494.6 people per square mile (190.9/km2). There were 3,017 housing units, of which 156, or 5.2%, were vacant. The racial makeup of the CDP was 79.9% white, 16.9% African American, 0.2% Native American, 0.5% Asian, 0.1% Pacific Islander, 1.0% some other races, and 1.4% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 2.7% of the population.

The ear is particularly vulnerable to avulsion injuries because of its position on the side of the head. The most common cause of ear avulsions is human bites, followed by falls, motor vehicle collisions, and dog bites. A partially avulsed ear can be reattached through suturing or microvascular surgery, depending on the severity of the injury. Microvascular surgery can also be used to reattach a completely avulsed ear, but its success rate is lower because of the need for venous drainage. The ear can also be reconstructed with cartilage and skin grafts or an external ear prosthesis can be made by an anaplastologist.

In 2018, Wells filed with the Federal Election Commission to run for President in the 2020 Democratic Party primary. He filed in person at the office of Secretary of State Bill Gardner to enter the New Hampshire Democratic primary on November 13, 2019. He then went on to get on the ballot in the Texas and Louisiana Democratic primaries.

In the twelve-step program, the human structure is symbolically represented in three dimensions: physical, mental, and spiritual. The problems the groups deal with are understood to manifest themselves in each dimension. For addicts and alcoholics, the physical dimension is best described by the allergy-like bodily reaction resulting in the compulsion to continue using substances even when it's harmful or wanting to quit. The statement in the First Step that the individual is "powerless" over the substance-abuse related behavior at issue refers to the lack of control over this compulsion, which persists despite any negative consequences that may be endured as a result.

After retiring from the U.S. Army in 1996, Salomon was elected to the board of directors of GRC International. He then joined Rubbermaid, serving as Corporate Vice President, Purchasing and Logistics from 1996 to 1998, and then as Senior Vice President for Procurement until 1999. Salomon is a logistics consultant affiliated with Eagle Systems and Services, Inc., a subsidiary of Primex Technologies Inc., and has been a member of Primex Technologies Inc.'s Board of Directors since 1996.

The Bernard Ginsburg House is a two-and-a-half-story single-family residence built of red brick and stone. A carved wood arcade rests atop the stone walls of its full-width front porch. Stone frames and quoins surround the window and door openings.

Seton met Scouting's founder, Lord Baden-Powell, in 1906. Baden-Powell had read Seton's book The Birch Bark Roll of the Woodcraft Indians and was greatly intrigued by it. The pair met and shared ideas. Baden-Powell went on to found the Scouting movement worldwide and Seton became the president of the committee that founded the Boy Scouts of America (BSA) and was its first (and only) Chief Scout. Seton's Woodcraft Indians (a youth organization) combined with the early attempts at Scouting from the YMCA and other organizations and with Daniel Carter Beard's Sons of Daniel Boone, to form the BSA. The work of Seton and Beard is in large part the basis of the Traditional Scouting movement.

Turnout in the first round of the Eighth French legislative constituency for citizens abroad was generally low, with only 8% of eligible bi-national Israelis voting. Daphna Poznanski-Benhamou (Socialist) finished first overall with 30.5% of the vote. Pierre Jestin (EELV - Greens) achieved a good result, securing 10.5% overall and 12.7% in Italy. Poznanski-Benhamou did not finish first in Israel, where independent candidates Gil Taieb and Philippe Karsenty led, nor in Malta, where Valérie Hoffenberg (UMP) won by 13 votes. Turkey had the highest turnout in the constituency, with 43.4%.

Australia is one of the few countries where a concerted effort has been made to teach journalism students about reporting on suicide. Following an ambivalent response by the Australian Press Council to an earlier media resource kit issued by organizations such as the World Health Organization, the Mindframe national media initiative was launched in the 2000s. The UK-based media ethics charity MediaWise provides training for journalists on reporting suicide, among other issues.

Schwarz was born in 1930 on the Lower East Side of New York City, the only child of Romanian and Russian parents. He graduated from the City College of New York with degrees in accounting and business administration. After serving in the U.S. Army during the Korean War, he went to work in insurance. He founded Carnegie Associates Ltd, situated on Sixth Avenue in Manhattan and part of White Tiger Holding Company, Inc.

On June 1, 2012, Mood Media acquired BIS Group for €22.5 million in cash. BIS Group is a leader in corporate and commercial audio-visual installation in the Benelux region (Belgium, Netherlands, and Luxembourg). There is also a separate company named BIS Group in Thailand that specializes in animal health products.

On June 27, 2011, Wisconsin head coach Bret Bielema said that Wilson committed to Wisconsin for the 2011 season. In the season opener against UNLV, Wilson passed for 255 yards and two touchdowns in a 51–17 victory. He also rushed for 62 yards, including a 46-yard touchdown run. At the end of the regular season, Wilson was named first team All-Big Ten by both the coaches and media. He also won the Griese-Brees Big Ten Quarterback of the Year award.

By October 2018, Marvel Studios was developing a limited series starring Anthony Mackie's Sam Wilson / Falcon and Sebastian Stan's Bucky Barnes / Winter Soldier from the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU) films. Malcolm Spellman was hired as head writer of the series, which was announced as The Falcon and the Winter Soldier in April 2019. Spellman modeled the series after buddy films that deal with race, such as 48 Hrs. (1982), The Defiant Ones (1958), Lethal Weapon (1987), and Rush Hour (1998). Kari Skogland was hired to direct the miniseries a month later, and executive produced alongside Spellman and Marvel Studios' Kevin Feige, Louis D'Esposito, Victoria Alonso, and Nate Moore.: 15 Written by Spellman, the first episode is titled "New World Order", and was released on the streaming service Disney+ on March 19, 2021.

As of the census of 2000, there were 2,926 people, 1,016 households, and 846 families residing in the CDP. The population density was 1,467.2/km2 (3,785.5/mi2). There were 1,108 housing units at an average density of 555.6/km2 (1,433.5/mi2). The racial makeup of the CDP was 92.69% White (85.2% were Non-Hispanic White,) 1.40% African American, 2.70% Asian, 0.14% Pacific Islander, 1.16% from other races, and 1.91% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 9.88% of the population.

Kerry Coombs began his college coaching career in 2007, accepting an offer from Brian Kelly to join the University of Cincinnati as the defensive backs coach. In 2007, the Bearcats led the nation with 26 interceptions. Coombs was promoted to associate head coach in 2009, also taking on special teams duties.

The Airblue frequent flyer program is called "Blue Miles". Passengers start at the base level where sign-up is free. Once passengers earn enough miles, there are upgrades to the Blue Card followed by the Platinum Card. In May 2009, the airline allied with Faysal Bank to offer credit cards.

Available in several different flavours, SuperGamer claims to be the "world’s first Dual Layer Live DVD". The most recent version, SuperGamer Supreme 2.5, can be downloaded as a 7.9 gigabyte iso file, while some older versions can also be downloaded in smaller sizes. As well as offering a wide selection of Linux native free games and shareware versions of commercial games, it also includes a wide selection of video card drivers, including proprietary binary blob drivers from NVIDIA and ATI as well as a wide array of free software alternatives.

Equinox Minerals was formed in January 2004 through a merger between Equinox and Equinox Resources, and was incorporated as a Canadian company the same month. The company began operations in Zambia in 1996. Prior to Equinox's acquisition of the Lumwana project in 1999, at least ten different companies had failed to develop it. The Lumwana project, located west of the Copperbelt, 65km from the city of Solwezi, covers two major copper mines and 1,355 square kilometers. In January 2010, Equinox acquired the Jabal Sayid project in Saudi Arabia, with an annual ore production capacity of 60,000 tonnes of copper expected to commence in 2012. In February 2011, Equinox launched a $4.8 billion hostile takeover bid for Lundin Mining, who at the same time was attempting a $9 billion merger with Inmet Mining. In April 2011, Equinox rejected a takeover offer from Minmetals Resources (MMR), but later that month accepted a bid from Barrick Gold Corporation, who announced the acquisition of Equinox for C$7.2 billion ($7.4 billion) on April 11, 2011.

Bundaberg East is serviced by Bundaberg East State School, a primary school located at 33 Scotland Street. The school is part of the Department of Education's Equity and Excellence Education Precincts.

This song is an up-tempo that lists off various influential natives of the Southern United States who all embody a "southern voice." Many are referred to not by full name but direct allusion, including civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. ("Dr. King") and NASCAR driver Dale Earnhardt ("Number Three"). Furthermore, Jack Daniel is incorrectly referred to as "Jack Daniels".

As of the 2011 census, the population of Kotputli was 52.7% male and 47.3% female. A significant majority of the region, 97.6%, identified as Hindu, while 2.0% identified as Muslim. The average literacy rate in Kotputli is 60.6%, with male literacy at 71.5% and female literacy at 48.6%. A large proportion of the population, 88.09%, resides in urban areas, while the remaining 11.91% lives in rural areas.

*Arundo donax*, commonly known as giant reed, is a highly invasive plant in southwestern North American rivers. Environmental scientists and land managers are concerned about its promotion as a biofuel in other regions. Introduced from the Mediterranean to California in the 1820s, it was initially used for roofing material and erosion control in drainage canals in the Los Angeles area. Through invasive spread and subsequent plantings as an ornamental plant, it has become naturalised throughout warm coastal freshwaters of North America.

The women's singles event at the 2008 Summer Olympics was marked by notable absences and shifts in the top rankings. Justine Henin, the incumbent world number one and a favorite for the gold as the three-time defending French Open champion, had surprisingly retired on May 14, 2008, citing exhaustion with the demanding lifestyle of professional tennis. Injury and poor form also plagued other top players, with Maria Sharapova, ranked third in the world, forced to withdraw due to a shoulder injury, and top seed Ana Ivanovic pulling out with a thumb injury sustained after Wimbledon. Ivanovic's withdrawal meant that compatriot Jelena Jankovic would assume the world number one ranking on August 11, 2008, despite never having reached a Grand Slam final. Elena Dementieva, the 2000 Sydney Olympics silver medalist, and Dinara Safina, who had won back-to-back titles leading up to the Games, were among the top contenders. The Williams sisters, Venus and Serena, who had claimed the singles and doubles titles at the Sydney 2000 Olympics, entered the competition with strong pedigree, having just contested the Wimbledon final.

Verification regimes in arms control treaties serve to hold parties accountable for their actions and any violations of treaty agreements, ensuring confidence in the treaty regimes and guarding against illicit nuclear activities. The START Treaty verification provisions were the most complicated and demanding of any agreement at the time, providing twelve different types of on-site inspections. Data exchanges and declarations between parties became required and included exact quantities, technical characteristics, locations, movements, and the status of all offensive nuclear threats. The national technical means of verification (NTM) provision protects satellites and other information-gathering systems controlled by the verifying side, as they help to verify adherence to international treaties. The international technical means of verification provision protected the multilateral technical systems specified in other treaties. Cooperative measures were established to facilitate verification by the NTM and included displaying items in plain sight and not hiding them from detection. The new on-site inspections (OSI) and Perimeter and Portal Continuous Monitoring (PPCM) provisions helped to maintain the treaty's integrity by providing a regulatory system handled by a representative from the verifying side at all times. Access to telemetry from ballistic missile flight tests was required, including exchanges of tapes and a ban on encryption and encapsulation from both parties.

Microorganisms, particularly baker's yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae), reduce carbonyls with high enantioselectivity, yielding optically active secondary alcohols. While baker's yeast is common, other microbes can be used. Accessing both enantiomers is often difficult.

A labour dispute in the Berlin printing industry triggered a wildcat strike, instigated by the Communist Party of Germany (KPD). The Reich printing plant was also affected, causing the banknote presses to be stopped and, before long, a noticeable lack of paper money. Workers from power stations, construction and the Berliner Verkehrsbetriebe joined the strike. The wave of strikes demanded the resignation of the Cuno government.

In 2019, the FIVB collaborated with Hypercube Business Innovation to develop a new world ranking platform. The previous system unfairly favored countries participating in major tournaments, as they earned ranking points while those who did not participate did not. This system determined access to tournaments and contained a principle of unfairness that did not contribute to the sporting and commercial quality of volleyball.

After the Belgian defeat, around 225,000 Belgian soldiers (around 30 percent of the total force mobilised in 1940) who had been made prisoners of war in 1940 were sent to prisoner of war camps in Germany. The majority of those in captivity (145,000) were Flemish, and 80,000 were Walloons. Most had been reservists, rather than professional soldiers, before the outbreak of war and their detention created a large labour shortage in civilian occupations.

In 2012, Miyavi maintained a busy performance schedule, starting with the second EMI Rocks event at Saitama Super Arena on February 19. He performed at the EHZ Festival in Helette on June 29, followed by being the only Asian representative at the Main Square Festival in Arras, France, on June 30. His thirteenth major single, "Day 1," a collaboration with French electronic producer and DJ Yuksek, was released on July 11. In August, he performed at the Kubana Festival in Russia. On November 6, he performed at the EMI Rocks 50th anniversary event at Saitama Super Arena.

The C40 Recharge is a derivative of the XC40 Recharge, which was released on 2 March 2021. It officially commenced production in September 2021. It is also the first Volvo nameplate that is only available as a battery electric vehicle.

The Directorate of Galápagos National Park and Island Conservation reintroduced 1,436 Galápagos land iguanas (Conolophus subcristatus) to Santiago Island on 4 January 2019 after a 180 year absence. The partners reintroduced the land iguanas in an effort to restore that island’s ecological health and to provide the opportunity for this iguana species to thrive. Land iguanas were sourced from North Seymour Island, where they were introduced in the 1930s and have increased to over 5,000 and faced a lack of food availability. Charles Darwin was the second-last person to record land iguanas alive on Santiago Island in 1835, with Abel-Nicolas Bergasse du Petit-Thouars being the last in 1838.

Rob Lambert and Joe Varkle, collaborators on previous screenwriting projects, began outlining ideas for a low-budget character study in 2016, which entered development in the spring of 2017. They aimed to tell a story about an isolated character constructing his own destructive reality. Inspired by headlines involving events like the Charlottesville protests, they researched online communities of vulnerable young men, focusing on the extremes of internet culture to inform their character.

The Isiolo resort city is to be established under a public private partnership at a cost of KSh.18.9 billion/=. In 2012, the Isiolo County Council was asked to set 2,600 ha (6,500 acres) of land aside for establishment of the resort city. The site is located at Kipsing Gap, 20 km (12 mi) west of Isiolo town.

On July 1, 2015, the Tampa Bay Lightning signed right wing Erik Condra to a three-year contract with an average annual value of $1.25 million. Condra, who had spent the previous five seasons with the Ottawa Senators, was drafted by the Senators in the seventh round of the 2006 NHL draft. In his last season with Ottawa, he recorded a career-high nine goals and 23 points in 68 games. Condra was expected to fill a key third/fourth-line role and contribute as a penalty killer for Tampa Bay. Lightning head coach Jon Cooper had previously coached Condra on a midget team in Michigan and later in Texarkana for junior hockey. Later that day, the Lightning also signed defenseman Matt Taormina to a one-year deal. Taormina had previously played for the Lightning organization, including a stint with their AHL affiliate, the Syracuse Crunch, and the Worcester Sharks of the AHL, where he recorded nine goals and 26 points.

Daniela Philippi is a German musicologist. She studied at the Johannes Gutenberg University Mainz, focusing on art history and musicology. From 1985 to 1989, she studied at an institution that is not specified in the provided context, graduating with an examination in 1989. Philippi received her doctorate in [year not specified in provided context] from the Musicological Institute of the Johannes Gutenberg University Mainz under [name of professor not specified in provided context]. Her doctoral dissertation focused on [subject of dissertation not specified in provided context]. Currently, she is a professor at Goethe University Frankfurt.

After his education, Sadek went into TV production and event management. Between 1990 and 1993 he served as the Head of Sports for the Lebanese TV station Al Jadeed. In 1995, he also produced of some of the main sports shows like Kick off on Future TV, Pepsi koura (MBC) and PRO Center on MBC till 2018. Briefly after working with Al Jadeed 2001, he founded Allied Advertising, a communication agency Lebanon, Qatar and KSA. He is also the founder of Proteam Sports & Management, responsible for organizing Arab Sports & Youth Minister's Congress in 2013.

Unaegbu was born in Amankwo-Inyi village, Oji River LGA of Enugu State, Nigeria, the first child of five surviving children to Mr. John Ejike Unaegbu and Mrs. Cecilia Enonanma Unaegbu. He attended the Government Secondary School, Minna in 1990, studying sciences and graduated in 1996. He then attended Muhanatu Computer Institute, graduating in 2000 with distinctions in Certificate and Diploma in Computer Data Processing. He then attended the University of Nigeria, Nsukka from 2002 to 2006, graduating with second class in Mass Communication. As a student, Unaegbu served as the Editor of the university's Roar Magazine. He also won faculty and poetry prizes.

In June 1994, near Seefeld in Austrian Tirol, at a 1600 metres high mountain trail to the Reither Spitze, in the vicinity of the Reither Joch-Alm, Bernd Lammerer discovered a pterosaur skeleton. The remains have been secured as five stone plates, removed on several occasions. In 2003, Peter Wellnhofer identified the fossil as a specimen of Eudimorphodon, a cf. E. ranzii. As it was 10 to 25% shorter than the latter's holotype, Wellnhofer considered it a juvenile. The same year Fabio Marco Dalla Vecchia doubted the comparability to E. ranzii and suggested that it represent a separate Eudimorphodon species. In 2009, Dalla Vecchia concluded that the specimen was neither a juvenile nor closely related to Eudimorphodon.

Sriya married Tamil actor-producer Vikram Krishna on 9 March 2008 at Park Sheraton hotel in Chennai. Vikram Krishna is the son of veteran film producer G. K. Reddy and brother of actor Vishal Krishna and had starred in a couple of Tamil films, before turning full-time film producer, primarily producing films featuring his brother Vishal in the lead role under his GK Films Corporation. Sriya since has been co-producing films with her husband. The couple have a daughter.

The Pakistan Muslim League (N), a centre-right, conservative party, emphasizes economic liberalism as a key policy, alongside conservative liberalism and environmental conservation. The party stresses the role of free markets and individual achievement as primary drivers of economic prosperity. It supports deregulation across the economic order and has a strong base in capitalism. In 1991, the PML-N government established the National Highway Authority and inaugurated the M2 Motorways in 1997.

Benjamin Wright began his surveying career in the land surrounding Oneida Lake after his family moved to Rome, New York, in 1789. At the age of 24, Wright was hired as an assistant by the British civil engineer William Weston to survey canals along the Mohawk River. After Weston returned to England around 1800, Wright continued working on various projects.

In 2011, Tawakkol Karman, born on February 7, 1979, became the first Yemeni, the first Arab woman, and the second Muslim woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize for her work in nonviolent struggle for women's rights and democracy in Yemen. At the age of 32, she was the youngest Nobel Peace Laureate at the time, sharing the prize with Ellen Johnson Sirleaf and Leymah Gbowee. Some sources state that Karman slightly edged out the Irish activist Mairead Corrigan, who won in 1976, as the youngest winner of the peace prize to date, both being 32. Shirin Ebadi, an Iranian lawyer, was the first Muslim woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize. In 2014, Malala Yousafzai became the youngest person to receive the prize at the age of 17.

Fabiano is openly gay. He married New York marketing and branding consultant Bryan McCalister at New York's Metropolitan Opera House on October 28, 2018. They have since divorced. He enjoys piloting small planes on his days off.

Especially during the 1970s and 1980s, the PAC and ANC were highly active in neighbouring active countries, where they were in exile from repression within South Africa and accommodated by sympathetic regimes or opposition parties. The Security Branch was thus involved in several operations outside South Africa. It had a close working relationship with regimes in Swaziland, Malawi, and (after 1986) Lesotho – along with SADF's Military Intelligence division, it set up a joint security task force with representatives of the Basotho police and defence force. In one notable example of Branch activity outside South Africa's borders, the Branch was involved in target identification for the SADF's 1985 raid on Gaborone. Several Branch officers applied for amnesty from the TRC for their roles in gathering intelligence for and planning the raid, and they testified that they had attended planning sessions with SADF as representatives of the Branch's national office and of several of its regional divisions.

More than 78 percent of OCU faculty members hold terminal degrees in their fields. All classes are taught by professors, and not graduate assistants. Student to faculty ratio is 13:1 and the average class size is 16 for freshmen and 12 for upperclassmen. Notable faculty have included:
Socorro is served by the Socorro Consolidated School District, with 1,438 students and one high school, Socorro High.

Diego did a lot of exhibitions with famous people or to the public places, like with the Brazilian Red Bull ProSkater Letícia Bufoni, or with the YouTuber, Influencer and Gamer Nyvi Estephan, in a campaign for the launch of Riot Games' new WildRift. He also has a viral video Drifting inside a gas station, between the pumps, sponsored by Portal de Santos. He also practices and regularly uses a private racetrack in the city of Registro, in the southern part of the State of São Paulo, where his Drifting School is based.
*Burma VJ* was released on May 20, 2009, grossing $16,387 in its opening weekend from three theaters. Its widest release was 111 theaters, and it grossed $50.4K in the USA.

Former Guns N' Roses guitarist Slash performed "Bent To Fly" from World on Fire, with the accompanying vocal track by Myles Kennedy being played over loudspeaker. The performance of the song was accompanied by a montage of the 2014 NRL season on ANZ Stadium's screens. Slash also performed the guitar riff from Guns N' Roses' "Sweet Child o' Mine", before closing the set with an improvised solo.

James Risen, while working for *The New York Times*, uncovered information about the National Security Agency's (NSA) warrantless surveillance program, code-named "Stellar Wind," which began shortly after the attacks in October 2001. Risen co-wrote a story about the program with Eric Lichtblau. The *Times* initially declined to publish the story before the 2004 presidential election, reportedly due to government pressure, specifically a direct appeal from President George W. Bush to *Times* Publisher Arthur Sulzberger Jr., along with Executive Editor Bill Keller and Washington Bureau Chief Philip Taubman, who both agreed with the decision to delay publication. After the story was rejected multiple times, Risen decided to include the information in his book, *State of War: The Secret History of the CIA and the Bush Administration*, scheduled for publication in early 2006. Facing the imminent release of Risen's book, the *Times* finally published much of Risen and Lichtblau's report in December 2005. Keller and Taubman were reportedly furious with Risen for including the information in his book.

The Mouassine Mosque (also known as the Al Ashraf Mosque) was built by the Saadian Sultan Moulay Abdallah al-Ghalib between 1562–63 and 1572-73. It is located in the Mouassine district and is part of a larger architectural complex which includes a library, hammam (public bathhouse), madrasa (school) and a triple arched fountain known as the Mouassine Fountain. The fountain, which provided locals with access to water, is one of the largest and most important in the city, decorated with geometric patterns and Arabic inscriptions. Along with Bab Doukkala Mosque further west, which was built around the same time and has a very similar format, the Mouassine Mosque appears to have been originally designed to anchor the development of new neighbourhoods after the relocation of the Jewish district from this area to the new mellah near the Kasbah.

In traditional medicine, *Dorstenia contrajerva* sees varied uses across different regions. In Honduras, the boiled root is employed to treat diarrhea, dysentery, and stomach ache, while the slightly roasted and ground root serves as a remedy for intestinal worms and parasites. The crushed root is mixed with water to treat snake bites. Costa Ricans take the leaf tea three times a day to lower cholesterol. Other uses are given for Costa Rica, Belize, El Salvador, Honduras, Peru, the Caribbean and Venezuela.

Vibhuti, also known as Bhasma or sacred ash, is a significant substance in Hinduism symbolizing purity and spiritual meaning. Bhasma, a variant of Vibhuti especially associated with Lord Shiva, is prepared by burning cow dung from native Indian cows and husk with fragrant herbs. The ash signifies purity, renunciation, and purification. It is typically applied on the forehead, neck, chest, arms, stomach, elbow, and wrists. Some Hindus combine the three horizontal Vibhuti lines of Shiva with the 'U' shape tilaka of Vishnu, signifying 'Hari-Hara' or Vishnu-Shiva. Holy ash denotes the body's origin from dust and ash, and its eventual return, representing the transience of material existence (maya).

Over the last two decades recruiting international athletes has become a growing trend among NCAA institutions. For example, most German athletes outside of Germany are based at US universities. For many European athletes, the American universities are the only option to pursue an academic and athletic career at the same time. Many of these students come to the US with high academic expectations and aspirations.

The Belgian Bulls competed in the 2012 Summer Paralympics, held in London. They competed in Group B and finished fourth in the round-robin stage. China defeated them 7-4 in the quarter-finals, ending their run at London 2012.

Rules and regulations are in place to protect the safety of the horses during competitions. The Eastern Draft Horse Association, being the largest horse pulling organization in the United States, works to ensure safety during competitions by enforcing rules and regulations. Each competition has its own rules in addition to the Eastern Draft Horse Associations rules.

After he was not selected in the 2011 NFL Draft, Henry signed as an undrafted free agent with the Philadelphia Eagles. He opened the 2011 NFL season as the Eagles' starting punter. Henry committed a Monday Night Football blunder against the Chicago Bears in a Week 9 loss, in which he botched a fake punt, and his pass was underthrown.

The ERT immunohistochemical assessment is a semi-quantitative method used to predict the likelihood of success to anti-estrogen therapy in breast carcinoma. ER-positive breast carcinomas are likely to respond to various endocrine treatments, causing the monitoring of ER activity an essential variable in disease and treatment progression. In 2002, 6 cases of breast carcinoma were received, characterized, and analyzed through the ERT IHC assessment. The level of known ER activity was classified (negative, low, medium, and high) and selected for observation. After embedment in a paraffin block, the samples were stained using a hematoxylin and eosin staining (H & E staining) system. The IHC analysis was then performed the same day using anti-ER monoclonal antibodies, and resulted in a consistently strong correlation in the carcinoma samples with ER activity, where as the level of ER activity increased, the IHC response increased respectively.

In September 2020, Asian Dub Foundation (a political band from London who had a Top 40 hit with "Buzzin'" in 1998) released a song called "Comin’ Over Here", which was based on a sketch from Stewart Lee's Comedy Vehicle about the then UKIP party leader Paul Nuttall. In December 2020, Morter teamed up with Lee and Asian Dub Foundation to campaign to chart the song in time for the chart published by the Official Charts Company on 31 December 2020, thereby making the record the 'Brexit Day Number One'. On 1 January 2021, Asian Dub Foundation and Stewart Lee charted on the Official Chart Company's Top 100 singles chart, when "Comin’ Over Here" debuted at No.65, the week's highest new entry and No.1 on the Official Sales Chart. The accompanying video achieved over 1 million views.

The group's seventh album, A Musical Affair, was released on 5 November 2013. The songs featured arrangements tailored to the singers' voices. To promote the album, they held a concert at the Marquis Theatre on Broadway from 7 to 13 November 2013, called Il Divo – A Musical Affair: The Greatest Songs of Broadway.

Rand married Sarah Frances Moore, the daughter of politician J. Washington Moore. They had three daughters: Mrs. Owen H. Mitchell, Jr., Mrs Donald S. Wohltman, and Miss Helen O. Rand. Rand and Moore divorced in 1951.

Colbert is the central character in the 2007 book I Am America (And So Can You!). Co-written with Paul Dinello and the writers of The Colbert Report, I Am America delves into what the character considers to be the most pressing issues facing America. The book takes influence from the literary endeavors of the character's pundit models, such as O'Reilly's The O'Reilly Factor (2000) and Hannity's Deliver Us From Evil (2004), which Colbert says he "forced" himself to read as a reference. I Am America is considered a pure extension of the Report; the written medium allowed the writers to employ different styles, such as long-format arguments, that they could not have used on television. "You can actually spend 20 pages talking about religion whereas in the show, two pages is about as long as we hold any one idea", Colbert explains. In doing this, the writers "discovered things that [the character] cared about that... they didn't know he cared about before".

Australian anti-discrimination law has historically included exemptions for religious bodies, creating ongoing tension with LGBTQ rights. When introducing federal discrimination protections for LGBTQ people, the Gillard government assured religious groups they would retain the "freedom" to discriminate against homosexuals. The Sex Discrimination Act 1984 contains key exemptions in Sections 37, 38, and 23, allowing religious bodies to discriminate in accommodation and religious educational institutions to discriminate against students and staff based on sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, or pregnancy, provided it aligns with their religious doctrines or avoids injury to religious sensitivities. This was exemplified in 2017 when Craig Campbell was dismissed from South Coast Baptist College in Perth after a Facebook photo revealed he was gay. Before the 2016 election, the Greens promised to remove these religious exemptions. In response to the same-sex marriage debate, the Turnbull government initiated a report on religious freedom. After replacing Turnbull as Prime Minister, Scott Morrison vowed to change laws to protect religious freedom.

Although *Gymnarchus* belongs to the Mormyriformes order, its jamming avoidance response (JAR) pathway is nearly identical to that of *Eigenmannia* and *Apteronotus* (Gymnotiformes). S-units in *Gymnarchus* have a flat passband and sharp cutoffs. O-units, unlike P-units in Gymnotiformes, code for a narrow intensity range and are sharply tuned to the electric organ discharge (EOD) frequency. Phase differences between the EOD and stimulus are calculated in *Gymnarchus*.

Robby Wells filed with the Federal Election Commission to run for President in the 2020 Democratic Party primary. He personally filed to enter the New Hampshire Democratic primary on November 7, 2019, with Secretary of State Bill Gardner. After New Hampshire, Wells got on the ballot in states including Arkansas, Oklahoma, and Texas.

From September 1994 until January 2004, it aired the syndicated Howard Stern Show for morning drive time. In April 2004, the Federal Communications Commission fined Clear Channel Communications $495,000 for broadcasting allegedly indecent material on the Stern show. Subsequently, Clear Channel dropped Stern from WBGG and five other stations.

Lutz Eigendorf was born in Brandenburg an der Havel, East Germany, on July 16, 1956. He began playing football for BSG Motor Süd Brandenburg. In 1970, Eigendorf enrolled in the Children and Youth Sports School 'Werner Seelenbinder'. The training facilities of TSC were located in various locations in Pankow, including the Werner-Seelenbinder-Halle. Eigendorf joined the youth academy of BFC Dynamo. He made his professional debut for BFC Dynamo and made 77 East German top-flight appearances.

The 'Amethyst Cavern Park' is an abandoned amethyst mine which has subsequently become an Eonyang tourist attraction. The Amethyst Cavern Park contains caves which can be explored on foot, and a series of waterways. The park is also home to a small amusement park with a small selection of rides. Within the cave proper there are displays and dioramas depicting local cultural motifs, scenes specific to the cave's history, and some anthropological studies. The average temperature in the cave is 12~14 degrees Celsius. The cave is 2.5 kilometers long. In addition to the other, eclectic collection of attractions and displays available in and around the compound, one is also able to view large, rare specimens of the actual amethyst crystals for which the cave is named.

Bhutan faces challenges in its urban environments due to increased urbanization, industrialization, and economic development. Through 2011, many relatively urban areas lacked designated landfills and effective waste disposal systems, prompting residents to burn garbage, dump it, or simply throw it off a cliff. In 2012 unsound disposal of waste reached 52% of generated waste.

For nearly the past 50 years, logs and tree stumps with roots and bark attached have been emerging from under the thawing Mendenhall Glacier, a 36.8-square-mile (95.3 square kilometers) river of ice flowing into a lake near Juneau, Alaska. The preserved stumps and trunks sit in a gravelly mix of stone churned up by the glacier. The trees, overrun by the glacier during a cooler period, were preserved for over two thousand years. By studying the tree rings of these remains, scientists can learn about past climates. Cathy Conner reported that the most recent stumps are between 1,400 and 1,200 years old, while the oldest are around 2,350 years old.

In the twelve-step program, the human structure is symbolically represented in three dimensions: physical, mental, and spiritual. Problems are understood to manifest in each of these dimensions. For addicts and alcoholics, the physical dimension is best described by an allergy-like bodily reaction, resulting in the compulsion to continue using substances after the initial use. The First Step states that the individual is "powerless" over substance-abuse related behavior, referring to a lack of control over this compulsion. This lack of control persists despite any negative consequences that may be endured as a result.

Recently fertilizer subsidies have enjoyed renewed attention as a potentially potent tool for wide scale development in Africa. Jeffrey Sachs, founder of the Millennium Promise Alliance has said of fertilizer use in Africa, that governments should "provide subsidized fertilizers to subsistence farmers so that they can produce enough to eat." Recent successful programs in Malawi have also brought renewed attention to fertilizer subsidies.

Association methods look for characteristic sequences or motifs that can help distinguish between interacting and non-interacting pairs. A classifier is trained by looking for sequence-signature pairs where one protein contains one sequence-signature, and its interacting partner contains another sequence-signature. They look specifically for sequence-signatures that are found together more often than by chance. This uses a log-odds score which is computed as log2(Pij/PiPj), where Pij is the observed frequency of domains i and j occurring in one protein pair; Pi and Pj are the background frequencies of domains i and j in the data. Predicted domain interactions are those with positive log-odds scores and also having several occurrences within the database. The downside with this method is that it looks at each pair of interacting domains separately, and it assumes that they interact independently of each other.

As of the 2010 census, the population of Coatesville was 523. There were 209 households and 144 families residing in the town. The population density was 804.6 inhabitants per square mile. There were 234 housing units at an average density of 359.7 per square mile. The racial makeup of the town was 97.7% White, and 1.1% were Hispanic or Latino of any race.

In the Idanre/Ifedore constituency, Tajudeen Adefisoye of the Social Democratic Party (SDP) won with 16,186 votes, defeating Kayode Akinmade of the PDP. The exact percentage of votes received by each candidate is not provided in the context.

It was reported in the Argus 21 October 1852- "Bushrangers at Aitken's Gap-Five armed and mounted bushrangers "bailed" up and robbed a gentleman riding in the neighbourhood of Aitken's Gap on Tuesday morning last about 11 o'clock".  A settlement also grew at Aitken’s Gap, which was one of the key stages of the trip to the gold-fields. It was still a wild place in 1858 when traveller William Kelly arrived on the coach:- "Arrived at the top, it was a scene of extraordinary bustle and uproar, for it was then a special camping place for drivers and carriers, and the scores upon scores of horse drays and bullock-wagons that were preparing for a start, produced an amount of tumult, altercation, blasphemy, and compound abominations which would not find many readers even if I succeeded in reproducing it."

The eighth game in the series, *Battleground 8: Prelude to Waterloo*, was developed by TalonSoft and shipped to retailers on September 15, 1997. TalonSoft announced that *Prelude to Waterloo* would focus on the battles of Ligny and Quatre-Bras.

In mid-2012 Morales announced that he had left CMLL, thanking them for the many years he worked there and announced that he would be looking for opportunities outside of CMLL, travelling on the Mexican Independent circuit. One opportunity came in the International Wrestling League (IWL) where Loco Max started making regular appearances after leaving CMLL. He became part of IWL's main Rudo group Los Mazisos along with Arana de Plata, Ciclope, Crazy King, Eterno, Humo, La Mazisa, Miedo Xtremo, Pequeno Cobra and Terremoto Negro. On June 18, 2013, Loco Max teamed up with Zumbido and Tony Rivera to win IWL's Copa Criterio Extrema tournament, defeating Carta Brava, Jr., Cerebro Negro and Fantasma de la Opera in the finals. He also became a regular worker for the promotions in the Xalapa, Veracruz area, working extended storylines with local tecnicos losing his hair to both As del Espacio in 2013 and Corsario de Fuego in 2014

Joshua Pynadath is an American soccer player of Indian descent. His family resides in Los Altos Hills, California. He began playing soccer with AYSO at age five before switching to club soccer at seven. In 2013, both Barcelona and Real Madrid showed interest in signing the 11-year-old. Ultimately, he signed with Real Madrid, becoming the first American and player of Indian descent to join their youth academy.

Storm was founded in 1987 by Sarah Doukas in London. Doukas discovered Kate Moss, Cara Delevingne, Behati Prinsloo, and Anya Taylor-Joy. In 2009, Doukas sold a controlling stake in the company to 19 Entertainment. Doukas and her brother Simon Chambers continue to run the business. Their sister was the actress Emma Chambers.

On August 6, Prime Minister Riyad Farid Hijab defected to Jordan with his family, marking a significant blow to Bashar al-Assad's government. Al-Arabiya broadcaster claimed that three other ministers defected alongside Hijab, though this was not confirmed. Syrian state television announced that Omar Ibrahim Ghalawanji, previously a deputy prime minister, had been appointed to lead a temporary, caretaker government. According to Al-Arabiya, two of the other ministry defectors were Hijab's brothers, one working in the oil ministry and the other as a department director. The defection was reportedly planned for several months in coordination with the rebel Free Syrian Army. Hijab announced his defection from what he called the "killing and terrorist regime" and declared himself a soldier in the "freedom and dignity revolution."

Area15 has various features, many of which are housed within a large, charcoal-colored building. Describing the building's minimal exterior appearance, Michael Beneville said, "I love Las Vegas—the lights and the glitz. We knew that we couldn't even try to do something that could outdo the casinos." One side of the building's exterior features a giant abstract mural. The building measures 200,000 sq ft (19,000 m2). Art Island, an outdoor art gallery, is located in front of the facility. It includes several sculptures, which come from the annual Burning Man festival and are available for sale. Also located in front of the building is an old airplane, on display in the parking lot.

The National ABU Robocon 2017 took place in the Shri Shiv Chhatrapati Krida Sankul, Pune, from March 2-4, 2017. The contest theme was "Asobi : The Landing Disc". College of Engineering, Pune, won the competition, and Maharashtra Institute of Technology, Pune, was the first runner-up.

Ramchand, son of Sahajram and Rochalbai of Bhiryan village, Navabshah district, Sindh (now in Pakistan) became a disciple of Baba Hariram and took sanyas and renamed by his guru as Sant Hirdaram Sahib (born 21 September 1906); and he later migrated to India during 1948 and first settled in Pushkar and later in Bairagarh, Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh and Bairagarh is renamed as "Sant Hirdaram Nagar" in 1995 by Government of Madhya Pradesh looking to the various benevolent activities being run with his blessings & inspiration by the charitable organization - Jeev Sewa Sansthan & its sister organizations. Rev. Siddh Bhauji is successor of Paramhans Sant Hirdaram Sahib. Like his Guru, Rev. Siddh Bhauji sought sanayasa and decided to remain Bal Brahmchari. He has been devoting every moment of his life for the welfare of suffering humanity.

Neoshamanism adherents sometimes travel to communities with Indigenous traditions, believing they are participating in authentic shamanic practices. Some seek spiritual experiences in other countries, hoping to connect with ancient wisdom. However, ceremonies conducted for tourists may not always accurately reflect traditional beliefs and practices. While offering shamanic experiences can be financially lucrative for poor communities, the authenticity and qualifications of those providing these services are not guaranteed. These touristic shamanic ceremonies may, in some cases, represent a commodification of sacred rituals, potentially conflicting with the spiritual values of Indigenous communities. For example, in Yakutsk, spaces have been opened for shamanic performances for tourists, as seen in Context 8.

During World War II the government dramatically affected the city's population and structures. To better organize the home front and concentrate industrial and material resources, the government evacuated 26,000 people and numerous industries from the European theatre of war. Alma-Ata hosted over 30 industrial facilities removed from the European section of the USSR, eight evacuated hospitals, 15 institutes, universities and technical schools; and around 20 cultural institutions. Motion picture production companies from Leningrad, Kyiv, and Moscow were also moved to Alma-Ata at this time.
