Some countries have achieved higher levels of cost recovery, enabling them to rely on commercial credit financing. In Colombia, transfers to municipalities for water and sanitation are calculated based on specific criteria. Similarly, municipalities in Mexico must fulfill certain conditions to apply for matching federal grants. In Ecuador, transfers are allocated to municipalities based on specific criteria. The amount of transfers to utilities can vary from year to year. International financial institutions also play a significant role in financing water and sanitation projects in the region, providing loans and grants to support infrastructure development and improve access to these essential services. The need for increased investment in water and sanitation is significant, with estimates suggesting a $1 trillion USD market opportunity to provide universal access by 2030.

The Thompson Ice House stands on the east side of SR 129, just south of its junction with McFarlands Cove Road. It is set between the road and Thompsons Pond, a 3-acre (1.2 ha) freshwater pond. The ice house is a single-story wood frame structure, with a gabled main section flanked on its long sides by shed-roof ells. Its exterior walls are unpainted horizontal pine boards, and its interior walls are separated from the outside by a nine-inch gap filled with sawdust for insulation. Large doors on the east and west sides facilitate the movement of large ice cakes into and out of the building. The east (pond-facing) side has a channel which could be used to float the ice cakes into the building and onto conveyors that would move it into the storage area that occupies most of the building.

The Cape Ann Museum was founded in 1873 as the Cape Ann Scientific and Literary Association, with antecedents reaching back to the early 19th century and the cultural revival sweeping New England. The Gloucester Lyceum, organized in 1830, provided a platform for distinguished speakers like Ralph Waldo Emerson and Henry David Thoreau. Dr. Davidson, the first president of the Cape Ann Scientific & Literary Association, received a sketch of a "Dream Painting" from Fitz Henry Lane. In the early 1920s, the Association acquired its first permanent headquarters in the Captain Elias Davis House (1804) on Pleasant Street, which remains part of the Museum today. In 1926, the Association added "Historical" to its name, becoming the Cape Ann Scientific, Literary and Historical Association. The Cape Ann Historical Society had incorporated in 1892 to investigate, record and perpetuate the history of Cape Ann through the collecting and preserving of ephemera, documents, books and memoirs that illustrated local history and the individuals associated with it. In 2007, the Board voted to adopt the name Cape Ann Museum, with the tagline "Art | History | Culture."
He was the son of Edmund Lechmere MP and his second wife Elizabeth, daughter of Rev. John Whitmore. He matriculated at Merton College, Oxford in 1785 aged 18.

The French Covered Court Championships emerged as an open tournament held on the indoor courts of the Tennis Club de Paris, originally located at 91 Boulevard Exelmans in the Auteuil district. The club, wholly devoted to tennis, initially featured four indoor wooden courts and five outdoor clay courts. The tournament was typically staged in November or over the Easter weekend. While the exact original location remains obscure, the tournament moved to Rue de Civry in the 16th arrondissement of Paris shortly before World War I, and then to a new location shortly after World War II. The men's competition ceased in 1954, while the women's continued until 1970, running for a total of 75 editions. The French Covered Court Championships is an early predecessor of the modern day Paris Masters.

In his speech at the Urbanization Work Conference on December 12, 2013, CCP general secretary Xi Jinping said "When upgrading the urban drainage system, priority should be given to retaining limited rainwater and using the power of nature to drain water. Build a sponge city with natural retention, natural penetration, and natural purification." To this end, in October 2014, the Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development issued 'Technical Guidelines for Sponge City Construction,' emphasizing the importance of the top-level design of urban rainwater management, as well as planning to guide urban construction with ecological priority as the basic principle.

North Carolina's executive branch is governed by Article III of the state constitution. The first North Carolina Constitution in 1776 called for a governor and a seven member Council of State elected by the legislature. Currently, the ten-member Council of State of North Carolina includes the following members elected by voters:

During the 1990s, Fox River Grove experienced a period of economic growth that spurred housing development proposals. In 1994, six out of seven village trustees approved a luxury housing development planned for 38 acres near Route 31. The development, proposed by Fiduciary Real Estate Development, included amenities such as a dog park, pool house, and pickleball courts. The location, known as Picnic Grove, straddles an area that many nearby residents wanted to be reserved for single-family homes. The community was outspoken in advocating for single-family homes on the property instead. Developers might advance to the zoning process as early as this spring or summer, he said.

The M777 began in 1987 as the Ultralight Field Howitzer (UFH), developed by Vickers Shipbuilding and Engineering's (VSEL) Armaments Division in Barrow-in-Furness, UK. VSEL was bought by BAE Systems after the UFH prototypes had been manufactured and demonstrated, and consequently BAE became responsible for future design refinements and renamed the gun "M777". Upon taking over responsibility for the weapon, BAE "Americanised" to a large degree the construction and assembly through its US-based BAE Systems Land and Armaments group. The M777 now uses about 70% US-built parts including the gun barrel, which is manufactured at the Watervliet Arsenal.

On 6 June 2007, the UK Ministry of Defence (MoD) approved the private finance initiative (PFI) for the procurement of 14 A330-200 tankers from AirTanker. Following this announcement, AirTanker was permitted to release the surge fleet capability, when not required by the Royal Air Force (RAF), for other purposes, including the civil market or to partner nations. A 27-year PFI deal was agreed with AirTanker in March 2008, valued at £10.5 billion. The 14 converted A330 aircraft were delivered between September 2010 and July 2016 and will be operated until at least 2035. Since 2016, the UK has paid AirTanker £2 billion, which equates to an annual payment of £400 million.

Higher education in Venezuela exists within a complex framework of autonomy and government oversight. While universities, particularly the traditionally autonomous ones, strive to maintain academic freedom, the Ministry of Education, headed by the Minister, exerts considerable influence. Universities can supplement their budgets through private donations, patents, and copyrights. However, the Ministry oversees a large educational budget and the Minister ultimately holds responsibility for university management, including budget, inspection, administration, curriculum planning, infrastructure, and policy implementation. Public universities that do not fall under the government's control have had their funds stripped.

One of the hallmarks of China's socialist economy was its promise of employment to all able and willing to work and job-security with virtually lifelong tenure. Reformers targeted the labor market as unproductive because industries were frequently overstaffed to fulfill socialist goals and job-security reduced workers' incentive to work. This socialist policy was pejoratively called the iron rice bowl. The iron rice bowl in 1978 ended collective farming and was then replaced by the "household responsibility system". It provided an informal market for farmers to increase prices above government ceilings placed on goods.

The Orlando Branch Campus of Everglades University is located in a 28,000 square-foot building on Trafalgar Court, in Maitland, Florida. The campus relocated from Altamonte Springs to Maitland on July 7, 2015, and held a ribbon cutting ceremony which Maitland Mayor A. Dale McDonald attended. The facility houses offices, classrooms, a computer lab, a library, faculty lounge and a student lounge. The university expects that due to its proximity to the metropolitan Orlando area that the campus will quickly expand to 1,000 students, making it the largest Everglades University campus. The staff, faculty, and students at the Boca Raton Campus have participated in the Walk to Cure Arthritis, Making Strides Against Breast Cancer Walk, collecting toys for Toys for Tots, and hosted several blood drives with OneBlood.

The Charlotte Edwards Cup, first held in 2021, saw eight regional teams divided into groups compete in May and June. The two group winners and the best second-placed team advanced to Finals Day. The South East Stars won the final by seven wickets.

The 1991 Tour de France included teams of nine cyclists. The top 16 teams in the FICP ranking for teams in May 1991 qualified automatically. Following these qualifications, the Tour organiser gave six additional wildcards.

The St Peter's College houses are the basis of organisation in the school by year. Each year level has six house classes and each house class is the unit of attendance, pastoral care, competitive activity and many daily activities. There are house leaders (students) and house leaders (staff) assigned to each.

The Bretagne Classic, an elite cycling classic held annually in late summer around Plouay, France, boasts a rich history dating back to its creation in 1946. Originally known as the Circuit de Plouay, it was later named GP Ouest-France from 1989 to 2015. Created by a former blacksmith, the inaugural edition was won by Ange Le Strat. The race was dominated by French riders in its early decades. The first non-French winner was Rik Van Steenbergen in 1949, followed by Briek Schotte in 1950. Oliver Naesen is the only non-French rider to have won the Bretagne Classic two times, in 2016 and 2018.
Draft picks are not automatically signed to the team roster. The 2020 college draft was held on January 16, 2020. Orlando had seven selections.

During the cold season, extratropical cyclonic storms known as nor'easters bring a significant portion of wintry precipitation to the Northeastern United States. These storms typically form off the coast between Georgia and New Jersey before moving northward along the U.S. East Coast. While nor'easters impact the coast, lake-effect snow is prominent further inland in New York, with Lake Erie and Lake Ontario being large enough to produce significant snowfall. Syracuse, NY, is known for its high annual snowfall totals due to lake-effect snow from Lake Ontario. The Finger Lakes in upstate New York can also produce lake-effect snow until they freeze over. The Appalachian Mountains largely shield New York City and Philadelphia from lake-effect snow. Ocean-effect snows are possible near Southern New England, and bay-effect snows can occur in areas like Virginia and North Carolina when cold air is present.

John Key's National Party secured a third term in the general election held on 20 September 2014. The National Party won 47.0% of the party vote (Context 10) and secured 60 seats out of a total of 121 in Parliament (Context 1, Context 7). This was one less than the 61 seats the party appeared to hold on election night (Context 1, Context 7, Context 11), with the Green Party gaining a list seat at National's expense on the official count (Context 1, Context 11). Following the election, National re-entered a confidence and supply arrangement with other parties (Context 12).

The monobasic malonate:Na+ symporter, found in *Malonomonas rubra*, is composed of two essential integral membrane proteins, MadL and MadM. MadL consists of 129 amino acids, while MadM contains 255 amino acids. These subunits exhibit 4 and 7 putative transmembrane spanners, respectively. The transporter is believed to catalyze the electroneutral reversible uptake of H+-malonate with one Na+. A close two-subunit homologue exists in *Pseudomonas putida* (gbAB017138), encoded within the malonate decarboxylase gene cluster. The NapA Na+/H+ antiporter of *Enterococcus hirae* (spP26235; CPA2 family, TC #2.A.37) shows a distant relationship to MadM. MadM also interacts with BunA.

In 1816, the citizens of the village of San Cristóbal de Alvarado sought the designation of Town from Ferdinand VII, King of Spain. The King granted the village the title of Town on 8 September 1816 by Royal Decree, which had a cost of 92,800 maravedís. Almost two years later, on 5 September 1818 the same Ferdinand VII authorized the first Honorable Ayuntamiento or city council. On 3 October 1818, the first City Council was established with Tomás Hondal serving as first Mayor.

Ashill was originally called Asleigh, which meant a clearing in the Ash wood. The parish church of St Nicholas dates from the 14th century and stands close to the group of houses that form the oldest part of the village. The village centres on the green and a duck pond. Drovers travelling to Swaffham market would stay overnight on the green, using a shed as accommodation, whilst their cattle grazed on the green and drank from the pond.

Zentmyer was born in North Platte, Nebraska to Mary Elizabeth Strahorn and George Aubrey Zentmyer, Sr. While an undergraduate at University of California, Los Angeles Zentmyer was a sportswriter for the Bruin. He went on to graduate work at University of California, Berkeley. Both Zentmyer's master's and doctoral theses discussed the cytospora attacking the Italian cypress.

The Cambria Iron Company was founded in 1852 in Johnstown, Pennsylvania, for the sole purpose of rail production. In 1854, a group of Philadelphia merchants led by Matthew Newkirk purchased the iron works. A fire in 1857 led Newkirk to persuade his co-investors to rebuild the main rolling mill on a larger scale.

Wang Liang started his career with Liaoning F.C. making his debut in 1999. The following season he would establish himself as a regular playing in 17 games. He would eventually play for Liaoning for seven season before transferring to Shandong Luneng Taishan where he had a mixed period, winning the China Super League with them but unable to establish himself as a regular. He would move back to Liaoning after two seasons with Shandong on loan. Liaoning were relegated in the Chinese Super League 2008 season and Wang Liang returned to Shandong.

Warner Bros. Pictures announced a live-action Teen Titans film adaptation in May 2007. Mark Verheiden was brought on board to write the script, with Akiva Goldsman and Kerry Foster producing. Years later, in October 2015, news surfaced of a Teen Titans TV series called *Titans* being developed by Akiva Goldsman and Marc Haimes for TNT. On the October 13, 2015 episode of Collider Heroes, Umberto Gonzalez (El Mayimbe) was a guest, but the context does not mention what he said about Warner Bros.' development plans. The status of the original Teen Titans film is unknown.

On December 5, 2009, the Diocese of Los Angeles elected openly lesbian Mary Douglas Glasspool as a suffragan bishop. Her consecration took place on May 15, 2010. In April 2010, leaders from 20 Anglican provinces met in Singapore and declared that Glasspool's election and intended consecration demonstrated a disregard for the mind of the Communion.

Kanai's Cube was added to *Diablo III* in patch 2.3, and is named after the late Blizzard artist Kevin Kanai Griffin. It serves as an improvement over the Horadric Cube from *Diablo II*, granting players the ability to break down and convert legendary items to gain bonuses. To acquire the Cube, players must first find it in Adventure Mode. The ghost of Zoltun Kulle directs players to the Ruins of Sescheron in Act III. The first character in a new Season must travel to the Ruins of Sescheron and locate the Cube within the Elder Sanctum. Once found, the Cube can be used to extract legendary powers from items, adding their affixes to the Cube. These legendary powers are marked by orange text in an item's stats.

The team competed at the 2019 Parapan American Games from 23 August 2019 to 1 September 2019, at the Miguel Grau Coliseum, Lima, Peru. This championships was a qualifier for the 2020 Paralympic Games. There were eight men's teams: Argentina, Brazil, Canada, Guatemala, Mexico, Peru, USA, Venezuela.

Keppel Sands is serviced by Keppel Sands State School, a small rural primary school. Located in the coastal township, the school is approximately 40 kilometers from Rockhampton. As of 2023, the school is structured into two multi-age classes, with a dedicated teaching Principal, a part-time teacher, a full-time Teacher Aide, a full-time Business Manager, part-time cleaner, and part-time groundskeeper.

Eoporpita’s namesake is Porpita, a genus of hydrozoans in the family Porpitidae. When describing Eoporpita in 1972, Mary Wade noticed slight similarities in their affinities. However, Wade ultimately decided that Eoporpita’s ‘tentacles,’ which she interpreted as dactylozooids and gonozooids, differentiated the two enough.

Broad is the author or co-author of eight books, including Germs: Biological Weapons and America's Secret War (Simon & Schuster, 2001) which was a number-one New York Times Best Seller. His other titles include Betrayers of the Truth: Fraud and Deceit in the Halls of Science (co-written with Nicholas Wade, Simon & Schuster, 1982), Teller's War: The Top-Secret Story Behind the Star Wars Deception (Simon & Schuster, 1992), The Universe Below: Discovering the Secrets of the Deep Sea (Simon & Schuster, 1997), and The Science of Yoga: The Risks and the Rewards (Simon & Schuster, 2012). His books have been translated into more than a dozen languages. His work focuses on the social repercussions of science.

Generalbezirk Estland was the last of the four districts to be formally established on 5 December 1941. It was organized on the territory of German-occupied Estonia, which had until then been under the military administration of the Wehrmacht's Army Group North. The capital of Generalbezirk Estland was Tallinn (Reval).

The design of the medal used a portrait of Professor Hawking by cosmonaut Alexei Leonov, the first man to perform a spacewalk and member of the Advisory Board of Starmus since its first edition. The other side combines the image of Alexei Leonov during the first spacewalk, and the “Red Special” – Brian May’s guitar – to demonstrate music, another major component of the Starmus Festival. The Medal itself was designed by Alexei Leonov and Brian May.

Following the German invasion of Poland in September 1939, the Jewish population of Wyszków, including Berek Lajcher and his family, were expelled from the town. Some older Jews and Poles were locked in a barn and burned alive. Later that month, the Nazis shot almost all of the Jewish men in Wyszków. The Lajcher family relocated to Węgrów, where Berek established a medical practice around the summer of 1940. He also became a member of the Jewish Council and the Aid Committee, organizing a folk kitchen, hospital, and bath to combat typhus. In February 1941, the Germans imposed a ban on the Jewish population to leave the city.

In 2010, Kevin Cramer won election to the North Dakota Public Service Commission on November 2. This was not his first time running for North Dakota's seat in the United States House of Representatives, as he had lost elections to the At-Large District three times before winning in 2012. During this time, Cramer, who was a member of the North Dakota Public Service Commission, spoke at events in North Dakota about energy, taxes, jobs, and the U.S. Constitution. In March 2010, Rick Berg, who would be elected to Congress in November 2010, won the Republican congressional nomination.

An elaborate system of canals cut from the Murghab irrigates the oasis. The country is renowned throughout the East for its fertility. Every kind of cereal and many fruits grow in great abundance, e.g. wheat, millet, barley and melons, also rice and cotton. Cotton seeds from archaeological levels as far back as the 5th century are the first indication that cotton textiles were already an important economic component of the Sassanian city. Silkworms have been bred. Turkmens possess a famous breed of horses (Turkoman horse) and keep camels, sheep, cattle, asses and mules. Turkmens work in silver and armour. One discovery of the 1990s excavations was a 9th- to 10th-century workshop where crucible steel was being produced, confirming contemporary Islamic reports by Islamic scholar al-Kindi (AD 801–866). He referred to the region of Khorasan as producing steel. This was made by a co-fusion process where cast iron and wrought iron are melted together.

A total of 2 candidates registered with the Independent National Electoral Commission to contest in the election. SDP candidate Tajudeen Adefisoye won the election, defeating PDP Kayode Akinmade. Adefisoye received 53.28% of the votes, while Akinmade received 46.72%.

Born and raised in Ottawa, Ontario, Charette attended Carleton University, where she completed a Bachelor of Commerce degree. Charette is married to Reg Charette. Together they have two adult children, Jed and Cassie.

The Royal Botanic Garden consist of 29 hectares (72 acres) of closely cultivated land surrounded by 51 hectares (130 acres) of open parklands comprising The Sydney Domain. The Garden forms a large natural amphitheatre, wrapped around and sloping down towards the 'stage' of Farm Cove. It is divided into four major precincts called the Lower Gardens, the Middle Gardens, the Palace Gardens and the Bennelong precinct. Within the four major precincts are many smaller gardens and features as well as large amounts of lightly wooded lawn areas. Located approximately in the middle of the four precincts is the Palm Grove Centre which offers a restaurant, cafe, visitors centre and bookshop. A large and complex public botanic garden, largely of late 19th-century character; being not only an historic site of the first importance but containing within it numerous structures which have been nominated separately by the National Trust of Australia (NSW). A predominantly nineteenth century character of landscape layout strengthened by large mature trees. Traditionally designated as four areas reflecting its development.

Rick and Morty holds an average approval rating of 95% on review aggregator website Rotten Tomatoes for the entire series. David Weigand of San Francisco Chronicle described it as "offbeat and occasionally coarse... the take-away here is that it works". He praised the animation direction by James McDermott for being "fresh, colorful and as wacky as the script", and states that the series possesses "shades of Futurama, South Park and even Beetlejuice", ultimately opining that its humor felt "entirely original". Neil Genzlinger of The New York Times praised the series and stated that it was "Grandparenting at its unhinged finest." In a review shortly after the second-season premiere, Sean Sebastian of Junkee said that although the crude animation and over-the-top voice acting might have discouraged some viewers to continue watching, Rick and Morty is "exceptionally well-made" and that "the more you dissect it, the more you find it has to say."

The Cambria Iron Company was founded in 1852 to provide iron for the construction of railroads. In 1854, the iron works, which had gone out of blast, was purchased by a group of Philadelphia merchants led by Matthew Newkirk. After a fire destroyed the main rolling mill in 1857, Newkirk persuaded his co-investors to rebuild it on a larger scale.

The Dauphiné is raced over 8 days in the Rhône-Alpes region in the southeast of France, traditionally covering portions of the French Alps. The race has often, but not always, started with an opening prologue on Sunday. The Monday and Tuesday stages are usually held in the lower hilly regions of Rhône-Alpes, before addressing the high mountains in the second half of the Dauphiné. Often there is one long individual or team time trial included.

The partially preserved skull of *Livyatan melvillei*, including teeth and a mandible, was discovered in November 2008 by Klaas Post, a researcher from the Rotterdam Natural History Museum. The discovery occurred on the final day of a field trip to Cerro Colorado in the Pisco Formation, 35 kilometers south-southwest of Ica, Peru. The holotype specimen of *L. melvillei* was mechanically prepared in Lima and is now part of the collection of the Museum of Natural History, Lima of the National University of San Marcos.

Hirst held a number of appointments during his career. He was a member of the Prime Minister's Republic Advisory Committee, the chair of the Commonwealth Civics Education Group, a member of the Film Australia Board, a council member of the National Museum of Australia, and a member of the board of Old Parliament House in Canberra. He wrote the official history of Australia for new citizens and took a prominent part in the history summit convened by Prime Minister John Howard in 2006. Hirst advised the Victorian Government on the school history curriculum and was history adviser to the National Curriculum Authority. He was elected to the Academy of the Social Sciences in Australia in 1986. A committed republican, Hirst was the Convenor of the Republican Movement in Victoria.

Nekhorosh's national championships took place in Kyiv, Ukraine, in 1999 when she was 26 years old. At the outdoor track September 21, Nekhorosh bolted from the start of the 10,000 meter race and beat her competitors to finish in 33:09.00. Just two days later, the best in the nation lined up for the 5K. Nekhorosh took the lead and kicked to win in a time of 16:01.05, giving her both national championship titles, something that had only been done previously by Yelena Vyazova.

In The Color of Water author James McBride writes both his autobiography and a tribute to the life of his mother, Ruth McBride. Ruth married Andrew Dennis McBride, a black man from North Carolina. James's childhood was spent in a chaotic household of twelve children who had neither the time nor the outlet to ponder questions of race and identity. Ruth did not want to discuss the painful details of her early family life when her abusive father, Tateh, lorded over her sweet-tempered and meek mother, Mameh ("tateh" and "mameh" are Yiddish terms of endearment for "father" and "mother," roughly equivalent to "daddy and "mommy"). Ruth had cut all ties with her Jewish family, as they had essentially disowned her when she married James's father.

Minot is on the Drift Prairie of northwestern North Dakota. It is at 48°13′59′′N 101°17′32′′W﻿ / ﻿48.23306°N 101.29222°W, about 100 miles (160 km) north of Bismarck. The Mouse River, or Souris River, runs through the city west to east.

DeWeese was born January 31, 1934 in Rochester, Indiana. He began writing science fiction stories in grade school, beginning with a Mickey Mouse story he wrote. In high school, he wrote articles and science-fiction columns for his local newspaper. DeWeese became an active member of science fiction fandom, and his stories were published in science fiction fanzines such as Yandro, Fan-Fare, and The Chigger Patch of Fandom. He earned an associate degree in electronics from Valparaiso Technical Institute in 1953. He worked for General Motors' Delco Electronics Division as an electronics technician in Kokomo, Indiana, from 1954–1959, and as a technical writer (including for the Apollo Program) in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, from 1959 to 1974 (when he became a full-time freelance writer).

*Look Who's Talking Too* received "appropriately dire reviews" compared to its predecessor. Rotten Tomatoes gives the film a score of 17% based on 6 reviews, with an average rating of 3.6/10. Audiences polled by CinemaScore gave the film an average grade of "B+". *Look Who's Talking Too* was released in the United Kingdom sometime after April 1990.

Established in 1927, El Rodeo School, located at 605 N. Whittier Dr., Beverly Hills, CA 90210, originally served elementary grades. The school closed in 2019, with most students transferring to Hawthorne Elementary or Beverly Vista Middle School. Beverly Vista Middle School principal Kevin Allen, who was previously the principal at El Rodeo School, announced his retirement in 2024. El Rodeo reopened in 2024 as a TK-5 elementary school. The current enrollment numbers are unavailable. Ms. Kaber is the current principal.

The fifth season of *Justified* centers on the arrival of the Crowe crime family from Florida, led by Darryl Crowe Jr. (Michael Rapaport), Dewey Crowe's cousin. After learning about Dewey's unexpected fortune, Darryl decides to relocate his family to Harlan, Kentucky, seeking to take a piece of the local drug trade. Raylan Givens attempts to run Darryl out of town after learning about his relocation. Meanwhile, Boyd Crowder faces challenges as Ava is imprisoned, and he struggles to maintain his criminal empire while ensuring her safety. Art Mullen's season is marked by professional and personal turmoil. Art learns from Picker that Theo Tonin fingered him as the gunman that put Art in a coma. The relationship between Art and Raylan deteriorates when Art realizes that Raylan has crossed a line, leading to a physical altercation and a deep rift between them.

Eileen Kramer was born on 8 November 1914 in Mosman Bay, New South Wales, Australia, and grew up there with her brother. Her father worked as a car salesman and began showing signs of alcoholism when Kramer was about 10. When Eileen was 13, her mother left her father and secretly relocated with the children to the suburb of Coogee, where she worked as a store detective at Farmers.

On March 19, 2012, Mood Media announced its agreement to acquire DMX Holdings, Inc. for US$86.1 million. DMX, a US provider of multi-sensory branding services, delivers services to over 100,000 locations, creating branded connections with customers. In 2000, DMX Music acquired AEI Music Network Inc., which had previously acquired Rediffusion Music in 1996. In 2014, Stingray Digital Group acquired the Canadian assets of DMX Music Inc. from Mood Media for $16M.

Jones was born in Richmond, Virginia and grew up in Highland Springs, Virginia before his family relocated to Richmond, Texas right before he entered high school. He attended George Bush High School. He committed to play college basketball at Richmond over offers from Akron and South Alabama.

Gene DeWeese, born in Rochester, Indiana, on January 31, 1934, to Thomas Jacob and Alfreda (Henning) DeWeese, began his writing career early. He attempted a sequel to a Mickey Mouse story involving subatomic shrinking in grade school. During high school, he wrote articles and science fiction columns for the local newspaper. DeWeese also published stories in amateur science fiction magazines, marking his early involvement in science fiction fandom. He earned an associate degree in electronics from Valparaiso Technical Institute in 1953. From 1954 to 1959, he worked as an electronics technician for General Motors' Delco Electronics Division in Kokomo, Indiana, before becoming a technical writer in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, from 1959 to 1974.

In his review in Variety, Dennis Harvey said, "Strong performances and a brisk pace downplay the original script's more dated, preachy aspects. No one will mistake this well-produced but inevitably dialogue-driven piece for pure cinema, but Leon and adapter Paris Qualles open up the play just enough to avoid the usual stage-to-screen claustrophobia. Mervyn Warren's score is a bit more earnest and old-fashioned than would be ideal for this essentially faithful yet refreshed take on a dramatic golden oldie."

Catherine Cox represented Australia at under-21 level. In 1996, she stood out at the World Youth Netball Championships, where Australia defeated New Zealand in the grand final. Cox made her senior debut for Australia on June 11, 1997, aged 21, against South Africa in Perth. Australia defeated South Africa 65-38.

Borden is commemorated with various monuments and plaques. In his home town, Canning, Nova Scotia is a statue by Hamilton MacCarthy. In Halifax, Nova Scotia the base of the South African War Memorial has a panel commemorating the Battle of Witpoort. There is also a memorial plaque to Borden at McGill University, Montreal, in the Strathcona Medical Building (now the Strathcona Anatomy and Dentistry Building). Borden was a medical student at McGill before joining the war effort. He is listed on the Memorial Arch at the Royal Military College of Canada. Finally, there is also a plaque to Borden and others who died at Witpoort at Braamfontein Cemetery in South Africa where he is buried.

The Berthouville Treasure consists of approximately ninety gilt-silver objects dating from the first to third centuries AD. Weighing roughly 55 pounds (25 kilograms), the treasure includes bowls, cups, pitchers, and plates, many bearing votive inscriptions. These vessels are the most common items in the find. The treasure was intentionally buried in antiquity. The repoussé silver jug was made in Italy. The treasure also includes heirloom pieces.

On December 31, 2019, Bailey signed a one-year contract with the Minnesota Twins. On July 28, 2020, Bailey made his Twins debut earning his first victory over the Cardinals. Days after being activated from an IL stint, Bailey was designated for assignment by the Twins on September 25, 2020. He was released on September 27.

Stepanov supported the Bolsheviks after the October Revolution, and continued to serve in the Workers' and Peasants' Red Navy. Between February and May 1918 he participated in the Ice Cruise of the Baltic Fleet, the evacuation of the fleet's ships from Tallinn (Reval) and Helsinki to Kronstadt. From September 1918 to March 1919 Stepanov commanded the minelayer Sheksna, and from March 1919 to March 1920, he served as Chief of Staff of the Onega Military Flotilla [ru]. In March 1920 he was briefly acting commander of the flotilla, and saw action against the White Finns on Lake Onega. In May 1920 he became chief of staff of the Black Sea Fleet's marine detachment, temporarily serving as chief of the West Black Sea coast defence sector, between Ochakov and Odessa, from June to August 1921. From August 1921 to December 1924 Stepanov was Chief of the Operational Directorate of the Naval Staff, and from December 1924 to October 1925 Chief of Staff of the Black Sea Fleet.

Smart TVs are vulnerable to security exploits, as demonstrated by the discovery of flaws in LG's WebOS that allow for root access. These vulnerabilities can be exploited to install malware, potentially through malicious apps disguised as legitimate ones. Unauthorized access can lead to unwanted content display and, in some documented cases, remote modification of the TV's boot logo and app icons.

On December 2, 2015, the Oakland Athletics traded Pomeranz to the San Diego Padres along with minor league lefty Jose Torres and either a player to be named later or cash, in exchange for first baseman Yonder Alonso and reliever Marc Rzepczynski. Pomeranz approached the Padres about an opportunity to start, securing a role in their starting rotation. His performance earned him a spot in the 2016 MLB All-Star Game. In the first half of 2016, Pomeranz had a 2.47 ERA and led the National League in batting average against.

There are no treatment modalities for acute and chronic chikungunya that currently exist. Most treatment plans use supportive and symptomatic care like analgesics for pain and anti-inflammatories for inflammation caused by arthritis. In acute stages of this virus, rest, antipyretics and analgesics are used to subside symptoms. Most use non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs). In some cases, joint pain may resolve from treatment but stiffness remains.

‘Dasein’ is the story of a young painter Aniket Chatterjee, who commits suicide by taking sleeping pills. This movie is about what the character thinks and realises as he steadily approaches death while standing at the line between life and death. It is a journey from one's consciousness to sub consciousness, and occasionally the line between the two gets hazy.

Johnson was previously married to Keri D'Angelo before their divorce in 2010. He began dating Jessica Simpson in May 2010 and they announced their engagement that November after six months of dating. The couple married on July 5, 2014, at San Ysidro Ranch in Montecito, California. Together, Johnson and Simpson have two children, Maxwell and Ace, born in May 2012 and June 2013, respectively.

Corruption in Venezuela extends to its police force, with sources like Human Rights Watch documenting widespread abuses, including extrajudicial executions and arbitrary arrests. The government itself admitted in 2009 that police were responsible for up to 20% of all crimes. Political scientist Ana Maria Sanjuan suggests that people see career police officers as corrupt, abusive, and frequently in cahoots with criminals. Many victims fear retaliation or distrust the authorities, leading to underreporting of crimes. Human Rights Watch found no evidence that Venezuelan judicial authorities properly investigated any of the cases documented. The Caracas Metropolitan Police (PM) was disbanded due to corruption.

Genetic factors play a significant role in colorectal cancer (CRC) risk, with SMAD7 gene variants being associated with susceptibility to CRC. Specifically, a common genetic variant, rs4939827, in SMAD7 has been linked to CRC risk through genome-wide association studies, case-control studies involving Iranian populations, and meta-analyses. In CRC, impaired Smad signaling, due to Smad7 perturbation, is associated with poor prognosis.

Christine Maggiore, an HIV-positive AIDS denialist, died at her California home on December 27, 2008, at the age of 52. News reports initially stated that she had been treated for pneumonia in the six months prior to her death, according to officials at the Los Angeles County coroner's office. While a doctor familiar with Maggiore's family noted that antiretroviral medications could have prevented her death, some fellow AIDS denialists argued that a radical "detox cleansing" regimen may have weakened her and made her unable to fight off the pneumonia. Maggiore's death certificate lists the cause of death as disseminated herpes virus infection and bilateral pneumonia, with oral candidiasis as a contributing factor, all conditions that can be related to HIV infection. The death certificate also indicates that no autopsy was performed.

Bruni was educated at The Loomis Chaffee School, an independent boarding and day college preparatory school in Windsor, Connecticut, followed by the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, from which he graduated Phi Beta Kappa in 1986 with a B.A. in English. He was a Morehead Scholar and wrote for the student paper, The Daily Tar Heel. He then attended Columbia University's Graduate School of Journalism, from which he graduated second in his class with a master of science degree in journalism, and also won a Pulitzer Traveling Fellowship.

In the summer of 2019, at the initiative of the Association of the French aerospace industry, the Paris Air Show-2019 was held in Paris. Within the framework of the exhibition, an adviser to the Minister of Defense Industry of Azerbaijan Farrukh Vezirov met with the heads of French companies such as Nexter, Thales, CS Communication, Lacroix and others. Various projects were discussed: joint production of wheeled armored vehicles, etc.
Hearst is a Nashville, Tennessee, native and graduated from MLK Magnet High School. Her mother and father both hail from the Mississippi Delta. She is married to Michael Trent.

At All Saints University School of Medicine, the 5 Year MD program is designed for recent high school graduates seeking an MD degree. It includes four semesters of premedical courses, followed by the 4-year MD program. Upon completion of the premedical courses, students proceed to the Basic Medical Science portion of the degree program.

The white-tailed spider, belonging to the family Lamponidae, has been the subject of an enduring urban myth associating its bite with necrotising ulcers. However, research, including a prospective study of 130 verified Lampona bites (79 *Lampona cylindrata* and 51 *L. murina*), has failed to find any evidence linking the spider to such severe skin ulcerations or confirmed infections. The Lamponidae family comprises around 200 species of ground spiders. All but two species are exclusively found in Australasia. The most commonly encountered species are *Lampona cylindrata*, found throughout Australia except for eastern Queensland, and *Lampona murina*, which is restricted to eastern Australia. Lamponids have eight eyes arranged in two rows. They range in body length from 3mm to 13mm. Despite their frequent encounters with humans and ability to bite, studies have debunked the myth of flesh-eating white-tailed spiders.

Sokratis Kokkalis is the founder and majority shareholder of Intracom Holdings, one of the largest multinational technology groups in Southern Europe. He also serves as the Chairman and majority shareholder of Intralot S.A., a gaming technology supplier and licensed lottery operator. Kokkalis established the Kokkalis Foundation, a non-profit organization promoting education, culture, social welfare, medical research, information technology, and athletics, both in Greece and abroad. In 2015, his net worth was estimated at US$1.2 billion.

Lord Harewood was born at his parents' London house, 2 Orme Square, Bayswater, London, the eldest son of the 7th Earl of Harewood and his first wife, Marion Stein. He was baptised at All Saints' Church, Harewood. His father was a first cousin of Queen Elizabeth II. His godparents were The Princess Elizabeth, Duchess of Edinburgh (later Queen Elizabeth II), his great-grandmother Queen Mary, Viscountess Boyne, Benjamin Britten and his uncle Gerald Lascelles. At the time of his birth, he was 13th in line to the throne.

Karman became the first Arab woman, the youngest person at that time to have become a Nobel Peace Laureate and the category's second Muslim woman. At 32, Tawakkol Karman was then the youngest winner of a Nobel Peace Prize. She is younger (born 7 February 1979) than Mairead Maguire (born 27 January 1944), who was a co-recipient of the award in 1976 and previously held that record. In 2014, Malala Yousafzai, age 17, displaced Karman as the youngest winner ever. In 2003, Shirin Ebadi was the first Persian woman and first Muslim woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize. Karman was the third female journalist awarded the Nobel after Bertha von Suttner in 1905 and Emily Greene Balch in 1946. Before the 2011 Nobel Peace Prize was announced, only 12 other women had ever been recipients in its 110 years, and after the presentation there were 15 women.

Jessie Joe Parker, formerly Jessie Joe Nandye, represented Papua New Guinea in 2007 in matches against France and Wales in Europe. In 2008, he was part of PNG's squad for the 2008 World Cup. He scored a try for PNG at the 2009 Pacific Cup and played for PNG at the 2010 Four Nations. As a member of PNG's 2013 World Cup squad, Parker scored a try against Samoa.

Kate Elizabeth Russell's debut novel, *My Dark Vanessa*, tells the story of the traumatic sexual relationship between Vanessa Wye, a 15-year-old student at Maine's Browick boarding school, and Jacob Strane, her 42-year-old English teacher. The narrative, told from Vanessa's perspective, jumps between 2000, when the affair begins, and 2017, when Strane is accused of sexual abuse amidst the rise of allegations against powerful men, reflecting the #MeToo movement.

Soumaré won her first European Indoor Championships medal at the 2013 European Indoor Championships - a silver for the 60 metres. She had initially won the bronze. But the initial winner of the 60m final, Tezdzhan Naimova, was stripped of her gold medal after being found guilty of doping during the competition. Soumaré was thus elevated to receive the silver medal.

The show follows the story of an audio archivist named Dan, who has recently been hired for a job that involves reviewing and cataloguing a set of old audio tapes. The contract is for a group called the Housing Historical Committee of New York State and involves working at a remote and isolated bunker in the middle of the woods. Throughout his time on the job, Dan is contacted by his boss who insists that Dan must continuously record himself for archival and legal purposes. The audio tapes that Dan is listening to are over two decades old and contain interviews conducted by someone named Melody Pendras.

After visiting Moscow (where he produced no fewer than eight poetic pieces of invective aimed at Benckendorff), on 9 May 1841, Lermontov arrived to Stavropol, introduced himself to general Grabbe and asked for permission to stay in the town. Then, on a whim, he changed his course, found himself in Pyatigorsk and sent his seniors a letter informing them of his having fallen ill. The regiment's special commission recommended him treatment at Mineralnye Vody. What he did instead was embark upon the several weeks' spree. "In the mornings he was writing, but the more he worked, the more need he felt to unwind in the evenings," Skabichevsky wrote. "I feel I'm left with very little of my life," the poet confessed to his friend A. Merinsky on 8 July, a week before his death.

Brookfield, Wisconsin, was originally inhabited by Potawatomi Native Americans. William Howe was the first white settler, arriving in 1820 with a Presidential Land Grant. In 1836, Robert Curren bought a claim and established a tavern and inn.

*Licence to Kill*, produced on a budget of $42 million, grossed $156,167,015 worldwide. This figure includes $34,667,015 in the United States and Canada and $121,500,000 internationally. While every other James Bond film had managed to eclipse $100 million prior to accounting for inflation, *Licence to Kill* grossed $81.8 million. Its performance was considered lackluster compared to previous entries in the series. Some analysts suggested that its darker tone and higher violence level, resulting in a PG-13 rating, may have contributed to its underperformance.

In November 2005, NICTA announced that Qualcomm was deploying NICTA's L4 version on their Mobile Station Modem chipsets. This led to the use of L4 in mobile phone handsets on sale from late 2006. In August 2006, ERTOS leader and UNSW professor Gernot Heiser spun out a company named Open Kernel Labs (OK Labs) to support commercial L4 users and further develop L4 for commercial use under the brand name OKL4, in close collaboration with NICTA. OKL4 Version 2.1, released in April 2008, was the first generally available version of L4 which featured capability-based security. OKL4 3.0, released in October 2008, was the last open-source version of OKL4. More recent versions are closed source and based on a rewrite to support a native hypervisor variant named the OKL4 Microvisor. OK Labs also distributed a paravirtualized Linux named OK:Linux, a descendant of Wombat, and paravirtualized versions of SymbianOS and Android. OK Labs also acquired the rights to seL4 from NICTA.

WBGG-FM, then WRKZ, carried the syndicated Howard Stern Show for morning drive time until Clear Channel dropped it after the Federal Communications Commission fined them $495,000 in January 2004 for indecent content.

On 14 January 2022, Zhegrova signed a four-year contract with Ligue 1 club Lille and received squad number 23. Lille reportedly paid a €7 million transfer fee. Eight days later, he was named as a Lille substitute for the first time in a league match against Brest. His debut with Lille came on 6 February in a 1–5 home defeat against Paris Saint-Germain after coming on as a substitute at 85th minute in place of Jonathan Bamba. One month after debut, Zhegrova scored his first goal for Lille in his fourth appearance for the club in a 4–0 home win over Clermont in Ligue 1.

In August 1830 Lermontov enrolled in Moscow University's philological faculty. "Petty arrogance" (as Skabichevsky puts it) prevented him from joining any of the three radical students' circles (those led respectively by Vissarion Belinsky, Nikolai Stankevich and Alexander Hertzen). Instead he drifted towards an aristocracic clique, but even this cream of the Moscow's "golden youth" detested the young man for being too aloof, while still giving him credit for having charisma. "Everyone could see that Lermontov was obnoxious, rough and daring, and yet there was something alluring in his firm moroseness," fellow-student Wistengof admitted.

The Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food is responsible for the Canadian Grain Commission. The Commission is governed by the Canada Grain Act (R.S.C. 1985, c. G-10), which provides for the appointment of three commissioners by the federal cabinet, one of whom is named chief commissioner. The CGC is headed by a Chief Operating Officer (CEO), who reports to the chief commissioner.

In July 2019, a researcher from San Diego-based Stemson Therapeutics, partnered with UCSD, successfully grew his own follicles on a mouse using iPSC-derived epithelial and dermal cell therapy. The hair grew straight and was aligned properly with a 3D printed biodegradable shaft. The hairs were permanent and regenerated naturally.

Brian Ibbott, who dreamed of being a deejay since childhood, began his DJ career spinning at weddings, but found the repetitive music selection boring. Discovering podcasting in 2004, Ibbott launched *Coverville*, a podcast dedicated to cover songs. *Coverville* quickly became one of the longest running podcasts in the Apple Podcast store. *Coverville* passed episode #300 on March 4, 2007, and celebrated its 500th episode in August 2008 with a gala held in Las Vegas.

Théophile de Giraud is a figurehead of the denatalist movement. He co-created Non-Parents Day, celebrated alternately in Paris and Brussels between 2006 and 2010. In 2020, de Giraud covered the equestrian statue of Leopold II at the gates of the Royal Palace in Brussels with red paint, an action mirroring similar protests against the glorification of colonialism. He also organized a 'denatalist' event in Paris.

Il Divo was conceived by Simon Cowell, inspired by the popularity of operatic singers Andrea Bocelli and Sarah Brightman, and his appreciation for lyrical voices reminiscent of The Three Tenors. From 2001 to December 2003, Cowell conducted a worldwide search for young singers to form a multinational quartet. The search concluded with the signing of American tenor David Miller. Prior to joining Il Divo, members had varying levels of success, ranging from Madrid opera stars to pop singers.

Suicide awareness expresses the need for open constructive dialogue as an initial step towards preventing incidents of adolescent suicide. Once the stigmas have been overcome, there is an increased possibility that education, medical care and support can provide a critical framework for those at risk. Lack of information, awareness of professional services, judgment and insensitivity from religious groups, and financial strain have all been identified as barriers to support access for those youth in suicidal crisis. The critical framework is a necessary component to implementing suicide awareness and suicide prevention, and breaking down these barriers.

The Benedictine Cadets have a rich athletic history, particularly in basketball. Warren Rutledge served as the head coach for 43 years, amassing 949 total wins, making him the winningest high school coach in Virginia and eleventh in the nation. The basketball program has secured 25 Virginia State Catholic titles (1959, 1961, 1964, 1965, 1967, 1968, 1970-1985, 1994, 1999, 2000, and 2003) and five VISAA State Championships for Boys' Basketball (2003, 2007, 2008, 2011, and 2013). Since the early 1950s, the Cadets have played home games on "Coach Rut Court" in the Memorial Gymnasium. From 1951 to 1954, the University of Richmond's basketball team used Benedictine's Memorial Gymnasium as their home court. Benedictine has hosted the annual Benedictine Capital City Classic, a holiday boys' basketball tournament, since 1966.

Takaji represented Lebanon internationally in futsal, playing at the 2007, 2008, 2010, and 2012 AFC Futsal Championships, captaining Lebanon in the last edition. With 13 goals, Takaji was the 2008 Arab Futsal Championship top scorer, and was awarded the Best Player award. He also helped Lebanon to a sixth-place finish at the 2010 Mediterranean Futsal Cup.

CHI St. Luke’s Health–Lakeside Hospital, located in The Woodlands, Texas, has received several accolades. Note that Providence Holy Cross Medical Center, not CHI St. Luke's Health–Lakeside Hospital, was named one of Modern Healthcare magazine's "Best Places to Work in Healthcare." St. Luke's Health–Lakeside Hospital received the ISO 9001:2008 Quality Management System certification from DNV Healthcare, Inc. It was also distinguished as one of the top hospitals for surgery in Houston by Consumer Reports.

Rain delayed the start of practice until 5:00 p.m. Due to the delay, officials extended the practice session until 7:00 p.m. A total of 29 drivers completed 1,044 laps in a busy session. Just after 6 p.m., rookie Jack Hawksworth lost control and did a half spin in turn three, and crashed into the outside wall. It was the first crash of the month. Hawksworth was not injured. Simon Pagenaud, who won the Grand Prix of Indianapolis, turned the fastest lap of the day. He became the first driver over 226 mph for the month. At 6:24 p.m., light rain began to fall once again, and the track was closed for the day.

Breast cancer is the most common cancer in American women, with approximately 1 in 8 women in the U.S. developing the disease in their lifetime. Mammography, a specialized type of radiography, uses low doses of ionizing radiation to image the internal structures of the breasts. While mammograms are considered the best available screening tool, they have limitations, including the potential for false-negative results. The benefits of mammography outweigh the possible harm from radiation exposure. However, repeated exposure to ionizing radiation carries a risk of cancer. Furthermore, mammograms are not always accurate, sometimes leading to unnecessary biopsies; approximately 80% of breast biopsies performed after mammography are negative for cancer. The National Cancer Institute reports that the average relative 5-year survival rate for those diagnosed with distant spread ovarian cancer, which includes Stage 4 ovarian cancer, is about 31%.

In November 2008, a partially preserved skull, as well as teeth and the lower jaw, belonging to L. melvillei, the holotype specimen MUSM 1676, were discovered in the coastal desert of Peru in the sediments of the Pisco Formation, 35 km (22 mi) southwest of the city of Ica. Klaas Post, a researcher for the Natural History Museum Rotterdam in the Netherlands, stumbled across them on the final day of a field trip. The fossils were prepared in Lima, and are now part of the collection of the Museum of Natural History, Lima of National University of San Marcos.

The Nico recording of "These Days," originally released on her 1967 album *Chelsea Girl*, experienced a surge in popularity in 2001 after being prominently featured in Wes Anderson's film *The Royal Tenenbaums*. The film, co-written by Anderson and Owen Wilson, is a tragicomedy about a dysfunctional family of geniuses and their estranged father. The song is used during a memorable scene where Gwyneth Paltrow, as the character Margot Tenenbaum, exits a green line bus. Anderson himself has stated that this scene was one of the earliest conceptual pieces for the film. Jackson Browne, who played guitar on Nico's original recording, granted permission for the song's use but had forgotten about it until seeing the film in theaters. The Philadelphia City Paper lauded Anderson's "perfect usage" of "These Days" in the film.

Jason Gray, an American contemporary Christian singer-songwriter, was born and raised in Southwestern Minnesota. Before pursuing full-time music, he spent six years in youth ministry. In 1999, Gray began his full-time music ministry as an independent artist. He lived in the Mankato area until moving to Nashville in 2015. Gray, who has a stutter, signed with Centricity Music in 2006 after releasing independent records. In 2007, he released his first major label record with Centricity Music. One website referred to Gray as one of "Christian music's best-kept secrets."

Ludwig Kietzmann of GamesRadar+ stated that he liked the character of Kat, calling her an "exuberant girl in a scarf" that stood in contrast to most superheroes, who were a "tortured dude in a cape". He also praised how she was a "delightful klutz", which tied into the game's initially imprecise controls. Stating "there's an art to being utterly graceless in Gravity Rush", he stated that he imagined her apologizing to the people caught up in her gravity powers as she passed by and said that "Kat's optimism in the face of her innate clumsiness is what makes her special".

This museum began in 1913 when an Italian Jesuit priest named Chiapi donated around 2000 different types of minerals, Herbarium and a collection of Roman coins. In 1906, the De Dion car was the first automobile that was used in Mangalore. It was imported to Mangalore by P F X Saldanha of the Highland Coffee Works. This car is one of the souvenirs present in this museum. The museum also has a collection of domestic and agricultural utensils used by the ancient generation.

The Gastraea hypothesis was proposed by Ernst Haeckel, shortly after his work on the calcareous sponges. He proposed that this group of sponges is monophyletic with all eumetazoans, including the bilaterians. This suggests that the gastrulation and the gastrula stage are universal for eumetazoans. It has been perceived as problematic that gastrulation by invagination is by no means universal among eumetazoans. Only recently has an invagination been confirmed in a Calcarea sponge, albeit too early to form a remaining inner space (archenteron).

The TransPeshawar system in phase 2 is complemented by a feeder system consisting of 8 routes extending 68 kilometres. Feeder buses use dedicated BRT lanes as needed before exiting the system and entering onto city streets. The feeder routes add an additional 100 stops, all of which will be new construction. Feeder stations are spaced approximately 300-500 metres from one another.

ScienceSaves is a nationwide pro-science campaign to generate an appreciation for the role of science. National Science Appreciation Day started in 2022 and is part of the ScienceSaves initiative and happens annually on March 26. In 2022, CFI got proclamations declaring March 26 as National Science Appreciation Day from more than a dozen states.
*Narcissus papyraceus*, also known as paperwhites, features stems that grow upwards, reaching 12-18 inches tall. It bears clusters of fragrant, pure white flowers.

In the Middle East during the later medieval period, the composite bow was the preferred type of bow. Its primary advantage over the longbow was its suitability for use on horseback. Manufacturing these bows required skilled craftsmen due to their complex construction. Effective use of the composite bow also demanded extensive training. The Byzantine Empire was already using mounted archers extensively by the 5th century, and Emperor Mavrikios laid down principles for their use in his military work, the *Strategikon of Maurice*. The core of the Byzantine army was the Cataphract, an armoured horse archer, and they also recruited mercenary light mounted archers from the Steppes.

On 14 January 2022, Zhegrova signed a four-year contract with Ligue 1 club Lille and was assigned squad number 23. Lille reportedly paid a EUR7 million transfer fee for the right winger, who joined as a replacement for Jonathan Ikone. Eight days later, he was named as a substitute for the first time in a league match against Brest. Zhegrova made his debut for Lille on 6 February in a 1-5 home defeat against Paris Saint-Germain, coming on as a substitute in the 85th minute for Jonathan Bamba. One month after his debut, Zhegrova scored his first goal for Lille in a 4-0 home win over Clermont.

D-Black, born Desmond Kwesi Blackmore, began his career as part of the group "D-Black and Kwaku-t" in 2009, releasing their album *Target Practice*. In 2010, D-Black embarked on a solo career, releasing four albums: *Music, Love and Life*, *Loyalty*, and two others not mentioned in the provided context. He also released an E.P and a mix-tape, the titles of which are not mentioned in the provided context. The context does not mention D-Black winning a BET Award.

Passenger rail service is provided by Amtrak San Joaquins. The main passenger rail station is the renovated historic Santa Fe Railroad Depot in Downtown Fresno. The city of Fresno is planned to be served by the future California High-Speed Rail.

For ITTF world title events, a player is eligible to play for his association by registering with the ITTF. If the player chooses to play for a new association, he shall register with the ITTF, through the new association. The player will be eligible to play for the new association after three, five, seven years after the date of registration, if the player is under the age of 15, 18, 21 respectively. The player will be eligible to play for the new association after nine years if the player is at least 21 years old.

Ponsonby was commissioned into the Royal Air Force in April 1983 as a pilot, after nine years service as an infantry officer in the British Army. On 10 April 1983, he was appointed to a permanent commission and promoted to flight lieutenant with seniority from 10 March 1980. On 1 July 1988, he was promoted to squadron leader, and then to wing commander on 1 January 1994. As a group captain, he was appointed an Officer of the Order of the British Empire in the 1999 New Year Honours, and was made an aide-de-camp to Queen Elizabeth II on 1 August 2001. He relinquished this appointment on 3 May 2002, being promoted to air commodore on 1 July that year. Ponsonby was also awarded the Queen's Commendation for Valuable Service in the 2002 Operational Honours List "in recognition of gallant and distinguished services in Northern Ireland during the period 1 October 2001 to 31 March 2002". He was promoted to his final rank of air vice marshal on 4 January 2005.

The local civic association is the Wissahickon Interested Citizens Association (WICA), which was instrumental in preparing and implementing a Neighborhood Conversation Overlay (NCO) to mitigate the impact of new development and maintain the neighborhood's walkability and character. WICA holds public meetings on the second Wednesday of every month. Wissahickon Neighbors Civic Association (WNCA) serves the community northwest of Ridge Avenue and holds public meetings on the third Thursday of every month at Pilgrim Church. WNCA serves the northwest portion of Philadelphia's Wissahickon neighborhood.

James Jordan Sr. was born in Wallace, North Carolina, on July 31, 1936. While attending Charity High School, he met Deloris Peoples (born September 1941). The two began dating and remained together for the next three years. Upon graduation, Jordan Sr. joined the Air Force and was stationed in San Antonio, Texas. In 1956, he transferred to a base in Virginia and married Peoples shortly thereafter. Their first child, son James Ronald "Ronnie" Jr., was born the following year. Jordan Sr. left the Air Force and got a job at a textile mill in Wallace. The Jordans had two more children: daughter Deloris, and a son, Larry.

In 2003, Edel released Flowers: The Jennifer Paige Hits Collection as a result of her parting ways with her record label. In October of the same year, Paige admitted she was no longer with either Hollywood Records or Edel Records. Paige continued writing for other artists, and compiling songs for a new studio effort. In April 2006, "The Calling", was chosen by Lifetime Television for their made-for-TV movie, Augusta, Gone, based on the book of the same name. The song was written by Paige and co-writer/producer Chris Landon of Loudnoize Entertainment.

The film grossed ₹16.94 crore (US$1.9 million) on its opening day. Heroine had an average weekend of ₹25 crore (US$2.9 million) nett. The film had collected a fair ₹31 crore (US$3.5 million) in its first week. The film had a heavy fall in second week and managed only ₹60 crore (US$6.9 million) nett. It collected Rs. 144 million.

Bandy in its original, informal manner disappeared from the North American continent entirely once it and elements from the early game had become absorbed into a new sport of ice hockey. While ice hockey was growing and organizing in the United States, bandy was doing the same, but only in Europe and Scandinavia. It would not arrive in its organized format in the United States until the 1970s, almost a century after its initial development.

ISG15 was originally identified in the late 1970s as a 15-kDa protein produced in response to type I interferon, a potent class of antiviral cytokines. Given the molecular weight, it was originally termed ‘a 15-kDa protein’, but later renamed interferon-stimulated-gene-15 when the cassette of interferon-stimulated genes were recognized. In 1987 it was identified that ISG15 cross-reacts with anti-ubiquitin antibodies, and subsequent experiments uncovered the ubiquitin-like conjugation of ISG15 to other cellular proteins, coined ‘ISGylation’. Given its inducibility by IFN-I, studies in the following decades focused on the antiviral activity of ISG15. These studies were carried out predominantly with in vitro systems and mouse models, and ascribed several antiviral functions to ISGylation. During this time, it was also discovered that ISG15 could be detected outside of cells. and in human serum samples. This free form of ISG15 could stimulate IFN-II production in lymphocytes. Finally, ISG15 could also be detected as an un-conjugated intracellular molecule with functions independent of ISGylation.

Neel Kamal earned about ₹2.5 million (₹25 lakhs) at the box office in its initial theatrical run. According to Hindustan Times, it was a commercial failure. In the words of Sharma, the film "did not achieve much success."

Recruiting international athletes has become a growing trend among NCAA institutions. For many European athletes who aren't likely to play professionally for a club, the NCAA provides the only option to pursue an academic and athletic career simultaneously. Many of these students expect to achieve at least what their parents achieved and believe they control their destiny.

Sora was designed by Tetsuya Nomura as the protagonist of Kingdom Hearts. He was not originally slated to be the protagonist, as Disney wanted Donald Duck to fill the role, while Square wanted Mickey Mouse. Nomura designed Sora with the concepts of the Disney characters in mind, basing his outfit on Mickey Mouse's trademark white gloves, red shorts and giant yellow shoes. Sora originally wielded a weapon resembling a chainsaw; however, the weapon was not well received by Disney, which led Nomura to redesign the weapon into a Keyblade. He was also designed with lion-like features, which were removed as the staff found them to be similar to those of Final Fantasy IX protagonist Zidane Tribal. The design was further reworked after a talk with the Disney staff, and Nomura finished it after a night's work. Because Sora was a new character in the first Kingdom Hearts, Square added Final Fantasy characters who support the protagonist.
Sir Anthony Lechmere, 1st Baronet, was the son of Edmund Lechmere MP. Edmund's second wife was unknown. Anthony matriculated at Merton College, Oxford, in 1785.

Sky and BBC News reported that the East London Mosque on Whitechapel Road in Whitechapel had been surrounded by armed police, and residents were told to stay indoors. The mosque was evacuated at approximately 3:30 PM. Police searched the building and confirmed it was a bomb scare, giving the all-clear after just over an hour.

On August 28, 2017, Shoshi signed with Sigal Prishtina of the Kosovo Basketball League. However, on January 5, 2018, Shoshi parted ways with Sigal Prishtina to join the Lithuanian team Pieno žvaigždės for the rest of the season. On April 21, 2018, Shoshi recorded a season-high 20 points, shooting 9-of-12 from the field, along with five rebounds in an 85–94 loss to Lietkabelis. Shoshi went on to win the 2018 Baltic League Championship with Pieno žvaigždės.

In July 2011, Price and multiple associates were served with search warrants obtained by the Federal Bureau of Investigation arising out of acts of alleged public corruption. The warrant on Price's residence specified a search for data related to violations of the U.S. Code Titles 18, 26, or 31, specifically "Theft or Bribery Concerning Programs Receiving Federal Funds," tax evasion, fraud, false statements, money laundering, and "aiding and abetting and conspiracy to violate these statutes." The FBI seized multiple assets belonging to Price. Price is currently being investigated by the FBI. According to Federal sources, Price was arrested by the FBI on Friday, July 25, 2014. His trial has been scheduled for January 2016 and will have to contribute $80,000 towards his own defense.

Dora was born on 15 May 1890, at Shady Row in Meltham Mills, near Huddersfield in the West Riding of Yorkshire in 1890. She was one of seven children born to James and Eliza Thewlis, who was from Woodbridge, Suffolk. At the time James was working locally as a weaver. And Dora worked in a Yorkshire mill as a teen.

There were over 1,000 underground newspapers; among the most important were the Biuletyn Informacyjny of Armia Krajowa and Rzeczpospolita of the Government Delegation for Poland. In addition to publication of news (from intercepted Western radio transmissions), there were hundreds of underground publications dedicated to politics, economics, education, and literature (for example, Sztuka i Naród). The highest recorded publication volume was an issue of Biuletyn Informacyjny printed in 43,000 copies; average volume of larger publication was 1,000–5,000 copies. The Polish underground also published booklets and leaflets from imaginary anti-Nazi German organizations aimed at spreading disinformation and lowering morale among the Germans. Books were also sometimes printed. Other items were also printed, such as patriotic posters or fake German administration posters, ordering the Germans to evacuate Poland or telling Poles to register household cats.

Providence Cristo Rey High School, a private, Catholic, college and career preparatory high school in Indianapolis, opened in 2007 at the former site of IPS School 50. The school belongs to the Cristo Rey Network, founded by the Jesuits in Chicago in 1993, and aims to serve economically disadvantaged students. In 2007, when Sister Dawn Tomaszewski was installed as general superior of the Sisters of Providence, the Sisters of Providence opened the high school with the goal of preparing students for college through a work-study program with corporate partners. Providence Cristo Rey High School graduated its first class in 2011.

Heptinstall was born in Great Britain and earned his medical degree from Charing Cross Hospital Medical School in London. He served in the British Army during World War II as part of Force 136, parachuting into Japanese-occupied Thailand to care for freed prisoners of war. After the war, he did postgraduate study under Alexander Fleming. He moved to the United States in 1954 to serve a fellowship at Johns Hopkins Hospital.

The West Midlands is the second most populous city-region in the United Kingdom after Greater London, which had 8.7 million residents in 2015. The position of Mayor of the West Midlands was created in May 2017 following a devolution deal reached in November 2015 between the UK government and seven local council leaders. The Cities and Local Government Devolution Act of 2016 stipulated that a directly elected metro mayor was required for a combined authority to receive additional powers. The first mayoral term was set for three years, with subsequent elections held every four years. Andy Street, the Conservative candidate, won the inaugural 2017 election, defeating Labour's Sion Simon.

Certain foods and supplements, like protein, magnesium, and biotin, may help strengthen brittle nails. A few small studies show that 2.5-5 mg of biotin daily for six weeks to fifteen months improved symptoms in 63-91% of participants.

The local civic association is called Wissahickon Interested Citizen's Association (WICA). The association was instrumental in preparing and implementing a Neighborhood Conversation Overlay (NCO) to mitigate the impact of new development in the neighborhood as well as maintain the walkability and character of it. Public meetings are held on the second Wednesday of every month. Another area civic association, Wissahickon Neighbors Civic Association (WNCA), serves the community northwest of Ridge Avenue. Public meetings are held on the first Monday of every month at Pilgrim Church.

Superdelegates, also known as automatic delegates, are unpledged delegates free to support any presidential candidate. They include Democratic National Committee members, members of Congress, governors, and distinguished party leaders. Superdelegates represent roughly 15% of all delegates.

Budots music is characterized as a derivation from electronic and house music. It features 140 bpm four-on-the-floor patterns, pulsating basslines, multiple percussion layers, distorted and repetitive vocal samples, DJ fills placed throughout the track and distinct high-pitched synth hooks that locals onomatopoetically refer to as tiw tiw. It also bears similarities with Eurodance, but without the melodramatic vocals, over-the-top piano melodies, and expressions of emotional vulnerability in exchange for lewd jokes and calls for rowdy partying. Most budots tracks do not have the usual elements that make a pop song such as verses, choruses, and chord progressions. Instead, the energetic beats are held together with cheesy sound effects such as vinyl scratches or chipmunk laughter. Meanwhile, the budots tracks that do contain lyrics are written in any of the Bisayan languages.

A video game adaptation titled *R.I.P.D.: The Game* was developed by Old School Games and published by Atlus USA, releasing on July 16, 2013, for Xbox 360, PlayStation 3, and PC via Steam. The game is a two-player co-op shooter featuring wave-based combat similar to Old School Games' previous title, *God Mode*. *R.I.P.D.: The Game* received mixed to negative reviews.

As a medical student, Sachs developed an interest in transplantation and joined the laboratory of Drs. Paul Russell and Henry Winn at the Massachusetts General Hospital, where he remained as a surgical resident until 1970. From 1970 to 1972 Sachs fulfilled his military service working in Christian Anfinsen's laboratory at the National Institutes of Health. He remained in Bethesda and joined the National Cancer Institute, where his research led to the discovery of class II MHC in 1973. He was then appointed director of the Transplantation Biology Section of the Immunology Branch at NCI in 1974, and Chief of the Immunology Branch in 1982.

"Winter" was written for the 2009 war film *Brothers*, directed by Jim Sheridan, who had been friends with U2 since their early days and asked them to contribute a song. A key issue during Barack Obama's presidency was the confirmation of his judicial nominees, with Republicans accused of stalling the process through the use of filibusters and other delaying tactics (Context 4, 11). In 2013, Senate Democrats, frustrated by Republican obstruction, changed the filibuster rules to require a simple majority for most nominations, excluding Supreme Court nominees (Context 5). The call of the great spotted woodpecker is a sharp kik, which may be repeated as a wooden rattling krrarraarr if the bird is disturbed. The courtship call, gwig, is mostly given in the display flight. Drumming on dead trees and branches, and sometimes suitable man-made structures, serves to maintain contact between paired adults and to advertise ownership of territory.

Germaine Greer began her writing career penning columns as "Dr. G" for *Oz* magazine. The London version of *Oz* was owned by Australian writer Richard Neville, who co-founded it with Martin Sharp and Jim Anderson in London in early 1967. The original *Oz* magazine was first published in Sydney in 1963, edited by Richard Neville, Richard Walsh, and Martin Sharp, and covered counter-culture topics. In 2007, Ferentz' Hawkeyes started 2–4 and lost their first three conference games. Highly-ranked quarterback Jake Christensen struggled replacing the graduated Tate. An upset victory over #18 Illinois ended a nine-game conference losing streak for Iowa, and the Hawkeyes closed out the Big Ten season by winning their last three conference games. However, a disappointing loss in the season finale to Western Michigan dropped the Hawkeyes' season record to 6–6. Though Iowa was bowl-eligible, the Hawkeyes did not receive a bowl bid, snapping Iowa's streak of six consecutive bowl appearances.
