He appeared in all 28 games for the Breakers in 2016–17, averaging 17.3 points, 3.4 rebounds and 2.4 assists per game.

Traditional-style craft on the French waterways are renewed thanks to new builds like the Aslaug, which the Danish owners ordered after several years of cruising the waterways in their motor yacht. This style is a smaller barge than those for which the French canals were built, and is effectively an import from the Netherlands, where as recently as the 1970s many much smaller canals were navigated by craft of this type, carrying 70-90 tonnes. The barge was designed by Euroship Services, Heerewaarden, Netherlands, and built by Ament Metaalbewerking in Kinrooi, Belgium, which regularly builds recreational 'barge-type' craft up to 25m in length.

The New York Times Building was designed by Renzo Piano and Fox & Fowle and was developed by the New York Times Company, Forest City Ratner, and ING Real Estate. It was Piano's first design in New York City. Gensler designed the interior under the supervision of Margo Grant Walsh. AMEC was the main contractor for the core and shell, while Turner Construction was the contractor for the Times space in the lower section of the building. Other companies involved with the project included structural engineer Thornton Tomasetti, wind consultant RWDI, sealant supplier Dow Corning Corporation, and steel supplier ArcelorMittal. The mechanical, electrical, and plumbing system was designed by Flack + Kurtz. Officially, the New York City Economic Development Corporation owns the site.

Hitler's Table Talk reveals he continued to wish for a unified Protestant Reich Church of Germany for some time after 1937, which had largely proven unsuccessful. This was in line with his earlier policy of uniting all the Protestant churches so they would purvey the new racial and nationalist doctrines of the regime and act as a unifying rather than divisive force in Germany. By 1940, Hitler had abandoned even the syncretist idea of a positive Christianity. According to Thomas Childers, after 1938 Hitler began to publicly support a Nazified version of science, particularly social Darwinism, at the core of Nazi ideology in place of a religious one; a development that is reflected in his increasingly hostile remarks towards Christianity in the Table Talk. Historian Richard Weikart characterized Hitler's belief in "evolutionary ethics as the expression of the will of God" who routinely "equated the laws of nature and the will of Providence".

KOKI-DT2 is the MeTV-affiliated second digital subchannel of KOKI-TV, broadcasting in standard definition on channel 23.2. KOKI-TV launched a digital subchannel on virtual channel 23.2 in April 2011, originally serving as an affiliate of music video-focused network TheCoolTV through a groupwide agreement between network parent Cool Music Network, LLC and Newport Television, involving Newport-operated stations in ten markets. On July 30, 2012, through an affiliation agreement reached between Newport Television and network owner Weigel Broadcasting, KOKI-DT2 became an affiliate of the classic television network MeTV.

Founded in late 2014, the NRHL began its inaugural season on February 20, 2015. Four teams (Alkali Revive, Alkali Surge, Canfield Chiefs and Detroit Bordercats) played a 12-game regular season followed by playoffs. Following an undefeated regular season, the Detroit Bordercats needed three games to eliminate Alkali Revive to advance to the Commissioners Cup Finals where they swept the best-of three series against Alkali Surge. Kyle Siciliano was named playoffs MVP.

Youssef Shaaban was married four times. His first marriage was to actress Laila Taher, lasting four years. His second wife was Nadia Chirine, the daughter of Fawzia Fuad of Egypt; they had a daughter named Sinai. His third wife was actress Seham Fathi. In 1989, Shaaban married Kuwaiti national Iman Shreaan, with whom he had two children: a daughter, Zainab, and a son, Murad.

At the medieval University of Oxford, students organized themselves into two nations: the Australes, representing the south of England, and the Boreales, representing the north. The Australes were the more powerful of the two. The 'Welsh' were considered part of the southern nation. The Boreales mainly came from the north.

Litlington, a parish covering 2,171 acres, lies 20 km south-west of Cambridge. Roughly rectangular, its southern border follows the Icknield Way. The northern border with Abington Pigotts follows the Mill river. The western and eastern boundaries follow field boundaries with Steeple Morden and Bassingbourn respectively.

After the poaching crisis of the 1970s and 80s, northern white rhino populations were wiped out in Uganda and Sudan. By the mid-1980s, only 13 remained in Garamba National Park in Zaire. In response, international organizations became involved in conservation efforts. The Conservation Breeding Specialist Group (CBSG) of the IUCN met in Dvůr Králové Zoo in February 1986 to discuss strategies. That year, an older male rhino was imported from London. Saut, a calf from the original 1975 group, was returned to Dvůr Králové Zoo from San Diego Zoo in 1996. Attempts to breed the remaining northern white rhinos with southern white rhinos proved unsuccessful. Scientists have since been harvesting eggs from the remaining females for in vitro fertilization and potential surrogate pregnancies.

As of the 2000 census, there were 14,937 people residing in Exeter, Maine, within 6,465 households and 4,034 families. The population density was 1,146.0 people per square mile. There were 9,349 housing units, resulting in an average density of 717.3 units per square mile. The racial makeup of the town was 97.57% White, 0.17% Native American, and 0.68% from two or more races. 0.90% of the population were Hispanic or Latino of any race.

The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints has a small presence in Azerbaijan. A branch operated in Baku until the mid or late 2010s, but it was later discontinued. In 2018, there were approximately 40 members of the Church in Azerbaijan. Currently, a member group appears to function in Baku under the supervision of a mission branch.

Reynolds made his first game sale with Quest 1 to SoftSide magazine, its August 1981 cover feature. He was a gamer in high school, and a SysOp on Randolph School's (Huntsville, Alabama) PDP-11 mainframe computer. A 1990 graduate of the University of the South, Reynolds briefly pursued graduate studies in Philosophy at the University of California, Berkeley.

Bibiana Campos Seijo began her publishing career in the UK as a technical editor at the European Respiratory Society. She later became the Editor of *Chemistry World*, a magazine published by the Royal Society of Chemistry (RSC), though the exact year of her appointment is not specified in the provided context. Campos Seijo is currently the Editor-in-Chief of *Chemical & Engineering News* (C&EN), the news organization of the American Chemical Society, and vice president of the C&EN Media Group.

The Secret Service is tasked with safeguarding the payment and financial systems of the United States from a wide range of financial and cyber-based crimes. Financial investigations include counterfeit U.S. currency, bank and financial institution fraud, mail fraud, wire fraud, illicit financing operations, and major conspiracies. Cyber investigations include cybercrime, network intrusions, identity theft, access device fraud, credit card fraud, and intellectual property crimes. The Secret Service is also a member of the FBI's Joint Terrorism Task Force (JTTF) which investigates and combats terrorism on a national and international scale. Also, the Secret Service investigates missing and exploited children and is a partner of the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children (NCMEC).

Roger Jourdain was born in Louviers, France, in 1845. He pursued a career as a painter, studying under Alexandre Cabanel and Isidore Pils at the Ecole des Beaux-Arts in Paris. Separately, Roger A. Jourdain, a different individual, served as chair of the Red Lake Tribal Council. He played a role in reforming the Red Lake government to an elected council, supported by the Young Men General Advisory Council. This revised constitution led to the establishment of an elected council. Roger A. Jourdain was elected chair of the tribal council in 1959.

The great spotted woodpecker's call is a sharp "kik". When disturbed, this call may be repeated as a wooden, rattling "krrarraarr". The courtship call, "gwīg", is mostly given during display flights. Drumming on dead trees and branches serves to maintain contact between paired adults and to advertise territory.

The lazuline sabrewing (Campylopterus falcatus) is found in Colombia, Ecuador, and Venezuela. In Venezuela, it inhabits the hilly parts of the country. The species is also found in the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta, which spans the border between Venezuela and Colombia, and extends south into Ecuador. The lazuline sabrewing occupies habitats at elevations generally between 1900 and 2100 meters, though it is most common between 800 and 2200 meters in Venezuela.

After signing with Arista Records, under Clive Davis (Context 11), Q-Tip began recording *Kamaal the Abstract*. Initially intended as his sophomore album after *Amplified* (Context 14), it was originally titled *Live at the Renaissance* (Context 10). The album was first scheduled to be released in 2001, but was later pushed back (Context 3). Arista ultimately shelved the album, deeming it lacking mainstream appeal (Context 3). A promo LP was released in 2001 (Context 3). Q-Tip later discussed the album in an interview with *XXL* magazine in 2005 (Context 4).

On November 12, 2009, Iberia Airlines confirmed that it had reached a preliminary agreement to merge with British Airways. The merger between the two carriers will create the world's third-largest airline in terms of revenue. On April 25, 2010, it was confirmed that British Airways and Iberia had agreed to a merger, forming the International Airlines Group, although each airline would continue to operate under its current brand. International Airlines Group, established its head office in London and its registered office in Madrid, operating mainly from its two main hubs of London Heathrow Airport and Adolfo Suárez Madrid–Barajas Airport with secondary hubs at London Gatwick Airport and Josep Tarradellas Barcelona–El Prat Airport.

Due to its proximity to Guam upon formation, the island was placed under the third Condition of Readiness level, the first time such a high level had been issued since Typhoon Pamela in 1982. Ike tracked 165 km (105 mi) southwest of Guam. Although Ike briefly attained typhoon status, its compact size mitigated damage. Despite estimated one-minute sustained winds of 110 km/h (70 mi) near the center, a station on Nimitz Hill documented winds of only 30 km/h (20 mi), with higher gusts.

Mary Elizabeth Wood was born on August 22, 1861, in the township of Elba, near Batavia, New York, to Edward Farmer Wood and Mary Jane (Humphrey) Wood. She was one of eight children, with seven brothers. Soon after her birth, the family moved to Batavia, where she grew up and attended local schools. Wood began her career as the first librarian at the Richmond Memorial Library in Batavia at the age of twenty-eight, a public library founded in 1889. She worked at the Richmond Memorial Library for approximately ten years.

The wing was organized at the beginning of February 1952 as part of a major reorganization of Air Defense Command (ADC) fighter units responding to ADC's difficulty under the existing wing base organizational structure in deploying fighter squadrons to best advantage. The wing replaced the 33d Fighter-Interceptor Wing (FIW) at Otis Air Force Base, Massachusetts five days later and assumed control of the 33 FIW's operational elements. The wing's 564th Air Base Group assumed support responsibilities for Otis AFB from the inactivating 33d Air Base Group and 33d Maintenance & Supply Groups. The operational squadrons transferred from the 33d FIW were the 58th Fighter-Interceptor Squadron (FIS) and 59th FIS at Otis AFB and the 60th FIS at Westover Air Force Base, Massachusetts. The 58th and 60th FIS flew F-86 Sabre aircraft, while the 59th FIS was equipped with F-94 Starfire aircraft. The wing also was assigned a federalized Air National Guard (ANG) squadron from the 101st FIW, the 133d FIS at Grenier Air Force Base, New Hampshire, flying World War II era F-47 Thunderbolt aircraft. The wing mission was to train and maintain tactical flying units in state of readiness in order to defend the northeastern United States.

Before the ritual commenced, an effigy was placed in each of the 27 (or in some sources 24 or 30) shrines of the Argei (sacra Argeorum) throughout the Servian regions. The effigies were thought to absorb pollution within the area, and their subsequent sacrifice was a ritual purification of the city. The pontiffs and Vestals were the main celebrants. The exact route of the procession among the stations is unclear.

*The Color of Water* explores the life of Ruth McBride Jordan, an African-American woman, and her son, James McBride. Ruth is reticent to discuss the details of her early life as a white, Jewish woman. Her father, Fishel Shilsky, whom she calls "Tateh" (Yiddish for "father" or "papa"), was an abusive and controlling man. He also mistreated Ruth's mother, Hudis Shilsky, known as "Mameh" (Yiddish for "mother" or "mama"), who was a gentle and quiet woman, crippled by polio. Ruth's family disowned her after she demonstrated her conversion and married a Black man, actions considered unacceptable by her deeply racist father.

Several Ferguson trademarks are registered in the UK for class 09, audio visual equipment. Ferguson Sp. z.o.o. registered EU010787471 on 04/04/2012. Technicolor, formerly Thomson Multimedia, registered EU003131927, dating back to 26/09/1946.

Some accept the pre-existence of Christ without accepting his full divinity in the Trinitarian sense. For example, it is likely that Arius and most early advocates of Arianism accepted the pre-existence of Christ. However, Thomas Aquinas says that Arius "pretended that the Person of the Son of God is a creature, and less than the Father, so he maintained that He began to be, saying 'there was a time when He was not.'"

Like other tribes, the Tohono Oʼodham felt land pressures from American ranchers, settlers, and the railroads. Documentation was poor, and many members did not leave their lands in a written will.: 92–3 John F. Trudell, a US attorney general assistant recorded an Oʼodham man declaring "I do not know anything about a land grant. The Mexicans never had any land to give us. From the earliest times our fathers have owned land which was given to them by the Earth's prophet.": 30 Because the Oʼodham lived on public lands or had no documentation of ownership, their holdings were threatened by white cattle herders in the 1880s. However, they used their history of cooperation with the government in the Apache Wars to bargain for land rights.: 27–28 Today, Oʼodham lands are made up of multiple reservations:

Peest is an esdorp which developed around the heath. It was first mentioned around 1300 as "apud Peest". The etymology of the name is unclear. The farms which were constructed prior to 1850 are parallel to the communal pasture while the later farms are at right angles. In 1840, the village had 80 inhabitants.

Moore was a Lecturer in Psychology at the University of Reading from 1971 to 1977, spending the year 1973-74 as a Fulbright-Hays Senior Scholar and Visiting Professor at the Department of Psychology, Brooklyn College of the City University of New York. In 1977 he was appointed University Lecturer in Experimental Psychology at the University of Cambridge where he was subsequently appointed Reader (1989) and Professor (1995). He became Emeritus Professor in 2014. He was appointed as Fellow of Wolfson College in 1983 and is now an Emeritus Fellow.

In the summer of 2019, EnterTRAINment Junction opened the world's largest marble (toy) display, which ranks as the second largest marble museum globally. This was done in conjunction with marble enthusiasts Larry and Cathy Svacina. The display, titled "Marbles," features marbles dating back to the 1800s, including games, collectables, memorabilia, and fun facts.

The second installment in the anime trilogy was announced in a second post-credits scene in the theatrical release of the film revealing the film's Japanese title, poster featuring Mechagodzilla and the film's 2018 release date. The film's Japanese title was revealed as Gojira: Kessen Kidō Zōshoku Toshi (translations varied from Godzilla: Battle Mobile Breeding City to Godzilla: The City Mechanized for the Final Battle), while the English title was later revealed as Godzilla: City on the Edge of Battle. In March 2018, the film's official website revealed a new poster, plot details, and that singer XAI would return to perform the film's theme song The Sky Falls. The English dub was produced by Post Haste Digital.

Alfred, Lord Tennyson recalled the shocked reaction in Britain upon hearing of Byron's death. The Greeks deeply mourned him and proclaimed him a national hero. Greece's national poet, Dionysios Solomos, wrote a poem titled "To the Death of Lord Byron" about the loss. "Vyron," or Βύρων, the Greek form of "Byron," remains a popular masculine name in Greece, and a suburb of Athens is named Vyronas in his honor.

In the vicinity of the temple, initially there was a trend of writing on the bamboo tree. The young men who came to visit the temple would return only by writing a name on bamboo as it is believed that writing the name of lovers will give success in their love. But, the temple committee has stopped the name writings on the bamboo as the name writings of the bamboo hampered the growth of the bamboo with the increase in bamboo writings. Nowadays, lovers tie sacred threads to the bamboo after worshiping Budha subba.

Elonka Dunin is a part-time cryptographer who has written books and articles on cryptography-related subjects, and has been interviewed in various media about her work. She first became involved in cryptography in mid-1999 after a hacker group released PhreakNIC v3.0 Code, an amateur cryptographic puzzle, which she cracked in 2000. Winning a prize for cracking the PhreakNIC v3.0 Code introduced her to the unsolved Kryptos sculpture, sparking her interest in more complex ciphers. In October 2002, Dunin gave a presentation on steganography at CIA headquarters and visited the Kryptos sculpture, located at CIA headquarters, observing it closely. In September 2003, she led the team that cracked Sanborn's Cyrillic Projector cipher. Dunin is also co-founder and co-moderator of the Kryptos Group.

The year 1965 marked the beginning of major military activity in what became known as the secret war in Laos, although the full extent of the conflict was not revealed to the American people until 1969-70. Despite its "secret" designation, news of the fighting frequently appeared in publications such as *The Bangkok Post* and *The New York Times*. Congress was kept well informed, with former CIA Director Richard Helms noting that the Appropriations subcommittees, which provided war funds, were briefed regularly. Senator Stuart Symington and other Congressmen visited Laos, indicating their approval, believing it was a cheaper and better way to fight a war in Southeast Asia than committing American troops, as Helms pointed out.

Barry Stanton was born in London on 23 January 1941. The Stanton family—Stella Rose, Carl Leopold, Barry John and Rodney—migrated to Australia in 1948. They settled in the Sydney suburb of Neutral Bay. At the age of 15 Stanton became interested in rock and roll. He left school and worked as apprentice motor mechanic. His early bands included the Boppers and the Bellairs; they played gigs at local dance halls. Stanton provided "some Presley-type singing and guitar-playing." In 1957 he recorded material, which was not released until 1988, issued on his compilation album, A Tribute to the King Rare Songs 1957-1965, via Canetoad Records. According to Ainslie Baker of The Australian Women's Weekly, he "has brown eyes, is 6ft. 1in. tall, weighs 13-1⁄2 stone, likes cars and car-racing, surfing, and football."

In 1956 Giovanna Caleffi relocated to Nervi, which by this time had been subsumed into the long coastal Genoa conurbation. During the resistance years Nervi had been the operational base of a force of anarchist partisans which had included Lorenzo Parodi, and it was to Nervi that she had just transferred the management of "Volontà" in order to facilitate national distribution. The publication was in a period of crisis, since the personal break-up between Caleffi and Zaccaria had coincided with the latters' withdrawal from the anarchist movement. There are suggestions that his somewhat cerebral quasi-liberal approach had always presented a slightly awkward fit with the more passionate radicalism of Italian anarchism.

The SS *Clarksdale Victory* was a Victory ship, a class of cargo ship mass-produced during World War II. Built for the American President Lines, she served as a supply ship during the Battle of Okinawa from April 26 to May 8, 1945. On April 27, 1945, a shell landed approximately 45 feet (14 m) from the *Clarksdale Victory*, sending fragments across the deck and damaging one lifeboat. On the same day, the *Canada Victory* was struck by a kamikaze plane, causing an explosion that blew out the side of the ship, resulting in its sinking within seven minutes.

Demographic information for the Whanganui District Health Board (DHB) area is available from the 2018 New Zealand census. The North Island had a population of 3,808,005 at the 2023 New Zealand census, an increase of 213,453 people (5.9%) since the 2018 census, and an increase of 570,957 people (17.6%) since the 2013 census. Of the total population, 733,893 people (19.3%) were aged under 15 years, 743,154 (19.5%) were 15 to 29, 1,721,427 (45.2%) were 30 to 64, and 609,534 (16.0%) were 65 or older.

The entertainment at the 2014 NRL Grand Final included a performance by Slash of his new single "Bent To Fly" from *World on Fire*. Due to Myles Kennedy's tour with Alter Bridge, his vocals were played over the loudspeakers. ANZ Stadium's screens showed NRL season highlights, while TV viewers saw Slash playing guitar with pyrotechnics. After "Bent To Fly," Slash played the opening riff of "Sweet Child O' Mine," concluding his set.

Bang! Bang! Eche! began playing shows sometime after synth player Perry, formerly a drum & bass DJ known as "Vitamin P" at age 6, joined the band. Towards the end of 2007, the band wrote and recorded their self-titled demo EP, which was released in May 2008 and made available for free download on their MySpace page. The EP did not chart on US College Radio, nor was it played on KEXP or any Australian network. However, their first two singles, "4 To The Floor" and "Nikee," both reached number one on the New Zealand bNet (NZ college network). "4 To The Floor" charted for 10 weeks, and "Time Mismanagement" charted for 1 week on the NZ Industry Radioscope chart in 2008.

The township (Stadt) of Wildberg is situated on the eastern edge of the district of Calw, in the German state of Baden-Württemberg, and lies along the border with the district of Böblingen to the east. Wildberg is physically located in the valley of the Enz, between the Northern Black Forest to the west and the Upper Gäu [de] to the east. Elevation above sea level in the municipal area ranges from a high of 638 meters (2,093 ft) Normalnull (NN) to a low of 356 meters (1,168 ft) NN.

Due to the proximity of Ike to Guam upon its formation, the island was placed under the "Condition of Readiness" level; this was the first time that such a high readiness level was issued since Typhoon Pamela in 1982. Although Ike passed somewhat near the island, the storm's compact size during its formative stages mitigated any damage. Despite being near typhoon intensity at the time, a station on Nimitz Hill only documented winds of 30 km/h (19 mph), with higher gusts.

The Royal Botanic Garden, Sydney, encompasses 29-30 hectares of cultivated land, surrounded by 51 hectares of open parklands known as The Domain. The Garden forms a natural amphitheatre, sloping down towards Farm Cove. It is divided into four major precincts: the Lower Gardens, the Middle Gardens, the Palace Gardens, and the Bennelong precinct. Approximately in the middle of these four precincts is the Palm Grove Centre. The Palm Grove Centre offers a restaurant, cafe, visitors centre and bookshop.

On review aggregator website Rotten Tomatoes, the film holds an approval rating of 89%, with an average rating of 7.2/10 based on 44 reviews. Its critical consensus reads, "It takes its time coming together, but the quietly effective Paddleton pulls off a tricky tonal balancing act, thanks largely to the strengths of its well-chosen leads." Metacritic, which uses a weighted average, assigned a score of 70 out of 100 based on 17 critics, indicating "generally favorable reviews."

For the All Hope Is Gone World Tour, Lawrence Upton served as the tour's director, while Philippe Vachon acted as the tour's programmer and lighting co-director. Upton desired "very aggressive lighting that would attack and have punch" to complement Slipknot's intense music. Up-stage provided the gear package, which included 10 Martin Professional MAC Profiles and 54 Martin Mac 2000 Wash units. The MAC Profiles were used as "key lights," while the Mac 2000 Wash units generated higher light output. An issue arose during the tour where band members were sometimes barely visible due to the intensity of the lighting, requiring adjustments to increase front-of-house lighting with white light.

At this stage 43 clubs from the Football League Third Division North and South joined 25 non-league clubs having come through the qualifying rounds. Gateshead and Northampton Town as the strongest Third Division finishers in the previous season, were given a bye to the Third Round along with Stockport County, who qualified to the fifth round the last season. Of the four new clubs admitted to Football League this season, only Gillingham entered in this round and the other three were required to start in the qualifying rounds (which caused Shrewsbury Town to withdraw in protest). To make the number of matches up, non-league Willington received a bye to this round. Matches were played on Saturday, 25 November 1950. Six matches were drawn, with replays taking place later the same week.

By the mid-20th century, Vancouver had become the primary cultural, social, and religious centre for Punjabi Canadians, though by 1923, the largest ethnic Indian population in North America resided in another city. Throughout this period, the Punjabi population faced significant restrictions due to discriminatory immigration policies. However, the aftermath of World War Two prompted a reconsideration of oppressive measures, leading to the re-enfranchisement of Indo-Canadians in 1947.

The Thompson Ice House is located on Route 129 in the Midcoast village of South Bristol, Lincoln County, Maine, near the junction with McFarlands Cove Road. Adjacent to the ice house is Thompson's Ice Pond, a small, approximately 1-acre body of water. The Thompson Ice House itself is a reconstruction of the original ice house, built on the same site using salvaged materials. The exterior walls are made from wood and packed with sawdust for insulation. On the east and west sides of the building, inclined planes facilitate the movement of ice cakes into and out of the structure. A channel on the east side of the ice house allows for the floating of ice blocks from the pond directly into the building.

By 1923, Vancouver became the primary cultural, social, and religious centre of Punjabi Canadians as it had the largest ethnic Indian population of any city in North America. The Punjabi population in Canada would remain relatively stable throughout the mid 20th century as the exclusionary immigration policies practiced by the Canadian government continued. However, a shift began to occur after World War Two. The Canadian government re-enfranchised the Indo-Canadian community with the right to vote in 1947.

Ashton was selected for the England national team for a match against Northern Ireland, played at Windsor Park, Belfast, on 24 October 1925. He played as a centre forward and was appointed captain for the match, his first and only cap for his country. The game ended in a 0-0 draw. Ashton holds the distinction of being named captain for England in his debut match, despite never having played in a single Football League game.

On 23 March 2019, following the Battle of Baghuz Fawqani, Islamic State lost its final significant territory in Syria to the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) backed by the US and its coalition partners, including the UK. This was widely announced as the "defeat of ISIL" by the SDF and its allies. British Prime Minister Theresa May praised the courage of the British Armed Forces and its allies and stated: "The liberation of the last Daesh-held territory wouldn't have been possible without the immense courage of UK military and our allies". Major General Chris Ghika, Deputy Commander Strategy and Information of CJTF-OIR, stated that "Operation Shader would remain" as ISIL was not "leaderless or rudderless" despite its losses. He added that he could not predict how the losses would affect the terror threat posed by ISIL to the UK.

The U.S. market for manufactured goods, the world's largest at $7.2 trillion, sees domestic producers holding 69% of the market. The U.S. dominates manufacturing equipment, with 41.7% of the global market.

On September 22, the owners of Belmont Stakes winner Tiz the Law announced he would skip the Preakness. Kentucky Derby winner Authentic, who had previously won five of six career starts, became the favorite. Entries for the Preakness were taken on Monday, and Authentic drew post position 8 in a field of eleven.

Wright began his career surveying the frontier areas of Oneida and Oswego counties. In 1794 Wright was hired as a surveyor and planner by the noted English canal designer William Weston. Working for Weston, he helped lay out canals and locks on the Mohawk River. After Weston returned to England in 1801, Wright was commissioned to survey the Mohawk River between Schenectady and Rome, and then to the Hudson River.

John Nestor, a physician and FDA official, gained notoriety in the Washington, D.C. area in 1984 after *The Washington Post* published a letter detailing his unusual driving habits. Nestor favored driving in the leftmost lane of multilane highways, setting his cruise control to 55 mph, the posted speed limit at the time. He maintained this speed regardless of other drivers wishing to exceed it, refusing to move to the right. Nestor defended his actions by stating that he had no obligation to inconvenience himself for speeders, believing he was upholding the law and performing a public service by adhering to the speed limit. This practice led to the coining of the term "Nestoring" to describe his firm conformity to traffic regulations.

During the 1974 model year, sales of the model line began to collapse following the 1973 oil crisis. For 1973, the Chevrolet/GMC Suburban received a fourth passenger door for the first time, placing the widely-available model line in direct competition with the fuel-thirsty Travelall (achieving 10-12mpg on average). Though several years older than the Suburban (and far larger in size over the Jeep Wagoneer), the Travelall still retained high owner loyalty and satisfaction.

The 1991 elections were held as the previous Lok Sabha, with Chandra Sekhar at its helm had been dissolved just 16 months after government formation. Over 500 million eligible voters were once again given the chance to elect their government. The elections were held in a polarised environment and are also referred to as the 'Mandal-Mandir' elections after the two most important poll issues, the Mandal Commission fallout and the Ram Janmabhoomi-Babri Masjid issue.

Stonelake elected unopposed to the Aberdare Urban District Council in 1904. He retained his seat in 1907. He became chairman of the Council 1910. During the pre-war period he was a prominent advocate of building council houses, a programme that commenced before the First World War and continued thereafter. In 1922, however, he was forced to resign from the authority following a corruption scandal.

American Music reviewed the work, which they felt was "a timely critique of the musical, social, and cultural relationships between rap music, black culture, and American society". Black Noise also received reviews from Popular Music and Contemporary Sociology, the latter of which called it an "exciting book and an essential text for those interested in popular culture, music, race, gender, postmodernism-all central aspects of contemporary U.S. culture."

Olavo de Carvalho claimed that climate change was a hoax, referring to it as "Climategate," and that the Spanish Inquisition "is a fictional invention of Protestants" (Context 9). He based his claims on the Climategate controversy, which involved the unauthorized release of emails from the University of East Anglia's Climatic Research Unit (CRU) in November 2009 (Context 2, 7). This occurred shortly before the Copenhagen international climate talks (Context 1). Carvalho alleged that a "new world order" was behind the "Climategate" hoax (Context 9). Sarah Palin called for outright boycotts of the Copenhagen international climate talks (Context 1). He opposed vaccination and believed Barack Obama was not born in the United States (Context 12).

After retiring from baseball, Faut focused on her family and worked as a secretary. She divorced Karl Winsch in 1968 and married salesman Charles Eastman in 1977. This Charles Eastman is not to be confused with Charles Alexander Eastman, also known as Ohiyesa, the famed Dakota author and lecturer who died in 1939. Faut relocated to Rock Hill, South Carolina, with her two sons, where she still resides.

Löscher did postdoctoral research work for five years particularly in neuropharmacology and toxicology in Germany and Denmark. His research interests include pharmacology of antiepileptic (antiseizure) drugs, the mechanisms of antiseizure drugs, and the pathophysiology of temporal lobe epilepsy with the aim to find new targets for treatment and also evaluated antiepileptic drugs for dogs. While at FU Berlin, Löscher worked alongside Hans-Hasso Frey to pioneer neurochemical studies of antiepileptic drugs such as valproate on the synthesis and metabolism of the inhibitory neurotransmitter GABA. After his move to Hannover, models, and mechanisms of resistance to antiepileptic drugs developed into one of the major research areas of Löscher group.

Eastern Kentucky native Dr. Patrick Angel initially dedicated his career to the Office of Surface Mining Reclamation and Enforcement (OSMRE). However, around 2002, scientists like Dr. Angel began to recognize that Appalachian forests devastated by strip-mining were failing to regenerate. Subsequently, Dr. Angel spearheaded efforts to plant over 187 million trees across more than 275,000 acres of former mining land in Appalachia. *Hibbertia bicarpellata* is an erect shrub that typically grows to a height of 0.3–1 m (1 ft 0 in – 3 ft 3 in). Its branches feature prominent leaf scars and are covered, along with the leaves, in stellate hairs. The leaves are linear to narrowly elliptic, ranging from 4–12 mm (0.16–0.47 in) long and 0.5–1.5 mm (0.020–0.059 in) wide, with a petiole 0.5–1 mm (0.020–0.039 in) long. The flowers are arranged in terminal clusters on peduncles 2–6 mm (0.079–0.236 in) long, with bracts that are lance-shaped to egg-shaped, 2–3 mm (0.079–0.118 in) long. The sepals are described as narrowly oblong to lance-shaped, with outer lobes 5–7 mm (0.20–0.28 in) long and inner lobes 4–6 mm (0.16–0.24 in) long. The petals are egg-shaped, yellow, and 6–8 mm (0.24–0.31 in) long. There are 6–10 stamens arranged around the 2 carpels, each containing 2 ovules. Flowering occurs from September to November.

It is occasionally found on fens among and on the fringes of coniferous forests.

In *O'Donnabhain v. Commissioner of Internal Revenue*, decided on February 2, 2010, the United States Tax Court addressed the deductibility of medical expenses related to gender identity disorder (GID). The taxpayer, diagnosed with GID, sought to deduct costs associated with hormone therapy and sex reassignment surgery, including breast augmentation, under Internal Revenue Code Section 213. The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) initially disallowed the deduction, arguing that these procedures did not treat a medically recognized disease or promote proper bodily function, essentially viewing them as cosmetic. The court ruled in favor of O'Donnabhain, reversing the IRS position and establishing that treatment for GID qualifies as deductible medical care.

The Fosters was originally conceived by openly gay creators Bradley Bredeweg and Peter Paige who wanted to write a drama that reflected the "modern American family". After originally considering a story about two gay fathers, the pair decided the subject of two men raising a family had already been done on television and began to instead consider a story about two women. When asked about the concept of two lesbian mothers raising a blended family, Bredeweg stated, "We realized that there was a kind of a vacuum when it came to stories about women raising families. So we set off in that direction. Many of our own friends are moms raising biological kids. Some of them have fostered and adopted. Suddenly, we realized that we had a story here that hadn't been told on television before." Additionally, certain elements of the series which deal with the foster care system are said to have been inspired by a troubled childhood friend of Bredeweg, who struggled in the foster system before eventually being adopted in her senior year of high school.

For small or backyard growers, collecting and destroying all leaf debris on the ground is an absolute necessity due to the potency of this disease because the fungus overwinters in this leftover leaf litter. This is its main form of survival. By removing and destroying these leaves, a grower can significantly decrease the amount of primary inoculum available in the spring. It will greatly decrease the apparent infection rate. There has also been a study done on the addition of a straw mulch bedding to the ground after all the leaves have been picked up. The addition of this mulch further reduced the spring infection rate. Leaf litter removal is not very practical for large commercial growers due labor needs and number of trees but if at all possible, a majority of the old leaves should try to be collected.

A central tenet of Julian Simon's work was a critique of the notion that natural resources are finite. He argued that the term "finite" was inappropriate and misleading when applied to resources, contending that it was only accurate in a purely physical sense, such as the limited size of the Earth. Simon considered the issue of whether the quantity of copper on Earth is technically finite as "just semantic." He believed that focusing on physical limits muddles public discussion and leads to wrongheaded policy decisions. To illustrate, Simon pointed out that while the quantity of services from copper is limited at any given moment, it should not be considered finite because human ingenuity can discover new deposits, develop more efficient extraction methods, find substitutes, or even obtain copper from sources beyond Earth, such as the moon or asteroids.

Richard Dering composed three books of motets with continuo, two books of canzonets, and one book of continuo madrigals. His innovative use of a basso continuo part in his madrigals is considered an early example of the nascent Baroque style in English music. His continuo madrigals and small concertato motets are very much in the idiom of Alessandro Grandi or d'India, with wayward modulations and dramatic expression. *Cantica Sacra* (1618) contains 6-part motets that recall a more conventionally expressive Italian madrigal-like idiom. Another work with the title *Cantica Sacra* was published in 1662.

Matej Pavšič considered mirror particles as part of his research into the foundations of theoretical physics. He proposed in 1974 that nature could be exactly symmetric with respect to space inversion if for each ordinary particle there exists a corresponding "mirror" particle. The idea of mirror particles was introduced in 1966 by Yu. Kobzarev, L.B. Okun, and I.Ya. Pomeranchuk as a consequence of the discovery of CP-violation in 1964, following T.D. Lee and C.N. Yang's 1956 paper questioning parity conservation in weak interactions. Exact parity models, which replicate the Standard Model, are considered as an explanation of dark matter.

Gard walks her to his family's farm, where his mother returns the favor by filling her box with freshly cleaned catfish. Mattie misses supper but has no appetite when she returns home, and Eliza discerns from a tune she is humming that Mattie is taken with Gard and yearning to be a woman. Eliza innocently gives her daughter advice about judging men and Mattie bursts into tears, accusing her mother of trying to push her out. Eliza realizes that Mattie has glimpsed leaving home as a wife. While Jess recalls that Eliza herself once similarly shed tears, Eliza remembers leaving home as a happy time, humming the same song as Mattie, which she also fancied in her youth.

Landwirtschaftliche Rentenbank actively promotes agribusiness and rural areas through a variety of initiatives. Its special promotional loans are geared towards agriculture and agribusiness, renewable energy, and rural development. Beyond the agricultural sector, Rentenbank finances regional authorities and banks in rural areas, as well as banks within Germany and the broader EU. In the food industry, Rentenbank supports innovation, particularly startups developing tech-based business ideas. Through civic engagement and public-sector investments, Rentenbank promotes aquaculture and fisheries, and the expansion of broadband coverage. Its statutory promotional mandate includes providing loans at favorable interest rates for agriculture-related investments. These loans are extended to businesses via other banks on a competitively neutral basis. Rentenbank also awards grants for innovations, applied research projects, and institutions of particular importance to agribusiness and rural areas. The Rentenbank's Rehwinkel Foundation supports scientific research, promotes collaboration between agricultural science and industry, and awards scholarships to students in relevant Master's programs.

Born in Almaty on 5 December 1916. Little is known about her early life or even when she moved to the Uzbek SSR before her rise to fame. In the mid 1930’s she attended the local aeroclub while working as a radio operator for the railway in Tashkent. At the aeroclub, her instructor was Abdusamat Taymetov, the first Uzbek pilot. In August 1935 she became the first Uzbek woman to parachute from an airplane.

Egypt sent a five player strong delegation to Rio. The team included Ibrahim Al Husseini Hamadtou, the only table tennis player to compete while holding the paddle in his mouth. He went to the Rio Games when he was a 41-year-old. ElSaied Ragab was selected as an umpire for the Paralympic Games in table tennis.

In France, "L'Autre Finistère" debuted at number 22 on the chart edition of 27 February 1993, which was the highest debut that week; however, it dropped the two weeks later until reaching number 39, but climbed the following weeks and eventually peaked at number ten for a sole week in its 11th week. It remained for 17 weeks in the top 50, nine of them in the top 20. On the European Hot 100, it started at number 100 on 3 April 1993, reached a peak of number 54 in its eighth week and fell off the chart after ten weeks of presence.

Belmont's residents are a diverse mix of races, ethnicities, religious affiliations, sexual orientations, and national origins. Traditionally the Italian heart of the Bronx, it now has a significant population of Hispanic and Latino Americans, Albanians, long-standing Italians, and more recent Italian immigrants. Many Mexican families have also moved there, and Belmont hosts the annual Bronx Cinco de Mayo Celebration. As of the 2010 census, Hispanics and Latinos make up the neighborhood's largest ethnic group and form a majority.

Robinson taught at several college and universities in Toronto, including the University of Windsor (his alma mater) as writer-in-residence from 1992 to 1993. He was best known for the Inspector Banks series of novels set in the fictional Yorkshire town of Eastvale. His first novel, Gallows View, was published in 1987. It garnered him the Crime Writers of Canada Arthur Ellis Award, which he went on to win six more times during his career. The series was eventually translated into twenty languages at the time of his death. He also wrote two collections of short stories – Not Safe After Dark (1998) and The Price of Love (2009) – as well as another novel, Caedmon's Song, released in 1990.

As a student, Paterson received bursaries at Ridley College and the University of Western Ontario. Paterson has served for over a decade as a member of the board of governors of Ridley College. In 2004, Paterson gave a $1 million donation to his University of Western Ontario alma mater to open the G. Scott Paterson Wing in the school's Medical Sciences building. At the public announcement of his donation, Paterson described the impetus for his gift to the school in this way, "Ideas may start here that lead to discoveries that will extend or perhaps even save lives ... It's great to be a part of that."

The building was at first constructed in rather an undistinguished Perpendicular style, possessing a tower at the west end of the church. The church is best known for the twelve panels of its late-Gothic rood screen, rivalled in quality only by the rood screen of St. Helen's Church in Ranworth. The twelve panels, probably from between 1440 and 1450, appear to have been influenced by Flemish work, and depict St. Apollonia, St. Zita, St. Barbara, and the nine choirs of angels. (The representations of the Dominions and the Seraphim were defaced during the English Civil War, most likely due to their inclusion of “papistical” symbols such as the tiara and a censer). In the South Nave there are more recent (ca. 1490) representations of King Henry VI, St. Edmund, St. Edward the Confessor, and St. Olaf.

Born in Limburg an der Lahn, Philippi studied musicology, journalism as well as general and comparative literature at the Johannes Gutenberg University Mainz from 1985 to 1992. From 1985 to 1989, she also studied at the Episcopal Institute of Church Music of the Diocese of Mainz, where she graduated in 1989 with the church musician examination C. In 1992, she received her doctorate under Christoph-Hellmut Mahling at the Musicological Institute of the Johannes Gutenberg University Mainz on the subject Antonín Dvořák – The Spectre's Bride (Svatební košile) op. 69 and The St. Ludmila (Svatá Ludmila) op. 71. studies of the great vocal form in the 19th century.

Gospatric, Earl of Northumbria, was the father of at least three sons and several daughters. Symeon of Durham and *De obsessione Dunelmi* name Gospatric's sons as Dolfin, Waltheof, and Gospatric. A writ drawn up by the monks of Holmcultram around 1275 described Dolfin and Gospatric as illegitimate, claiming Waltheof was the only legitimate son, though *The Scots Peerage* disputes the writ's reliability. Ethelreda was a full sibling of Dolfin and Waltheof. According to the *Chronicon Cumbrie*, Waltheof of Allerdale had three sisters: Gunhilda, Maud, and Ethelreda. Ethelreda's son was William FitzDuncan.

Lewis was married three times and had numerous children. Some news outlets reported he had nine surviving children, others, 10 children. One of his children is actress Juliette Lewis with whom he acted in at least two films, Blueberry (aka Renegade) and The Way of the Gun. His other children include his daughters Brandy, Hannah and Dierdre Lewis and Emily Colombier; his sons Peter, Lightfield, Miles and Matthew; and nine grandchildren.

Justice Stephen Breyer wrote a plurality opinion in *Colorado Republican Federal Campaign Committee v. FEC*. Justices Ruth Bader Ginsburg, Sonia Sotomayor, and Elena Kagan joined Breyer's dissent in other cases, and his *Whole Woman's Health* opinion has been consistently applied by courts. Breyer based his opinion on the distinction between independent and coordinated campaign expenditures established in *Buckley v. Valeo*. He believed some restrictions on expenditures by political parties were unconstitutional. Breyer found the Party Expenditure Provision of the FECA unconstitutional as applied to the Colorado Republican Party's independent advertisement, which received First Amendment protection.

More than 85% of Oklahoma City University's full-time faculty hold terminal degrees. All classes meet in person. The student-to-faculty ratio is 11:1. Average class sizes are 17 students for freshmen and 13 for upperclassmen.

Ships in the eastern Atlantic Ocean suggested that a tropical storm was present on October 28 with peak winds of 50 mph (80 km/h). It was estimated to have originated the previous day. The small storm moved to the northwest and later west into an area of sparse observations, suggesting a weaker system. The system's track ended on October 29 due to uncertainty, but it was estimated to have dissipated soon after.

The score for *Eternals* was composed by Ramin Djawadi, who previously provided the score for Marvel Studios' *Iron Man* (2008), making it the first music in the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU). Two tracks from the *Eternals* soundtrack, "Eternals Theme" and "Across the Oceans of Time," were released as singles on October 22, 2021. The full soundtrack album was released digitally by Hollywood Records and Marvel Music on November 3, 2021. The soundtrack also features several licensed songs. "Time" by Pink Floyd plays over the opening MCU logo. "Sugarfoot" by Black Joe Lewis & The Honeybears plays during a montage of London reintroducing Sersi in the present day. "The End of the World" by Skeeter Davis plays when Phastos bids his family goodbye. "Feels Like the First Time" by Foreigner plays during the credits following the mid-credits scene.

Upon his election in March 1800, Pius VII appointed his friend Ercole Consalvi, who had served as secretary during the conclave in Venice, to the College of Cardinals and the office of Cardinal Secretary of State. Consalvi accompanied Pius VII to Rome, arriving on July 3, 1800. Subsequently, Consalvi traveled to France to negotiate the Concordat of 1801 with Napoleon Bonaparte, then First Consul. This agreement, formally promulgated in 1802, re-established the Catholic Church in France, recognizing the Roman Catholic Church as the religion of the majority of French citizens.

Construction at AFRRI's main headquarters/facility in Bethesda, Maryland began in November 1960, preceding the organization's official charter and establishment dates. It was occupied by January 1962 and fully operational by November 1963. Facilities include a Training, Research, Isotopes, General Atomics (TRIGA) nuclear reactor, laboratory space, an animal facility. a high-dose cobalt-60 facility, a 54-megaelectron volt linear accelerator (LINAC), and a low-level cobalt-60 irradiation facility.

In Europe, the Fuel Quality Directive permits fuel producers to blend up to 3% methanol by volume into gasoline. The U.S. allows up to 0.3% methanol by volume, or up to 2.75% if blended with an equal volume of butanol or a higher molecular weight alcohol. In contrast, China utilizes methanol as a transportation fuel in blends ranging from M5 to M100. Most modern gasoline vehicles can use alcohol fuels with minor modifications, potentially improving octane levels.

With investments totalling $25 billion since the early 1980s, the project is the largest resource development in Australian history. In the late 1980s, it was the largest engineering project in the world. The Venture is underpinned by huge hydrocarbon reserves within the Carnarvon Basin, with only about one-third of the Venture's estimated total reserves of 33 trillion cubic feet (930 km3) of gas produced to date.

Historian Frank Bongiorno described Hirst as a "creative historian capable of engaging a wide audience, as well as a public intellectual who delighted, infuriated and provoked". He contributed many opinion pieces and commentaries to Australian newspapers and journals. Jeremy Sammut has noted that Hirst was motivated by an independent mind and a distaste for unthinking conformity. He "defied simplistic categorisation as a partisan because his politics were idiosyncratic". Sammut wrote Hirst was committed "the rigorous pursuit of historical truth that drove him to explore the deeper patterns and meanings of the past, and the contemporary implications, that others had missed or misled us about". Hirst described himself as an old-fashioned social democrat.

Erminia Giuliano took over the Giuliano clan crime business after her brother, Luigi Giuliano, was arrested in early 2000. He was the last of five male family members to be arrested. In September 2002, Luigi became a *pentito* and testified against various Camorra figures, including members of his own clan. Erminia became the boss because the only direct male heir still unimprisoned, her nephew Pio Vittorio Giuliano, was deemed inept.

The name Chernobyl has become synonymous with the concept of a nuclear energy disaster. Referencing the political damage from the inept initial response to the disaster, pundits sometimes use the phrase "Chernobyl moment" to describe alleged analogous damaging failures, such as responses to the COVID-19 pandemic. Ukraine has announced an intention to open the disaster area to tourism. Numerous cultural works have referenced the disaster, some of which are listed in this article.

As a British woman, Muriel Darton was among the first to be enfranchised following the Representation of the People Act. She first appeared in the Electoral Register in 1919, residing at Cromwell Crescent. Darton ran a photographic materials business in Crouch End, London. She died on 3 January 1945. At the time of her death, she was 62 years old. According to probate records, she left her effects of £1,181 to her sister, Muriel Hope Rose.

The Mouassine Mosque, also known as La Mosquee Mouassine, is a significant historical mosque located in the Mouassine district in the heart of the Medina of Marrakesh. Constructed between 1562-1563 AD (970 AH) and 1572-1573 AD (980 AH) by the Saadian sultan Abdallah al-Ghalib, the mosque is part of a larger complex. This complex includes a library, a hammam, a midha (ablutions facility), and a former children's Qur'anic school (msid). The Mouassine Fountain, located just east of the mosque, is one of the city's finest public fountains and a landmark in the Medina. It features a carved wood awning and filigreed iron grill. The Mouassine Fountain is one of the largest in Marrakesh, along with the fountain of the Bab Doukkala Mosque and the Shrob ou Shouf Fountain, all from the Saadian period.

In November 1971, Thaler was elected to the New York Supreme Court. On December 21, he and three associates were indicted for selling $800,000 worth of stolen Treasury bills in 1970. On December 23, 1971, he pleaded not guilty. In February 1972, four counts of perjury were added to the indictment. On March 23, he was convicted of unlawfully receiving and disposing of stolen securities, and of perjury. On August 8, he was sentenced to one year in prison, and fined $10,000. On November 2, he was disbarred by the Appellate Division. On March 5, 1973, the conviction was upheld by the United States Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit.

The North Carolina Constitution governs the executive branch. The first state constitution, established in 1776, outlined positions within the executive branch. The governor was popularly elected for a two-year term after the 1835 amendments. The Council of State currently has ten elected officials.

The *El Rio*, hull number 467, was launched on July 3, 1917, by the William Cramp & Sons Ship and Engine Building Company of Philadelphia. Completed for the Morgan Line in September 1917, the *El Rio* had a gross register tonnage (GRT) of 7,433. She was assigned U.S. Official Number 215326 and signal code LKQW, with New York as her home port. The *El Rio* was intended for service between New York and Galveston, Texas, a route of the Morgan Line, which was later incorporated into the Southern Pacific system.

The film received mixed reviews from critics. On the review aggregator website Rotten Tomatoes, 60% of 15 critics' reviews are positive, with an average rating of 5.6/10. Metacritic, which uses a weighted average, assigned the film a score of 56 out of 100, based on 4 critics, indicating "mixed or average" reviews.

Michigan and Arizona held their presidential primaries on Tuesday, February 28, and allocated 29 and 30 Republican delegates, respectively. CNN moderated a Republican primary debate in Mesa, Arizona on February 22, 2012. The Arizona Republican primary is 'winner-take-all' and the Michigan Republican delegate results are allocated proportionately among the candidates by voting district. Romney won the Michigan primary narrowly, and won the Arizona primary by a wide margin.

The All-American Girls Professional Baseball League (AAGPBL) was founded in 1943 by Philip K. Wrigley, owner of the Chicago Cubs and a chewing gum corporation executive, who sought to fill ballparks in the event that World War II jeopardized Major League Baseball. Initially named the All-American Girls Softball League, the league played a hybrid of softball and baseball. Women's softball was popular in the early 1940s, with *Time* magazine estimating approximately 40,000 teams in the U.S. by June 1943, including popular touring clubs such as Barney Ross' Adorables and Slapsie Maxie's Curvaceous Cuties. Almost 300 women from the United States and Canada attended the initial call for the league. Of these, 75 were invited to final try-outs in Chicago, where 60 were chosen to become the first women to play in the AAGPBL. The league evolved over time, with overhand pitching introduced in 1949. The AAGPBL operated until 1954.

Universities have a certain level of autonomy and are allowed to receive funds from other sources, including private donations and from their research (e.g. patents) and other marketable pursuits, as long as this does not exceed the funding received from the Ministry. The ultimate responsibility for the management of the university also belongs to the Ministry, so universities cannot be independent from government control and the Minister for Education is able to take charge of all university activities if it deems necessary, including curriculum, research, policy, and the university management itself.

In 2017, Mike Dee joined Audacy, Inc., formerly known as Entercom, as President of Sports. He was recruited by David Field, Chairman, President, and CEO of Audacy, to elevate the company's sports business and develop new initiatives. In this role, Dee oversaw Audacy's sports business, including its play-by-play relationships across major professional sports and Power-5 Conference NCAA relationships. A senior management shakeup occurred at Audacy in 2023, resulting in Dee transitioning to a senior advisor role.

The New Zealand General Service Medal 2002 (NZGSM 2002) was established by royal warrant to recognize service since January 1, 2000. The New Zealand General Service Medal 2002 (Iraq 2015) is awarded to New Zealand Defence Force personnel who have served in Iraq supporting operations to build the capacity of the Iraqi Security Forces since November 2014. Eligibility extends to New Zealand citizens or residents employed by, or contracted to, the New Zealand Defence Force and deployed in the operational area. Other individuals may be eligible, as determined by regulation on the advice of the Chief of Defence Force.

Edward Thomas Ryan's research significantly contributes to the understanding of host-bacterial pathogen interactions and immune responses during cholera, an acute secretory diarrheal infection caused by toxigenic strains of *Vibrio cholerae*. His work, supported by organizations like the WHO, focuses on developing vaccines protective against enteric infections, with a specific emphasis on *Vibrio cholerae*. Ryan has collaborated with researchers such as Stephen B. Calderwood, John R. Butterton, and others to develop and optimize models for studying *Vibrio cholerae* infection and evaluating oral cholera vaccines. A significant aspect of his research involves analyzing the O-specific polysaccharide (OSP) of lipopolysaccharide (LPS), a key component of the *Vibrio cholerae* outer membrane, and the OSP-specific antibody responses it elicits. Ryan's work has demonstrated that vibriocidal antibodies largely target OSP and that anti-O-specific polysaccharide (OSP) immune responses following vaccination with oral cholera vaccine correlate with protection against cholera.

The team competed in the 2012 Summer Paralympics from 30 August to 7 September 2012, in London, England. In Group B, they finished the round-robin in third place behind China and Iran. The team was beaten by Brazil in the quarter-finals, 0:3, and finished in seventh overall place.

The Aboriginal Arts Board (AAB) was established in 1973 as one of the seven grant boards of the Australia Council. The board was composed of Aboriginal artists, writers, and performers. Its purpose was to allocate public money to promote both traditional and contemporary Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander arts, encourage creative endeavors, elevate the standing of Aboriginal arts, and direct funds to Aboriginal communities. Dick Roughsey was the inaugural chairman of the AAB, later succeeded by Wandjuk Marika. One of the AAB's earliest activities was a "National Seminar on Aboriginal Arts" hosted at the Australian National University in May 1973.

Warburton is notable for having two Anglican churches dedicated to St Werburgh. The ancient church of St Werburgh (grid reference WA13 9SS) is a Grade I listed building of 'significant importance' and is one of only 27 timber-framed churches surviving in England. Dating back 1,000 years, it retains rough-hewn timber uprights supporting the roof beams. Fixtures and furnishings show St Werburgh's journey through time, from a twelfth-century stone sarcophagus to the patch of nineteenth-century Minton tiles on the floor. Near the church is the base of an old village cross. The other church in Warburton dedicated to St Werburgh was built from clay pug in 1873 by a landowner on their own estate.

At the close of 1966, Les Gilbert and Keith Barber reformed The Wild Cherries, recruiting Danny Robinson, formerly a bass player and vocalist for The Weird Mob, as their new singer. Peter Eddey, also from The Weird Mob where he played lead guitar, joined as bass guitarist. In January 1967, Gilbert switched to organ, enabling the band's experimental sound, and Lobby Loyde completed this second incarnation of The Wild Cherries.

Robert Hodgson Heptinstall was born in Keswick, Cumbria, England. He earned his medical degree from Charing Cross Hospital Medical School in 1943 and served in the Royal Army Medical Corps (RAMC) during World War II, where he was trained for missions in Japanese-occupied Thailand. After the war, he prepared for a career in pathology at St. Mary's Hospital in London. He later moved to the United States and became the Pathologist-in-Chief of Johns Hopkins Hospital.
Socorro Consolidated School District has approx. 2,000 students and 285 staff. Socorro has one public high school, Socorro High School.

Rachel DeLoache Williams was born and raised in Knoxville, Tennessee, the daughter of two clinical psychologists. She graduated from Kenyon College in 2010 with degrees in English and Studio Arts. Shortly after graduating, Williams moved to New York City and began working in the photo department of *Vanity Fair*, where she helped produce photo shoots for the magazine's leading photographers and iconic subjects for nearly a decade.

The Beryl oil field is located in Block 9/13a of the UK North Sea. It is named after Beryl Solomon wife of Charles Solomon the President of Mobil Europe at the time of the field’s discovery. The Beryl field was discovered in May 1972 and is a Triassic and Jurassic sandstone at an average depth of 11,000 feet (3,353 m). The reservoir and the fluids have the following characteristics:

During World War II unconventional warfare was carried out in every major combat theater by the United States. Clandestine operations, ranging from leaflet dropping to parachuting OSS agents inside enemy-controlled territory, to "Air Commando" units in the China-Burma-India Theater played an essential role in securing victory against Nazi Germany and the Japanese Empire. With the demobilization of the armed forces after the war, most of this capability was lost as the Air Force concentrated on the acquisition of jet aircraft and trying to maintain combat ready forces with austere budgets.

In the case of *Suman Panta v. Ministry of Home Affairs et. al.*, Leslie Luin Melnik, an American citizen, married Suman Panta, a Nepalese citizen, on December 18, 2015, in Sacramento County, California, United States. On December 29, 2016, Melnik applied for a Non-Tourist Visa (NT Visa) at the Department of Immigration (DOIM) under Rule 8(1)(h) of the Immigration Rules 1994, as a dependent of a Nepalese citizen. The DOIM verbally denied Melnik's application, citing that Nepali law does not recognize same-sex marriage. Subsequently, Panta filed a Writ Petition with the Supreme Court seeking an Order of Mandamus against the Ministry of Home Affairs (MOHA) and the DOIM, compelling them to issue an NT Visa to Melnik. In 2017, the Supreme Court ruled that under Rule 8(1)(h) of the Immigration Rules, a foreign national who submits a valid marriage certificate establishing marriage with a Nepali citizen is eligible to obtain an NT Visa as a dependent, regardless of gender. The court affirmed Panta's fundamental right as a member of a sexual minority community to live a life with dignity without discrimination under the Constitution of Nepal. The ruling effectively meant that foreign same-sex spouses of Nepali citizens are eligible to obtain an NT Visa.

The general election on 20 September 2014 saw the National Government returned again. National won a plurality with 47.0% of the party vote and 60 of the 121 seats. On election night counts the party appeared to hold the first majority since 1994 with 61 seats, but lost a list seat (for Maureen Pugh) to the Green Party on the official count (including special votes) of the party vote. National re-entered a confidence and supply arrangement with United Future, the ACT Party and the Māori Party.

Keith was born in Wisconsin, where his father, a British-born field biologist and Canadian Wildlife Service civil servant named Anthony Keith. attended grad school. His mother, Deborah Gorham, was a history professor at Carleton University. Keith grew up in Great Britain and in Ottawa, Ontario. His stepmother was a biologist, like his father. Keith is a citizen of Canada, the United States, and the United Kingdom.

In 2019, Maria Ceplinschi placed fourth in the all-around at the Romanian Junior Championships. At the Horizon Cup in Thessaloniki, Greece, she won gold in the all-around, uneven bars, balance beam, and floor exercise. She also placed second in the all-around at the Senior Romanian Championships.

Carnegie left Portsmouth on 24 February 1809, bound for Madras and Bengal. Baring reached Madeira on 8 March and Madras on 5 July, and arrived at Diamond Harbour on 19 July. Homeward bound, she passed Saugor on 17 October, reached Vizagapatam on 31 December, Madras on 13 January 1810, Colombo on 3 February, and St Helena on 3 May. She arrived at Long Reach on 8 July.

Parker represented Papua New Guinea in 2007 when they played matches against France and Wales in Europe. In 2008, he was part of PNG's squad for the 2008 World Cup. He scored a try for PNG while competing at the 2009 Pacific Cup. Parker played for PNG at the 2010 Four Nations. He was a member of PNG's 2013 World Cup squad and scored a try against Samoa.

Rita Moss, born Lorita Waynesboro, appears to have kept her personal life private. Her parents were Wayne Bernice Waynesboro and Minnie Peters. Lorita grew up in Cabin Creek, West Virginia, and Long Island, New York, before settling in New York. She graduated with honors from Garnet High School in Charleston in 1936, playing Mendelssohn's "War March of The Priests" on piano at the ceremony. She had two younger sisters, Elnore and Frances, who were three and seven years younger than her, respectively.

Marchetti has made a contribution to the development of the field of active matter, which is matter composed of self-propelled energy-consuming particles. Her research focuses on understanding the structure, phase transitions and rheology of suspensions and gels made of active matter systems like self-propelled particles, biological filaments and biological cells. Marchetti is particularly interested in understanding the behavior of cell colonies that grow in confined geometries such as those found in animal and human tissues. The bulk mechanical properties of these colonies can reveal how the cells in the tissues interact, a problem Marchetti studies theoretically.

Leyland cypress is light-demanding, but is tolerant of high levels of pollution and salt spray. A hardy, fast-growing natural hybrid, it thrives on a variety of soils, and sites are commonly planted in gardens to provide a quick boundary or shelter hedge, because of their rapid growth. Although widely used for screening, it has not been planted much for forestry purposes. In both forms of the hybrid, Leyland cypress combines the hardiness of the Nootka or Alaska cypress with the fast growth of the Monterey cypress.

Gospatric was the father of three sons, and several daughters. The sons Dolfin, Waltheof and Gospatric are named in De obsessione Dunelmi and by Symeon of Durham, while a document from about 1275 apparently prepared to instruct advocates in a land dispute reports that of these three sons, only Waltheof was born to a legitimate marriage and that he was full sibling of Gospatric's daughter Ethelreda. A second document with some "nearly identical" content prepared about the same time names three sisters of Waltheof of Allerdale, Gunnilda, Matilla and Ethreda, their husbands, as well as Ethreda's son William Fitz Duncan. Gospatric's children were:

Coe was made an Honorary Doctor of Technology (Hon DTech) by his alma mater, Loughborough University, in 1985. In November 2009, he was awarded an honorary degree as Doctor of Science (Hon DSc) from the University of East London. Also in 2009, he received an Honorary Fellow title from the Royal Institute of British Architects. In 2011, he received an honorary Doctorate of Letters from the University of Sunderland.

Satannish is a Hell Lord, an extra-dimensional demon of immense power, often depicted with green skin, large horns, and a facial visage on his body, a form he often takes when interacting with human sorcerers. Described as an embodiment of concentrated evil and one of the most powerful demons in existence, even considered by some to be the most powerful mystical being, Satannish possesses vast mystical powers rivaling those of other Hell Lords. These powers include teleportation through dimensions, near-limitless control over time, space, and matter, illusion casting, shapeshifting, telepathy, demonic possession, immense strength, and the ability to project devastating mystical force bolts. While his power is amplified in his native dimension, his ability to operate on the earthly plane was once limited, though this weakness may have diminished over time. Satannish demonstrated his power when he eradicated all Skrulls in Great Britain after being freed by Pete Wisdom in Avalon.

Before the development of the Perceived Stress Scale (PSS), stress assessment primarily focused on identifying specific events and experiences that caused stress, overlooking the individual's perception of those stressors and their perceived ability to cope with them. In 1983, Sheldon Cohen and colleagues developed the PSS to address this gap. The PSS is based on Lazarus and Folkman's transactional model of stress, which posits that the stress experience is modulated by appraisal and coping mechanisms. This model emphasizes that an individual's perception of their available coping resources influences their experience of a stressor.

The Lancer assault rifle's iconic design has made it a popular subject for *Gears of War* merchandise, particularly replica models. These replicas are often offered as part of special edition bundles or sold as standalone collectibles. One of the earliest examples of Lancer merchandise was a life-size replica offered by Amazon.com as part of a special *Gears of War 2* retail edition bundle, launched in November 2008. These replicas are roughly 37 inches long, 12 inches tall, and 5 inches wide, weighing approximately 15 pounds. Pulling the trigger of the replica Lancer causes the gun to vibrate and emit a chainsaw-like noise. TriForce Sales also produces 1:1 scale replicas of Lancers using high-density foam, fiberglass, and polyurethane composites.

After 1980, the northern white rhinos were wiped out in Uganda and Sudan, and 13 were left in Garamba National Park in Zaire (now Democratic Republic of the Congo). The Conservation Breeding Specialist Group of the IUCN met in 1986 in Dvůr Králové Zoo to discuss ways to preserve the dwindling number of northern white rhinos. The efforts to save the subspecies included the import of Ben (an older male from London) in 1986 and the return of Saut (a calf from the original 1975 group) from San Diego Zoo in 1998 to Dvůr Králové Zoo. Attempts to join the group with several southern rhinos met only with mixed success. Several surgeries were performed on the females and their eggs; genetic material, including Sudan's semen, were preserved.

On August 21, 2013, Carpenter signed with the Arizona Cardinals. Carpenter was signed to compete for the starting placekicker job with incumbent kicker Jay Feely. On August 25, 2013, he was cut by the Cardinals.
Medary is located in Brookings County, approximately 4.5 miles south of Brookings. Its coordinates are 44°12′35′′N 96°47′11′′W﻿ / ﻿44.20972°N 96.78639°W. The Big Sioux River is located nearby.

On October 24, 1995, an anonymous man telephoned the police claiming he could identify the killer, but the call ended prematurely. In January 1996, a recording of the call was broadcast on national television in an attempt to find the caller. The team of detectives was later reduced to four. In February, a friend of the van den Hurk family, who was under arrest for drug trafficking, claimed to have been forced to smuggle heroin by men involved in the killing; however, the police stated his testimony was flawed and provided nothing meaningful to the investigation. *Passie* magazine offered a reward for details about the killer. Funnel cakes are made by pouring batter into hot cooking oil in a circular pattern and deep frying the overlapping mass until golden-brown. The batter is commonly poured through a funnel, creating its texture and giving it its name. When made at concession stands, a pitcher with an integral funnel spout is employed. Alton Brown recommends they be made with choux pastry, which expands from steam produced by its high water content.

The 4th cadet battalion maintained a special honour guard unit that served as the official ceremonial ambassador of the military forces of the republic. It participated in the march on of the school battle banner, the welcoming of the Soviet leadership (the General Secretary, the head of state and head of government) and foreign delegations to Baku, as well public events such as wreath laying ceremonies. In the late 60s, the only ceremonial units in Baku were an army and navy guard, a motorcycle detachment, and a military band. The uniform of the unit was based on that of the 154th Preobrazhensky Independent Commandant's Regiment in Moscow. Its first commander was Major S. Akopdzhanyan. It had participated in the welcoming ceremonies of the leaders of Turkey, Iran, Senegal (Prime Minister Mamadou Dia) and Egypt at Binah Airport (now Heydar Aliyev International Airport). It also took part in the visit of Leonid Brezhnev to Baku in 1982 and ruby jubilee celebrations of Victory Day in 1985. In 1965, the unit had the honor to represent the academy by opening a military parade for the first time.

Mallaby was born to actor and acting company manager William Calthorpe Mallaby (né William Calthorpe Deeley- his father insisted on a stage name; d. 1912) and Katharine Mary Francis, daughter of George Miller, CB, Assistant Secretary of the Board of Education (son of Rev. Sir Thomas Combe Miller, 6th Baronet) on 12 December 1899. His younger brother was the public servant George Mallaby, British High Commissioner to New Zealand from 1957 to 1959.

The demon or creature known as hala (or ala), whose name derived from the Greek word for "hail" took the appearance of a dense mist or fog, or a black cloud. Hala was believed to be the cause of strong winds and whirlwind in Eastern Bulgaria, whereas the lamya was blamed as the perpetrator in Southwestern Bulgarian lore. In Western Bulgarian tradition, the halla itself was regarded as the whirlwind, which guarded clouds and contained the rain, but was also regarded as a type of dragon, alongside the folklore that the smok (roughly equated with "grass snake" but actually the Aesculapian snake) was a crag-dwelling whirlwind.

Ashill was originally called Asleigh, meaning "a clearing in the Ash wood." The parish church of St Nicholas, dating from the 14th century, stands close to the oldest part of the village. The village centers on the green and a duck pond. Drovers traveling to Swaffham market would stay overnight on the green, while their cattle grazed and drank from the pond.
