Founded in 1958 as Dance Interlude by Ruth Carse, the company was later renamed the Edmonton Ballet in 1960 and the Alberta Ballet Company in 1971. Carse directed the company until 1975. After Carse's retirement, the directorship was taken over by Jeremy Leslie-Spinks (1975-1976), followed by Brian Macdonald (1976-1988), and then Ali Pourfarrokh (1988-1998). The company moved to Calgary and merged with the Calgary City Ballet in 1990.

Ernest Thompson Seton played a significant role in the early development of Scouting. In 1906, Seton met with Lord Baden-Powell, the founder of the Scouting movement, and gave him a copy of his manual for the Woodcraft Indians, which Baden-Powell used extensively in developing his Scouting ideas. Seton's book, *The Birch Bark Roll of the Woodcraft Indians*, provided a framework that Baden-Powell found intriguing. In 1910, Seton became chairman of the Boy Scouts of America's founding committee and served as its first Chief Scout until 1915. Seton's Woodcraft Indians, along with other early Scouting attempts and Daniel Carter Beard's Sons of Daniel Boone, contributed to the formation of the BSA. Seton's work is largely the basis of the Traditional Scouting movement.

Superman Tower of Power at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas was added to the Tower section of the park in 2003. With a structural height of 325 feet (99 m), it was the tallest ride in the park until the opening of the Texas Sky Screamer. Superman Tower of Power is also the tallest ride to use both space shot and turbo drop pneumatic (air powered) sequences in the United States, as well as the second highest of its kind in the world, with the tallest being La Venganza Del Enigma at Parque Warner Madrid which is structurally at a height of 377 feet (115 m).
Ylvisaker married Lucille Torgerson in 1930. They had two children. Ylvisaker died in 1981.

Pascoe Vale South, a residential suburb 8 km north of central Melbourne, was originally part of a larger property that included Pascoeville to the north. Pascoeville was owned by John Pascoe Fawkner, and in 1840 the area was subdivided into 'gentlemen's' estates. In 1839, Dr. Farquhar McCrae, a Scottish doctor, settled in the area and named his property 'Moreland'. McCrae built Wentworth House (formerly known as La Rose) around 1842. Wentworth House is one of the oldest surviving houses in Victoria and an example of early colonial building techniques.

The North West Shelf Venture, based in the Pilbara region of Western Australia, represents Australia's largest resources development. Since the early 1980s, $25 billion has been invested in the project. Underpinned by huge gas reserves in the Carnarvon Basin, the venture has an estimated total reserve of 33 Tcf of gas.

After the war, Geulen became deeply involved with the Jewish community, maintaining contact with many of the children she had helped save. She worked to reunite hidden children with their surviving parents and secure housing and necessities for them. Geulen became actively involved with the Aid for Israelite Victims of the War (AIVG), later the Jewish Social Service, an organization dedicated to assisting Jewish survivors. She oversaw the reclaiming of hidden Jewish children. In 1948, she married Charles Herscovici, a Jewish man who had survived the war in Switzerland, while his parents, grandparents, and a brother were murdered at Auschwitz.

Production began with an initial four days of filming in 2013, but the resulting footage was deemed too short. After Fremantle took over the project, the short film was expanded into a feature film, which led to the addition of a cast of professional actors and technicians. Filming took place over a one-year period, with production occurring over 25 days throughout 2013 and 2014. The project's release date was delayed due to a change in production companies. Sponsors contributed to the production budget.

The Byzantine Empire and Ottoman Empire are different regimes to the modern nations of Greece and Turkey, but factor into the nations' modern relations as heritage. Some view the Byzantine Empire, the Roman Empire during the medieval era, the medieval expression of a Greek nation and a pre-modern nation state. Some academics claim Turkey is not a successor state but the legal continuation of the Ottoman Empire as a Republic.

The inaugural season of the Charlotte Edwards Cup (initially named the Women's Regional T20) took place in 2021, running from June to September. The eight teams were divided into two groups, with South East Stars, Northern Diamonds and Southern Vipers progressing to Finals Day at the Rose Bowl. South East Stars beat Northern Diamonds by 5 wickets in the final.

On January 28, 1905, the Japanese cabinet decided to incorporate Liancourt Rocks, referred to as Takeshima, into Japan's territory, placing it under the jurisdiction of the Administrator of Okinoshima. The Japanese government maintains that this incorporation was based on the principle of *terra nullius*, asserting that no other country had occupied the islands. The cabinet stated that Nakai Yozaburo, a Japanese fisherman, had moved to the island in 1903 and engaged in fishery there, which was cited as evidence of occupation under international law. However, records indicate Nakai's activities were based out of Ulleungdo, and he only visited Liancourt Rocks for short periods.

In January 2012, Ryan Hall ran a 2:09:30 at the Olympic marathon trials in Houston, Texas, finishing second. Meb Keflezighi finished first at the trials. Hall's finish secured him a spot on his second Olympic team for the London Games. Unfortunately, Hall was forced to drop out of the Olympic marathon around the eleven-mile mark due to a hamstring injury. After the 2012 Olympics, Hall signed up for the New York City Marathon but withdrew due to ongoing injury issues.

Pretty Dirty Secrets is an American mystery web series from the television series Pretty Little Liars. The web series takes place between the events of the series' third season episodes, "The Lady Killer" and "This Is a Dark Ride". Set in the Rosewood Halloween Spooktacular Store, as the visitors of Rosewood prepared for Halloween.

USS *Concord*'s shakedown cruise, which began on 23 November 1923 and ended on 9 April 1924, took her through the Mediterranean, the Suez Canal, and around the Cape of Good Hope. She exercised with the fleet in the Caribbean before returning to Philadelphia. The novel is well known for its style, which is variously described as modern, hard-boiled, or understated. As a novice writer and journalist in Paris, Hemingway turned to Ezra Pound—who had a reputation as "an unofficial minister of culture who acted as mid-wife for new literary talent"—to mark and blue-ink his short stories. From Pound, Hemingway learned to write in the modernist style: he used understatement, pared away sentimentalism, and presented images and scenes without explanations of meaning, most notably at the book's conclusion, in which multiple future possibilities are left for Brett and Jake. The scholar Anders Hallengren writes that because Hemingway learned from Pound to "distrust adjectives," he created a style "in accordance with the esthetics and ethics of raising the emotional temperature towards the level of universal truth by shutting the door on sentiment, on the subjective."

Water pollution is a significant challenge in El Salvador. The Acelhuate River, which crosses San Salvador, is the country's most polluted tributary, carrying industrial waste, sewage, and wastewater. Its waters contain insecticides, cyanide, heavy metals, fecal material, and toxic algae, rendering it a mix of toxic liquids and solids, not a river. This contamination flows into Lake Ilopango.

The year 1965 marked the beginning of major military activity in what became known as the secret war in Laos. Although the full extent of the conflict was not revealed to the U.S. public until 1969-70, the war was not all that secret. News of the fighting frequently found its way into the pages of the Bangkok Post, The New York Times, and other newspapers. Congress was kept well informed. As former CIA Director Richard Helms has pointed out, the Appropriations subcommittees were regularly briefed on the war funds. Also, Senator Stuart Symington and other Congressmen visited Laos and gave every indication of approving what transpired. They believed, Helms noted, that "It was a much cheaper and better way to fight a war in Southeast Asia than to commit American troops."

The mating system of the Epipedobates machalilla includes cephalic amplexus. The female will produce around 15 eggs which are left on the ground or under leaves. Historically, the Katkari were forest dwellers primarily located in the Raigad district and parts of Palghar, Ratnagiri, and Thane districts of Maharashtra, as well as some areas of Gujarat. Their name, "Katkari," derives from their traditional occupation of making and bartering catechu (kath), an extract from the Khair tree (Acacia catechu), which is used as a food additive and dye. The most common surname among the Katkari is "Waghmare," meaning "tiger slayer" in Marathi. The Katkari also had a unique relationship with forest creatures, even creating a festival called Undir Navmi dedicated to rodents, and some Katkari communities consumed rodents. The 2001 Census reported 235,022 Katkari across Maharashtra, Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Karnataka.

In February 2012, LL Social, an online marketing blog, broke the news that Pinterest was using Skimlinks to monetize content posted by its users. Commercial links included in Pins were being affiliated via Skimlinks. Although the partnership had been ongoing for 2 years, Pinterest had just recently grown exponentially in popularity and the news was badly received. Most of the backlash centered on the company's failure to disclose its monetization practices rather than its decision to monetize users' content. Though it was claimed that the revelations prompted Pinterest to drop its affiliation with Skimlinks, Pinterest's CEO, Ben Silbermann, clarified that they had actually been using Skimlinks as a test while they explored various monetization solutions and had stopped using the service a week before the news broke.

Game II of 1991 is remembered for a number of dramatic incidents. Played in drenching rain, the game exploded into violence before half-time when volatile New South Wales forward Mark Geyer chopped down on Queensland hooker Steve Walters in a tackle. Prop Andrew Gee ran in to assist his teammate, sparking a brawl which involved most players from both teams. When the dust settled, referee David Manson issued a handful of cautions, but as players left the field for half-time, Queensland captain Wally Lewis continued to goad Geyer, clearly expecting the New South Wales forward to be antagonised into a violent response right in front of Manson that would have him sent off. This became a lasting image in Australian sporting folklore.

Hulme Grammar School traces its origins back to Oldham Grammar School, which was founded in 1606 when James Asheton of Chadderton donated land for its construction, with Laurence Chaderton playing a key role in its endowment. The original school, a modest one-room building erected in 1611, closed in 1867 due to the town's industrial growth rendering its location unsuitable. In 1882, the Hulme Trust, established in 1691, was granted permission to support local educational projects, leading to the refounding of the school as Oldham Hulme Grammar Schools. During the 1920s, elements from the original Oldham Grammar School building, including the doorway with its date stone and a window, were incorporated into the present school building. Thomas C. Burton was the first headmaster of the new era of the school.

BaaS providers generate revenue from their services in various ways, often using a freemium model. Under this model, a client receives a certain number of free active users or API calls per month, and pays a fee for each user or call over this limit. Alternatively, clients can pay a set fee for a package which allows for a greater number of calls or active users per month. There are also flat fee plans that make the pricing more predictable. Some of the providers offer the unlimited API calls inside their free plan offerings. While BaaS is most commonly offered as a commercial service, open-source options are available.

Merv's economy was historically rooted in its fertile oasis, sustained by an intricate irrigation system drawing from the Murghab River. The region was renowned throughout the East, particularly for its high-quality cotton, from which superior textiles were produced. The 12th-century Arab geographer al-Idrisi noted the abundance of silk and "Merv cotton," prized for its softness, with robes and turbans made from Merv cloth being popular throughout the Islamic world. Archaeological evidence from the Sassanid period, with cultural layers reaching 17 meters thick, further supports the importance of textiles. Turkmens engaged in animal husbandry, alongside home-based production and folk crafts. Men traditionally worked in woodwork, metalwork, and leatherwork, while women focused on handicrafts requiring patience and meticulousness. During the 1990s, Italian-Turkmen teams conducted surveys, creating archaeological maps and providing valuable data on Merv's early occupation phases. Tenth-century Arab geographers noted silver mining in Central Asia, including Khorasan, while other Islamic scholars reported on steel production in the region.

The violent and impulsive behavior present in ASPD has been correlated with chronic boredom. Other studies have found that people with ASPD tend to describe emotions with ambivalence, and experience emotions such as happiness and fear less clearly than others. They may also experience negative emotions such as anger and frustration more frequently and clearly than other emotions. People with ASPD can have difficulty mentalizing, or understanding the mental state of others. They also may display a perfectly intact theory of mind, or the ability to attribute a mental state to oneself and others, but an impaired ability to understand how another individual may be affected by an aggressive action. These factors possibly contribute to their aggressive and criminal behavior as well as their empathy deficits. Despite this they may be adept at social cognition, or the ability to process and store information about other people, which can contribute to an increased ability to manipulate others.

Noni Salma is an award-winning Nigerian screenwriter, filmmaker, and transgender activist whose work often explores themes of identity, sexuality, and social justice, drawing inspiration from her experiences growing up in Lagos. It was also screened at the CineHomo Film Festival in Spain. In 2018, Salma was nominated for The Future Awards Prize for screen producer of the year and Best Screenplay at the Best of Nollywood Film Awards for her feature film *We Don't Live Here Anymore*.

Positive displacement compressors compress air by trapping a fixed volume in a chamber and reducing its size. Once the maximum pressure is reached, a valve or port opens, and the compressed air is discharged into the outlet system. Common types include piston, rotary screw, and scroll compressors.

The biggest problem with how many CLV models are actually used is that they tend to deny the very idea that marketing works (i.e., that marketing will change customer behavior). Low-value customers can be turned into high-value customers by effective marketing. Many CLV models use incorrect mathematics in that they do not take account of the value of a far greater number of middle-value customers, over-prioritizing a smaller number of high value customers. Additionally, these high-value customers may be saturated (i.e., not have the ability to buy any more coffee or insurance), may be the most expensive group to serve, and may be the most expensive group to reach by communication. The dialect of Alexandria, *iskandarani*, is closely related to Standard Egyptian Arabic but exhibits a strong influence from the Bihera dialect originally spoken in the area. While Cairene Arabic is often considered the standard, Alexandrian Arabic is also viewed positively, being considered easy, pleasant, useful, and modern. One notable difference is the term for fava-bean fritters; while Cairo uses *ta'amia*, Alexandria, like other parts of the Delta, uses *falafel*. The Egyptian pound, officially called *al-ginaih al-masri*, is colloquially referred to as "guinea" (pronounced like the country "Guinea"), a term derived from the historical use of gold coins from Guinea in trade. Older Alexandrian families, reflecting the influence of the western Delta region, may exhibit a unique feature in their speech: the use of the /a/ vowel sound in the perfect tense where other dialects use /i/, such as saying *šarab* (he drank) instead of *širib*.

The Bañados del Izozog, a 615,882-hectare wetland located in the Santa Cruz department of Bolivia, receives most of its water from the Parapetí River, which originates in the Andes. Smaller tributaries also feed the Bañados. Due to high temperatures, much of the water evaporates. In wet years, the marshes overflow and drain into the San Miguel River, which eventually reaches the Madeira River in the Amazon Basin. The Quimome River flows into the Bañados del Izozog. A large proportion of surface flow and infiltration from the Bañados del Izozog recharges the Paraguay River catchment area in the Río de la Plata Basin.

He scored seven goals in 52 appearances in the 2014–15 season, but said the team under-achieved by finishing in mid-table. He made 44 appearances in the 2015–16 season, and was an unused substitute in the play-off final victory over Plymouth Argyle at Wembley Stadium that won Wimbledon promotion into League One. He was released from the club the day after promotion was secured.

The Pakistan Air Force (PAF) has been using their F-16A fleet to attack militant positions and support the Pakistan Army's operations in North-West Pakistan against the Taliban insurgency. Since May 2009, PAF F-16s have used 500 lb and 2,000 lb unguided and laser-guided bombs to attack caves, tunnels, training camps, ammunition dumps and hide-outs used by the militants. Over 100 combat missions have been flown in South Waziristan and over 300 in the Swat Valley as of 29 July 2009. Prior to the operations in the Swat Valley, approximately 10 of the PAF's F-16s were fitted with high-resolution infra-red sensors for reconnaissance purposes, supplied by the United States, to provide the Pakistani military with detailed imagery of the area.

Following the death of Mohammed Toufic al-Assad, nicknamed "Chief of the Mountain," in March 2015, his son Hassan al-Assad took control of the criminal network and its armed contingent, the Lions of Hussein Brigade (Liwa Usud al-Hussein). Officially founded around late June-early July 2015, the Latakia-based militia, according to analyst Aymenn Jawad Al-Tamimi, may have derived its name from its leader, Hussein Tawfiq al-Assad, or from Imam Hussein. While Hussein Tawfiq al-Assad reorganized the group, a media representative asserted in 2017 that the brigade had undergone a reformation and reconstitution, now using the name "Quwat Humat Souriya-Usud al-Hussein" (Guardians of Syria Forces-Lions of Hussein), though the precise reasons for this development were not made clear. The Lions of Hussein participated in several offensives in 2015, including fighting against the Islamic State near Palmyra in the Homs desert, combat in the Sahl al-Ghab, and the offensive to take Marj al-Sultan airbase in the Damascus area. They also participated in offensives in north Latakia province.

Pratchett's Discworld novels have led to dedicated conventions, the first in Manchester in 1996, then worldwide, often with the author as guest of honour. Publication of a new novel was sometimes accompanied by an international book signing tour; queues were known to stretch outside the bookshop as the author continued to sign books well after the intended finishing time. His fans were not restricted by age or gender, and he received a large amount of fan mail from them. Pratchett enjoyed meeting fans and hearing what they think about his books, saying that since he was well paid for his novels, his fans were "everything" to him.

Pius VII's papacy, lasting from 1800 to 1823, was largely defined by his tumultuous relationship with Napoleon Bonaparte. From the outset, Pius VII recognized Napoleon as the central crisis facing the Church, and sought to protect the papacy's spiritual and temporal authority. The French leader demanded Pius VII's subservience, even in spiritual matters like the appointment of bishops, and insisted the Papal States adhere to his Continental System. This included territories that had formerly belonged to the Papal States, which Pius VII sought the return of. The Pope also wanted the release of the "Black Cardinals," including Ercole Consalvi, who were exiled and stripped of their property for refusing to attend Napoleon's wedding to Marie Louise of Austria.

The French Covered Court Championships was played at the Tennis Club de Paris the original location was Rue de Civry in the 16th arrondissement of Paris shortly before the beginning of the 1st World War it changed location to Port de Saint Cloud until shortly after the 2nd World War when it moved again to its current location at 91 Boulevard Exelmans, Neuilly-Auteuil-Passy, Paris, France. The club still exists today, it originally had four very fast Indoor (Oak Parquet Wood) courts and five Outdoor Clay courts. The tournament was one of earliest events open to international players for the indoor event the staging of the tournament tended to fluctuate between February, April and November annually the men's competition ceased in 1969 but the women's continued until 1971, it ran for 69 editions and was an early predecessor of the current Paris Masters.

The largest types of expenditures made by the government are those related to education. In 2005 alone, the government expended more than $5 billion in public education and education-related programs, representing approx. 28% of total government expenditures (excluding public corporations). Other significant expenditures include public housing and welfare ($3.4 billion or 19%), public safety ($2.5 billion or 14%) and public health ($2.3 billion or 13%).

Arthur Frederick Dudgeon served during the First World War, raising and commanding the East African Mechanical Transport Corps. He was awarded the O.B.E. in 1919 and allowed to retain the rank of major after demobilization. After the war, he was an Honorary Game Warden. After graduating from high school, he attended Ohio State University in Columbus, Ohio and then the University of Florida in Gainesville, Florida, where he played for the Ohio State Buckeyes football team and the Florida Gators football team, respectively. He played for the Gators in 1926, 1927, and 1928, and Brumbaugh, Rainey Cawthon, Clyde Crabtree and Royce Goodbread were members of the 1928 Gators' "Phantom Four" backfield that helped the team lead the country with 336 points scored. The Gators finished the 1928 season 8–1, losing only to the Tennessee Volunteers by a single point, 13–12. Brumbaugh was later inducted into the University of Florida Athletic Hall of Fame as a "Gator Great."

Constructivism, particularly as it underpins phenomenon-based learning, views learners as active knowledge builders. In this approach, information is not passively received but actively constructed through problem-solving, piecing together understanding to suit the specific situation. When phenomenon-based learning occurs in collaborative settings, it supports socio-constructivist and sociocultural learning theories, where information is seen as being formed in a social context. Central to sociocultural learning theories are cultural artifacts, such as language, mathematical systems, and thinking tools. These artifacts allow learners to build upon existing knowledge transmitted by cultures, rather than needing to independently reinvent concepts.

For centuries, the Doom Bar was regarded as a significant danger to ships—to be approached with caution to avoid running aground. When sails were the main source of power, ships coming round Stepper Point would lose the wind, causing loss of steerage, leaving them to drift away from the channel. Sometimes, gusts of wind known colloquially as "flaws" blew over Stepper Point and pushed vessels towards the sandbank. Dropping anchor would not help, as it could not gain a firm hold on the sand. Richard Hellyer, the Sub-Commissioner of Pilotage at Padstow, gave evidence in 1859 that the Doom Bar was regarded as so dangerous that in a storm, vessels would risk being wrecked on the coast rather than negotiate the channel to Padstow harbour.

According to the 2000 census, Parma had a population of 852. There were 348 households and 242 families residing in the city. The population density was 10.14 people per square mile. There were 380 housing units at an average density of 4.57 units per square mile. The racial makeup of the city was 24.8% White, 14.9% Black, 8.5% Hispanic, and 7.5% from two or more races.

The Security Branch was responsible for extrajudicial killings, especially of anti-apartheid activists (see below). The TRC concluded that its officers had carried out many political assassinations, "frequently with the authorisation or involvement of senior Security Branch personnel." The Branch carried out some very prominent assassinations, such as those of Griffiths Mxenge, Ruth First, and the Cradock Four. It used gunshots, various kinds of explosives, and, in at least one case – that of Black Consciousness activist Siphiwe Mthimkulu – thallium. In 1989, the Branch also carried out the attempted murder of Frank Chikane, at Vlok's instruction, by lacing his underwear with a nerve agent. Targets were sometimes killed in or abducted from neighbouring countries, especially Swaziland, and were often ambushed based on information obtained from the Branch's informants.

Rusty Allen Ryal attended Perry High School in Perry, Oklahoma. He played two seasons at Cowley County Community College, earning NJCAA second team All-American honors in 2003 and was a two-time All-Jayhawk selection before playing at Oklahoma State University.

The Gibraltar Peak trail, a popular walk within Tidbinbilla Nature Reserve, is approximately 8-10.5km return and takes 2-4 hours to complete. Starting at the Dalsetta carpark, the trail initially passes through open grassland before climbing to Eliza Saddle.

In 1996, midway through the inaugural MLS season, Lagos was signed by the MetroStars. He impressed right away, but his season was cut short after he tore his left-knee's ACL, MCL, and LCL in a single horrific injury just six games in. According to the 2017 Pakistan Census, the population of Mirpur District was approximately 456,000. The original inhabitants are ethnic Paharis. Historically, people from the Rajouri and Poonch districts have emigrated to Mirpur, with a large diaspora residing in the UK.

Architect Albert O. Clark came from St. Louis, Missouri to Rogers, Arkansas in 1904. He utilized the Classic Revival style when designing the Applegate Drugstore and Bank of Rogers Building elsewhere in the county. Clark was hired to build many buildings in Bentonville, including the Benton County Jail and the county courthouse. His building replaced an Italianate style structure that had served the county administration needs since 1874.: 6, 18 The very first courthouse at Benton was a log building erected in 1837.

*Can You Dig It? The Music and Politics of Black Action Films 1968-1975*, often shortened to *Can You Dig It?*, is a compilation album released by Soul Jazz Records on September 29, 2009. It was released as a double-CD collection and two separate double-LP vinyl albums. AllMusic awarded the album four out of five stars. Jim Carroll, writing for Legs McNeil, praised the album for bringing Jim Carroll to life in a way that only a friend and master stylist can make happen. Pitchfork included *Can You Dig It?* in its list of best new reissues, with Patrin describing it as definitive, treating its subject seriously, lovingly, and with an attention to detail that makes it a necessary purchase.

George Aubrey Zentmyer was born in North Platte, Nebraska, on August 9, 1913. He earned a bachelor's degree in botany from the University of California, Los Angeles. Zentmyer then pursued a master's and doctoral degree in plant pathology from the University of California, Berkeley.

Oldham Grammar School was founded in 1611 by several charitable individuals including Laurence Chadeton, but closed in 1866 and was refounded, under the Endowed Schools Act 1869 (hence the claim to be a continuation of this earlier school is debated). The doorway of the original Oldham Grammar School building with its date stone and a window were incorporated into the current school building in the 1920s. When the school was refounded in 1887 it obtained some money from a charitable trust created in 1691 by a bequest from William Hulme, after whom the new school was named. The main buildings, incorporating were erected in 1895 by the Hulme Trust. The first headmaster of the new era was Samuel Ogden Andrew, who later achieved acclaim as a translator of Homer.

Sleepy Sun is a psychedelic rock band that formed in Santa Cruz, California, in 2005, initially under a different name. The band's core members, Bret Constantino (vocals), Matt Holliman (guitar), Evan Reiss (guitar), and Brian Tice (drums), began their musical journey as students, with Constantino, Holliman, and Reiss residing on the same floor during their freshman year. Hubert Guy joined as the band's original bass player. In 2007, the band transitioned from their original garage rock sound to a more psychedelic style and officially changed their name to Sleepy Sun. This transition also marked the addition of vocalist Rachel Fannan to the lineup. In 2008, Sleepy Sun self-released their debut album, *Embrace*, through their own record label and publishing company, Sol Diamond. Following the album's release, Hubert Guy departed, and Jack Allen stepped in as the new bass player. ATP Recordings re-issued *Embrace* in May 2009. Sleepy Sun's growing acclaim for their live performances led to an opportunity to support Arctic Monkeys during their 2010 United States tour.

In 2017–18 season with the San Diego State Aztecs men's basketball team, Schakel participated in 33 games averaging 14 minutes per game. The freshman scored 106 points, with 12 steals, 19 assists, 27 three-pointers, and only 9 turnovers in 455 minutes of playing time for the season. After winning the Mountain West Conference tournament, his team qualified as 11 seed at the 2018 NCAA tournament but lost in the first round to 21-seed Houston Cougars 67–65.

Only 77 original S.E.5 aircraft had been completed prior to production settling upon an improved model, designated as the S.E.5a. The initial models of the S.E.5a differed from late production examples of the S.E.5 only in the type of engine installed – a geared 200 hp Hispano-Suiza 8b, often turning a large clockwise-rotation four-bladed propeller, replacing the 150 hp H.S. 8A model. In total 5,265 S.E.5s were constructed by six manufacturers: Austin Motors (1,650), Air Navigation and Engineering Company (560), Curtiss (1), Martinsyde (258), the Royal Aircraft Factory (200), Vickers (2,164) and Wolseley Motors Limited (431).
The Kansas City Royals claimed Romero off waivers on July 5, 2018. On July 21, 2018, he was designated for assignment, and on July 26, he elected free agency.

In 1992, Daedra Charles was invited to compete in the Summer Olympics held in Barcelona, Spain. The United States women's basketball team, favored to win gold, started strong with three victories before facing the Unified Team in the semifinals. In a major upset, the Unified Team defeated the U.S. 79-73, ending America's 15-game Olympic winning streak. The dispirited U.S. team then faced Cuba in the bronze medal game, where they were tied 44-44 at halftime. The USA ultimately secured an 88-74 victory to win the bronze medal. Daedra Charles averaged 3.8 points per game in five games.

As of the census of 2000, there were 488 people, 209 households, and 127 families residing in the village. The population density was 757.2 people per square mile (294.4/km2). The racial makeup of the village was 95.90% White, 2.46% Native American, 0.20% Asian, and 1.43% from two or more races.

El Rodeo School used to serve students in kindergarten through eighth grade. Beginning in 2019, El Rodeo serves students only in kindergarten through fifth grade. El Rodeo School was established in 1927 and enrolls 743 students on its campus at 605 Whittier Drive. The school is temporarily closed for reconstruction. Catherine Eddowes was buried on the afternoon of Monday, 8 October 1888. Her coffin was of polished elm, and bore a plate inscribed with the words "Catherine Eddowes, died Sept. 30, 1888, aged 43 years."

Fyter Fest is an All Elite Wrestling (AEW) event that was initially part of the Community Effort Orlando (CEO) video game fighting festival. The professional wrestling matches at Fyter Fest involved storylines between wrestlers, who portrayed heroes, villains, or less distinguishable characters in scripted events that built tension and culminated in a wrestling match. These storylines were produced on AEW platforms.

The All-China Federation of Trade Unions (ACFTU) was established in 1925 to represent the interests of national and local trade unions and trade union councils. The ACFTU reported a membership of 130 million, out of an estimated 248 million urban workers, at the end of 2002. Chinese trade unions are organized on a broad industrial basis. Membership is open to those who rely on wages for the whole or a large part of their income, a qualification that excludes most agricultural workers. In theory, membership is not compulsory, but in view of the unions' role in the distribution of social benefits, the economic pressure to join is great. The lowest unit is the enterprise union committee. Individual trade unions also operate at the provincial level, and there are trade union councils that coordinate all union activities within a particular area and operate at county, municipal, and provincial levels. At the top of the movement is the ACFTU, which discharges its functions through a number of regional federations.

Lisbon's first pillory likely dates to shortly after 1147, following the city's conquest by Portuguese soldiers and Crusader Knights and the granting of a charter. The earliest records placing the pillory were later located in Municipal Square. The Great Lisbon Earthquake of 1755 destroyed all traces of this original monument, along with much of the city.

In the late 19th and early 20th centuries, Switzerland became a popular destination for winter vacations, attracting visitors from all over Europe. Winter sports like skiing, sledding, and bandy were enjoyed in Geneva and other towns. Students from Oxford and Cambridge Universities traveled to Switzerland to compete against each other in bandy. The first of these matches, a predecessor to the Ice Hockey Varsity Match, was played in St. Moritz in 1885. The European Championship was held there in 1913, possibly due to the popularity of Swiss winter sport venues.

Correlated color temperature (CCT) simplifies the description of the perceived "warmness" or "coolness" of a white light source. Measured in Kelvin (K), CCT approximates the color temperature of a black body radiator whose color most closely resembles the light source. Higher CCT values indicate a cooler, bluer light, while lower values indicate a warmer, more reddish light. For example, daylight is around 5600K and appears blue, while tungsten light is around 3200K and appears golden. Although the Kelvin scale measures the light source's color, light sources with the same CCT may still appear different due to variations in their spectral power distribution. Fluorescent lights, while inexpensive, often differ from ideal black body radiators. Some fluorescent lamps are designed with high faithfulness to an ideal light.

Epipedobates machalilla has a snout–vent length of 14.4 to 16 millimetres (0.57 to 0.63 in) for males and 15.0 to 17.6 millimetres (0.59 to 0.69 in) for females. Their heads are longer than they are wide, and the tympanum is small. The forelimbs have a moderate length and the fingers are unwebbed. The toes do not have lateral fringes and the terminal discs are expanded. It is dark-coffee in colour with a gold iris and cream ventral surfaces.

Ed Markey has been married to Rear Admiral Susan Blumenthal, M.D., M.P.A. since 1988. Markey was a contributing writer for *The Huffington Post* from 2005 to 2017. He had a cameo role as an Irish Brigade officer in the 2003 film *Gods and Generals*.

ShawnDre' Jones was born in Houston, Texas. Before high school, his family relocated to Richmond, Texas. He attended Fort Bend Bush High School. Jones committed to play basketball at the University of Richmond.

Asia's third album was initially titled *Arcadia*, but the name was changed when it was discovered that Duran Duran's spin-off project was also using it. The album was eventually released in November 1985 as *Astra*. *Astra*'s commercial performance was significantly weaker than the band's first two albums, leading the record label to cancel the planned tour due to lack of interest. Despite this, the single "Go," featuring Mandy Meyer's guitar work prominently, reached No. 46 on the Billboard Hot 100. In 1986, this Asia lineup disbanded, effectively bringing the group to a temporary halt. John Wetton noted that *Astra* performed well in Sweden, but that Swedish sales alone were not substantial enough to make the album a success.

Jean Bédard is a laundry employee and union advisor. He is a perennial candidate for the Marxist-Leninist Party, having run under its banner in seven federal and six provincial elections. He appears to have been a low-profile candidate; during the 2003 election, the newspaper Le Soleil indicated that it was unable to obtain an interview with him.

Melanie Susan Oxley is the daughter of Eric and Jan Oxley. Eric, an art teacher, was originally from Manchester and had migrated to Sydney before the Oxley family settled in the rural northern beach community of Kingscliff in 1965. Oxley's older brothers are Peter and Jeremy Oxley (born ca. 1961), who founded Australian pop-rock band The Sunnyboys, and her younger brothers are Damien and Tim (singer, guitarist, drummer in The Humdingers, The Verys, The Dearhunters, and Roger Loves Betty).

In 1996, midway through the inaugural MLS season, Manny Lagos joined the MetroStars after being acquired from the Minnesota Thunder. He made an immediate impact, but his season was cut short after just six games when he suffered a severe knee injury, tearing his ACL, MCL, and LCL in his left knee. Lagos returned the following season, playing in 15 league matches, though still affected by the injury. Frustrated with his injuries, the MetroStars exposed Lagos in the 1997 MLS Expansion Draft, where he was selected by the Chicago Fire. He subsequently joined the Fire and won his first MLS Cup with the team in 1998.

Georgy Morozov was drafted into the Red Army in 1941 and joined the Communist Party of the Soviet Union in 1943. He fought in the Battle of Belgorod in spring 1943 and participated in Operation Polkovodets Rumyantsev in July 1943.

Some of the graves have filled with gravel or stones from processes of erosion. The graves were dug through the upper silt layer. Mud was placed on the walls of the grave to prevent falling sand. After the person was placed in the grave, they filled the grave with silt or small pebbles. The people buried in the graves were usually placed on their left side. Direction of the body was aligned with the cardinal directions. It appears that when new graves were dug, they cut into older graves. The graves often contained pottery, tools, bone spatulas, mammal bone perforators. Bodies were adorned with ivory bracelets, stone and ivory bangles, stone necklaces, lip plugs, and stone pendants. Graves also contained pebbles, beads, and marine shells. Children were buried with furniture or distinctive signs of family. These children seem to have had the same treatment as adults. This is a sign that status is attributed at birth.

Terry Pratchett cultivated a large and dedicated fanbase, primarily through his *Discworld* series, which led to dedicated conventions. The first Discworld Convention was held in 1996. Pratchett himself often served as the guest of honour at these events. The publication of a new book was often accompanied by signing tours, with queues known to span multiple floors of venues due to Pratchett's dedication to each fan. His readership was diverse, encompassing all ages and genders. Pratchett received a large amount of mail from his fans and acknowledged their importance to him, stating, "My fans are everything to me."

For the 2009–10 NBL season, Damian Martin joined the Perth Wildcats, following his coach from the Sydney Spirit, Rob Beveridge. In his first season with the Wildcats, Martin helped the team win the NBL championship. His strong performance led to a call-up to the Australian national team (Boomers) following the season. In 2011, Martin won his first NBL Best Defensive Player award and was named in the All-NBL First Team. He then won the Best Defensive Player award for the next three consecutive seasons. During the 2013 semi-final series, Martin suffered a partial tear of his Achilles tendon. In January 2013, Martin was named co-captain of the Wildcats alongside Shawn Redhage. He was later named the sole captain of the team for the 2013/14 NBL season.

Obama's presidency saw the continuation of battles between both parties over the confirmation of judicial nominees. Democrats continually accused Republicans of stalling nominees throughout Obama's tenure. After several nomination battles, Senate Democrats in 2013 reformed the use of the filibuster so that it could no longer be used on executive or judicial nominations (excluding the Supreme Court). Republicans took over the Senate after the 2014 elections, giving them the power to block any judicial nominee, and the 114th Congress confirmed just 20 judicial nominees, the lowest number of confirmations since the 82nd Congress. Obama's judicial nominees were significantly more diverse than those of previous administrations, with more appointments going to women and minorities.

Multi-story buildings occupied by rental apartments, known as a *rab'* (plural *riba'* or *urbu*), became common in the Mamluk period and continued as a feature of Cairo's housing during the later Ottoman period. This type of housing was relatively unique to Cairo at the time. These apartments were often laid out as multi-story duplexes or triplexes. Sometimes, these apartment complexes were attached to caravanserais (*wikalas*), where the two lower floors were used for commercial and storage purposes, while the upper floors were rented out to tenants. Even in these *wikala-rab'* combinations, the apartment complexes had their own street entrances, separate from the commercial complex below. The oldest partially-preserved example of this type of structure is the Wikala of Amir Qawsun, built before 1341. Residential buildings in Cairo were organized into close-knit neighborhoods called a *harat*, which often had gates that could be closed off at night or during disturbances.

Archaeologist Denise Carpinteyro Espinosa, from National Institute of anthropology and history, Center INAH, reports that petroglyphs (engraved stone) in the site, some are related to astronomy while others are more closely related to everyday life of these hunter-gatherers human groups who inhabited this site 7 thousand years ago. The site is crossed by a river and it is inferred that this site was of great importance for diverse human occupations during over 7,000 years. Boca de Potrerillos, we very ancient settlements. The level of engravings offers a significant knowledge level of the skies and studies that have been made on the handling time for hunting which was related to the stars or for many everyday things of life.

Born in Workington, Cumbria, Dobie joined Carlisle United as an apprentice in June 1995, turning professional on 10 May 1997. While at Carlisle, it was Dobie's goalbound header that was parried into the path of Carlisle goalkeeper Jimmy Glass, who scored with the last kick of the final game of the 1998–99 season, preserving Carlisle's Football League status and relegating Scarborough. During 2000–01, Dobie attracted interest from other clubs, with Carlisle manager Ian Atkins claiming that the player could be worth as much as £2 million in the transfer market.

During his time with the Los Angeles Kings, Daniel Berthiaume served as a backup to Kelly Hrudey. Berthiaume won 20 games for the Kings during the 1990-91 season. In the 1991–92 season, before being traded to the Boston Bruins on January 18, 1992, Berthiaume appeared in 19 games and had a 4.04 goals against average, managing only one win.

From its commissioning in 1926 until 2007, the westernmost point of Pennsylvania Route 830 was at an intersection with PA 255 in DuBois. The route ran through the townships of Sandy, Union, and Huston. Before 2007, PA 830 began at an intersection with US 219 in DuBois and intersected with I-80 at Exit 86. After the interchange, PA 830 traveled east and made a right turn at US 322 near Emerickville, passing the old entrance to the DuBois Regional Airport. PA 830 then made a sharp right curve where the present routing continues today. The state-maintained route between US 322 and Red Barn Road is designated as SR 2010, a Quadrant Route. The section of Red Barn Road between SR 2010 and the modern-day Pennsylvania Route 830 is designated as SR 3015. The old Pennsylvania Route 830 between the PA 830 / SR 2010 intersection and Allens Mills Road is now a dirt road.

The LGBTQ community has been under scrutiny and controversy in the public eye of collegiate athletics, but the NCAA moves to support the inclusion of these groups. The NCAA provides many resources concerning the education of the college community on this topic and policies in order to foster diversity. Title IX protects the transgender community within intercollegiate athletics and on college campuses.

"Wild Horses" is a classic Jagger/Richards composition, the writing of which Keith Richards described as a "classic way" he and Mick Jagger worked together. Richards provided the riff and chorus, while Jagger focused on the verses, similar to their process for "Satisfaction." According to Richards, the song, like "Satisfaction," reflects the feeling of "not wanting to be on the road, being a million miles from where you want to be." In the liner notes of the 1993 Rolling Stones compilation album *Jump Back*, Jagger recalls originally working on the song with Gram Parsons, whose version he believes was released slightly before the Stones'. Jagger also notes that while many assume the song was written about Marianne Faithfull, he doesn't believe that to be the case, though he admits to being "very inside this piece emotionally."
