Probate records show her leaving her effects of £1,181 to Jolly.

He was elected to the House of Commons at the 1895 general election as the Liberal Unionist Member of Parliament (MP) for the Biggleswade division of Bedfordshire. Compton was re-elected unopposed in 1900, but at the 1906 general election he was defeated by the Liberal Party candidate Arthur Black. He was returned to the Commons at the January 1910 election as Unionist MP for Brentford, and held the seat until his resignation on 15 March 1911 by the procedural device of appointment as Steward of the Chiltern Hundreds.

The book collection at the William L. Clements Library originated with William L. Clements' personal Americana collection, which began in 1903. The library's holdings have since expanded to include materials covering American history from 1492 through 1900, with particular strengths in 18th and 19th-century American history. The collection also covers topics related to Native American history, including material culture, Indian Wars, and treaties. The Clements Library also holds materials that support a diverse array of research projects. Several collectors have shaped the collection, including James S. Schoff, who donated his Civil War collection in 1974.

Hector Boece (sometimes written as Boethius or Boyce) was born in Dundee in 1465. He received his initial education in Dundee before attending the University of Paris. There, he studied at the Collège de Montaigu and became close friends with Erasmus of Rotterdam. By 1497, Boece had become a professor of philosophy at the university, after serving as secretary to the Master of the College, Jan Standonck.

Dering wrote three books of motets with continuo, two of canzonets and one of continuo madrigals, and is represented in many MSS and anthologies. His music shows varying degrees of Italian influence; his innovative use of a basso continuo part in his madrigals is considered to be an early example of the nascent Baroque style in English music. The continuo madrigals and small concertato motets are very much in the idiom of Alessandro Grandi or d'India, with wayward modulations and dramatic expression; the Cantica Sacra (1618) contains 6-part motets that recall a more conventionally expressive Italian madrigal-like idiom.

Hatcham Social formed in New Cross in 2006 when David Fineberg met brothers Toby and Finnigan Kidd. Drummer Finnigan Kidd was still a member of Klaxons, but left in order to devote his time to Hatcham Social; they soon released their self-recorded debut 7-inch single "Dance as If..." on the indie label WaKs Records (Daniel Devine). After showing considerable interest and forming a friendship with the band, Faris Badwan of The Horrors (a former art student at Central Saint Martins) agreed to create the artwork for the band's second single, "How Soon Was Then?", released on PopGrooves (the label set up by David Fineberg); the artwork to the single was hand-printed on 200 limited edition 7" sleeves. The single gave the band their first national radio airplay on the BBC 6Music show Mint, hosted by Marc Riley, in January 2007.

Hrvoje Horvat Jr., born in Bjelovar on December 16, 1977, inherited a love for handball from his father, Hrvoje Horvat "Cveba" Sr. His first coach at ORK Partizan was Krunoslav "Kuna" Turkovic. After ORK Partizan, Horvat Jr. had short stints playing in other countries. He also played for Croatian national teams under the Croatian Handball Federation.

During World War II, the Soviet government dramatically altered Almaty's population and infrastructure, evacuating 26,000 people from the European theatre of war to better organize the home front and concentrate industrial resources. Almaty hosted over 30 industrial facilities, eight hospitals, 15 institutes, universities, and technical schools, and around 20 cultural institutions removed from the European USSR. Motion picture production companies from Leningrad, Kyiv, and Moscow were also relocated to Almaty. This influx of primarily ethnic Russians significantly impacted the region's demographics, making Kazakhs a minority.

Joseph Bentham was born in either 1593 or 1594, based on his age at death in 1671. He graduated with a Bachelor of Arts degree from Pembroke College, Cambridge, in 1615, and proceeded to earn his Master of Arts degree in 1618. He was ordained a priest in the Church of England at Peterborough on September 21, 1617.

Storm Management was founded in London in 1987 by Sarah Doukas. Doukas is credited with discovering models such as Kate Moss and Cara Delevingne, and actress Emma Watson. Simon Chambers runs Storm alongside his sister, Sarah Doukas. In 2005, Doukas sold a controlling stake in Storm to 19 Entertainment.

*21 Grams* holds a 74% approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes, based on reviews. The site's consensus praises Alejandro González Iñárritu's deft handling of the film's unconventional narrative, describing it as a stylish and haunting drama with fine performances. Metacritic assigned the film a weighted average score, indicating generally favorable reviews, based on critics' reviews.

During the Yugoslav Wars—in the Srebrenica massacre—about 7,500 people were killed by Bosnian Serb troops in a span of eleven days. It was later found to be an act of genocide by the International Court of Justice. The UK sponsored a resolution which would pronounce the Serbian people genocidal and commemorate the 20th anniversary of the massacre. The Russian Federation, after Serbian President Tomislav Nikolić sent a letter to Russian President Vladimir Putin, was the only country on the security council that was against the resolution (China and three other countries abstained): Churkin issued a veto on his country's behalf at the UN Security Council on 8 July 2015.

The squadron was first activated at March Field, California in January 1941 as the 2d Reconnaissance Squadron, and was attached to the 30th Bombardment Group. Four months later it moved to New Orleans Airport, Louisiana, where it trained with the Douglas B-18 Bolo. Following the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, the squadron returned to California where it participated in antisubmarine patrols, re-equipping with Consolidated B-24 Liberators for operations. It was redesignated the 392d Bombardment Squadron in April 1942 and assigned directly to the 30th Bombardment Group. In June 1942, the unit added the mission of training aircrews in the B-24 and in the LB-30 Lend Lease version of the Liberator.

Admiralty station, located on Hong Kong Island in the Admiralty district, is surrounded by office buildings and shopping centres. Pacific Place is a major shopping centre accessible from the station. Exit F is a 3 minute walk away from Pacific Place.

Game II of the 1991 series is remembered for the heated confrontation between Queensland captain Wally Lewis and NSW firebrand Mark Geyer. Played in driving rain at the Sydney Football Stadium, tensions escalated before half-time when Geyer's crude tackle on Queensland hooker Steve Walters drew the ire of several Queenslanders. Andrew Gee ran in to assist Walters, further inflaming the situation. Referee David Manson tried to diffuse the animosity with cautions, but the altercation between Lewis and Geyer continued as the players left the field for the break. Manson later stated that the Maroons targeted Geyer before and after the game.

The third Asia album was tentatively titled Arcadia, but during production it was discovered that that name was being used by a forthcoming spin-off project from Duran Duran. The retitled Astra, released in November 1985, was not as commercially successful as the first two albums. The record label cancelled the projected tour because of lack of interest. The band charted another single with "Go" (No. 46), featuring Meyer's guitar work as a primary element. The music video was another hit with MTV but in 1986 this Asia lineup folded, bringing the group to an end for the time being. Wetton is quoted as saying "[Astra] did really well in Sweden ... but Swedish sales aren't that large."

The Critérium du Dauphiné was created in 1947 by the French newspaper *Le Dauphiné Libéré* to boost its circulation after World War II. The newspaper was based in the Dauphiné region of southeastern France. The race was designed to precede the Tour de France on the cycling calendar, acting as a preparatory event. The inaugural edition of the race was won by Edouard Klabinski.

The population of Kotputli was 52.7% male and 47.3% female as of the 2011 census. When asked about religious preference, 97.6% of the region identified as Hindu and 2.0% as Muslim, with Christians, Sikhs, and Jains making up the remaining population. Kotputli has an average literacy rate of 60.6%, slightly higher than the national average of 59.5%; male literacy is 71.5%, and female literacy is 48.6%. 88.1% of the population lives in the city, while 11.9% live in rural areas. 15.5% of the population is under six years of age.

Child composes music for orchestra, chorus, computer synthesis, voice, and chamber groups. His compositional style has been compared to Charles Ives, Benjamin Britten, and Gustav Mahler. Among his works are Embers (1984), a one-act chamber opera based on the play by Samuel Beckett, and Clare Cycle (1984), four settings from the poetry of John Clare.

In North America, Unleashed was released by Rogue Pictures (which was a division of Focus Features). In its opening weekend in North America, the film grossed $10,900,901 which placed it third. It showed on 1,957 theaters for an average $5,570 per screen. The film grossed $24.5 million in North America and a further $26.3 million worldwide for a total of 50.9 million. This box office result surpassed Rogue's expectations of $18 million gross at the North America box office.
Mackay's major work was The Practice of the Court of Session (2 vols. 1877–9), which became a standard authority. Other works were:

At the very beginning of World War II, all Polish Jews of Wyszków, including Lajchers' family, were expelled by the Nazis in one massive action of 4 September 1939. The older 77 Jews, along with 8 Poles who were helping them, were locked in a barn and burned alive. Later that month, another 65 Jews were shot; afterward the town was declared Judenfrei. The Lajchers relocated to Węgrów, which was already swelling with hundreds of expellees. In the summer of 1940, Lajcher joined the local Jewish council and organized a hospital. In February 1941 the ghetto was closed off from the outside and hunger set in amongst its inmates. Lajcher wrote letters to the American Jewish Joint Distribution Committee, but in vain.

There is one exclave, the tiny village of Barak (population 627), in the Fergana Valley. The village is surrounded by Uzbek territory. It is located on the road from Osh (Kyrgyzstan) to Khodjaabad (Uzbekistan) about 4 kilometres (2 miles) north-west from the Kyrgyz–Uzbek border in the direction of Andijan. Barak is administratively part of Kara-Suu District in Kyrgyzstan's Osh Region.

Although Nate Schierholtz was expected to be the Giants' Opening Day right fielder in 2010, Bowker won the position after hitting six home runs in spring training. On April 7, Bowker hit a two-run home run against Brett Myers in a 10–4 victory over the Houston Astros. After batting .214 with four RBI in his first 10 games, Bowker was replaced by Schierholtz as the starting right fielder on April 17. On May 7, Bowker hit a ninth-inning game-tying home run against Francisco Rodríguez in an eventual 6–4 loss to the New York Mets. After batting .207 with 17 hits, 3 doubles, 3 home runs, and 8 RBI in 41 games, Bowker was optioned to Fresno on June 2 to make room for Pat Burrell on the roster. He was named the PCL Player of the Week from June 14 to June 21 after he had a 13-game hitting streak and a 6-game RBI streak end that week. After batting .310 with 61 hits, 12 doubles, 14 home runs, and 36 RBI in 51 games with Fresno, Bowker and Joe Martinez were traded to the Pittsburgh Pirates on July 31 for Javier López.

*Sans Soleil* is an experimental essay film incorporating documentary, travelogue, and fictional elements. Chris Marker shot the original footage on a Beaulieu 16mm camera, recording non-synchronous sound on a cassette player. The film also includes stock footage, some of which was processed using a Spectron video synthesizer. Marker borrowed a number of sequences, and specifically credits volcanologist Haroun Tazieff in the narration for footage of erupting volcanoes.

A committee of concerned Eastvale residents, the Eastvale Community Committee (ECC), was formed so that issues facing the growing community would be addressed. Formed in 2002, the ECC holds public meetings with county representatives, utility operators, law enforcement agencies, local school representatives, and local business owners. A group of volunteer residents publishes a summary of the meetings and other articles in the quarterly Eastvale Edition which is mailed to most residents and is also available online.

For Marvel's limited series *Angela: Asgard's Assassin* #1-6 (2014), Stephanie Hans shared art duties with Phil Jimenez. The series was written by Kieron Gillen and Marguerite Bennett. In his 2014 review for ComicBook.com, Russ Burlingame noted that Hans was billed as doing backup stories in the book. Jesse Schedeen of IGN highlighted the division of labor in the first issue, noting that Jimenez handled the art for the main story, while Hans tackled the interlude segments. Schedeen particularly praised Hans' "ethereal watercolor approach" that complemented the storybook quality of her segments, contrasting it with Jimenez's work, which he felt didn't fully capture the otherworldly vibe. Petra Halbur, in her review for The Mary Sue, described both Jimenez and Hans' artwork as "gorgeous," praising their character designs for striking a balance between cartoon and realism and their effective use of varied angles and panel arrangements.

Labour remained in control of the council with 23 councillors after holding 7 of the 11 seats contested, despite losing 1 seat to the Conservatives. The Conservative gain came in Furnace Green, where Brenda Blackwell defeated the former Labour mayor Ray Calcott by 1,269 votes to 895. This took to the Conservatives to 7 seats, as they also held the 2 seats contested in Pound Hill North, while the Liberal Democrats remained on 2 seats after holding Northgate.

Fabiano is gay and publicly announced his engagement in August 2017. On October 28, 2018, he married marketing consultant Bryan McCalister at the Metropolitan Opera in New York City. Fabiano enjoys flying small planes.

In 2009, D-Black began his career as an independent artist in the group D-Black & Kwaku T releasing a group album ‘Target Practice’. In 2010 D-Black and his rap partner both went solo. He went on to release three albums (Music, Love and Life, The Revelation, Lightwork’ 1 E.P (Smoke and Mirrors) and 1 mix-tape (Hunger & Thirst) over the years. He has been nominated for numerous awards and won several, including the 2011 BET Awards.

Today, original chromolithographs are relatively rare due to their fragility and the deterioration of the paper and inks over time. Initially, chromolithography offered a more affordable alternative to hand-painted artwork, though the process of preparing multiple stones for each color was laborious and costly. Despite efforts to streamline production, the emergence of more efficient photomechanical processes and offset lithography in the late 19th and early 20th centuries ultimately replaced chromolithography, making it less competitive.

Sherwood Schwarz, born in 1916, was the creator of iconic television shows such as "Gilligan's Island" and "The Brady Bunch". After initially planning to pursue a medical degree, Schwarz served in the U.S. Army for over four years. Following his military service, he returned to writing, having started professionally in radio on the Bob Hope Show in 1939. He later founded Sherwood Schwartz Productions in California. Carnegie Associates Ltd was part of Dale Carnegie & Associates.

Townsend made his senior Tottenham debut on January 9, 2011, in a 3–0 FA Cup win against Charlton Athletic. He marked the occasion by scoring the opening goal. After previous loan spells at Ipswich Town and Watford were cut short, Townsend joined Millwall on loan until the end of the season on January 20, 2011. His loan to Watford was terminated three months earlier than planned, after making just three appearances for the Hornets. On March 7, 2011, Townsend joined Millwall on loan. During his time at Millwall, he made 13 appearances and scored 3 goals. In April, he expressed his desire to stay at Millwall for the following season.

Theodore began working professionally at the age of twelve. She appeared Off-Broadway as Eustacia Vye in the 1977 Hudson Guild Theater production of Dance On A Country Grave. Her success on Broadway led to concerts at major performance venues such as Carnegie Hall.

Orlando Apopka Airport covers an area of 80 acres (32 ha) at an elevation of 143 feet (44 m) above mean sea level. It has one asphalt paved runway designated 15/33 which measures 3,987 by 60 feet (991 x 18 m). Runway 15 has a displaced threshold of 943 feet, leaving an available landing distance for that runway of 3,044 feet.

Can You Dig It? was released by Soul Jazz Records on September 29, 2009. It was released on compact disc, and two volumes of vinyl records, and digitally in mp3 format. Jim Carroll of The Irish Times awarded the compilation four stars out of five, praising the release's music as well as its content, noting that "along with a 100-page book detailing Blaxploitation’s peaks and troughs [...] [the album] oozes funk from every crevice and pore." Patrin gave the album a high ranking in Pitchfork, including it in their list of "Best New Reissues", calling it an "excellent compilation" noting that "the music survives as strong as ever, renewed through the lens of the hip-hop generation that grew up with and reinterpreted it."

On March 23, 2019, the Islamic State (IS), also known as Daesh, lost its final significant territory in Syria following the battle of Baghuz. The US-backed, Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) defeated the insurgents, effectively ending the group's five-year "caliphate." The SDF and its allies described this as a total elimination of the Islamic State in Syria. British Prime Minister Theresa May praised the "immense courage" of the UK military and its allies for the liberation of the last Daesh-held territory. She affirmed the UK's commitment to protecting its people and allies from the threat posed by Daesh. Despite the territorial defeat of IS, Major General Ghika stated that Operation Shader would continue, highlighting the ongoing need to counter ISIL and build the capacity of Iraqi security forces.

A significant problem with many Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) models is their misuse, particularly the over-reliance on aggregate metrics that mask inaccuracies at the individual level. While effective marketing can potentially convert low-value customers into high-value ones, many CLV models mathematically overestimate the value of low-value customers while underestimating high-value ones, often prioritizing current customers. High-value customers, unlike the average customer who may view a business as just one of many options, demonstrate brand loyalty and are less likely to churn. Furthermore, high-value customers often require less service and communication, and their advocacy drives down acquisition costs. Gathering feedback from potential customers through surveys is a viable way to understand their needs and gauge demand for products, ensuring future customer value.

Individuals with antisocial personality disorder (ASPD) often exhibit a diminished capacity for experiencing a full range of emotions. While they may struggle to clearly perceive emotions like shame or guilt, they can experience negative emotions such as irritability or aggression more frequently. Chronic boredom has also been correlated with problematic behaviors. Although individuals with ASPD may appear to understand the emotions of others, they often lack genuine empathy and may struggle to comprehend the impact of their aggressive actions on others. Despite these impairments, they can be adept at recognizing and exploiting others' weaknesses, using this knowledge to manipulate situations and people for their own benefit. This ability contributes to their tendency to be deceitful and manipulative in relationships.

Pierre Collingwood Donat, known professionally as Peter Donat, was born on January 20, 1928, in Kentville, Nova Scotia. He was the son of Philip Ernst Donat, and his younger brother is actor Richard Donat. His uncle was British film actor Robert Donat, known for winning an Oscar in 1939 for *Goodbye, Mr. Chips*. Peter Donat emigrated to the United States in 1950 and studied drama at Yale University. He first gained recognition as a stage actor in *Cyrano de Bergerac*. In 1961, he played a leading role in Donald Jack's *The Canvas Barricade*, the first Canadian play performed at the Stratford Festival.

Moreno redshirted in 2006, and entered the 2007 season as a redshirt freshman under head coach Mark Richt. After a great spring, Moreno shared time with Thomas Brown as the starting running back in the season opener against Oklahoma State. Moreno totaled 70 yards rushing on 12 carries with two receptions for 51 yards in the 35–14 win. In the following game against South Carolina, Moreno saw most of the rushing duties with 14 carries for 104 yards in the 12–16 loss. In the third game of the season against Western Carolina, Moreno scored his first career college touchdown in a 45–16 win over Western Carolina. His totals on the day were 94 yards on 13 rushes with one touchdown.

Prior to the release of *channel ORANGE*, Frank Ocean embarked on a solo tour through North America and Europe to promote his self-released mixtape, *Nostalgia, Ultra*. "Thinkin Bout You" was performed at all of Ocean's shows during the tour. The song was included in his setlist at the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival in April 2012. Complex magazine commented on Ocean's performance of "Thinkin Bout You", noting that his falsetto made women in the audience swoon. Ocean later performed the song live at the 2012 MTV Video Music Awards.

By 1943 a new All-American Girls Softball League was formed, playing a hybrid form of softball and baseball which never really became baseball until overhand pitching began in 1948. Women's softball was enormously popular in the early 1940s, and often drew good crowds. In June 1943, Time magazine estimated there were 40,000 women's softball teams in the U.S., including popular touring clubs such as Barney Ross' Adorables and Slapsie Maxie's Curvaceous Cuties. The new league was conceived by chewing gum magnate and Chicago Cubs owner Philip K. Wrigley, who decided in 1942 to start a women's professional softball league, concerned that the 1943 major-league season might be canceled because of World War II. The circuit would eventually shift gears and become the All-American Girls Professional Baseball League, and was dissolved at the end of the 1954 season. About 500 women attended the initial call. Of these, 280 were invited to the final try-outs at Wrigley Field in Chicago, where 60 of them were chosen to become the first women to ever play professional baseball. The 60 players were placed on the rosters of four fifteen-player teams: the Rockford Peaches, the South Bend Blue Sox, the Racine Belles and the Kenosha Comets.

On 9 January 2011, Townsend made his senior Tottenham debut in a third round FA Cup tie at White Hart Lane against Charlton Athletic; he scored the first goal and was voted Man of the Match. This proved to be his only appearance for Tottenham for the rest of the season, with two further loan spells occurring. He joined Championship club Watford on 20 January 2011 on loan until the end of the season, but this was terminated early, on 23 February, with Townsend having made three appearances. On 7 March, Townsend again went on loan to a Championship club until the end of the season, this time Millwall. By April he said he would be happy to stay at the club beyond his loan spell. In 11 appearances he scored twice.

Egypt sent five table tennis players to the 2016 Summer Paralympics in Rio de Janeiro, including Ibrahim Al-Husseini Hamadtou, aged 41, the only player to compete while holding the paddle in his mouth. Umpires from Egypt were also selected to officiate the table tennis events.

A significant conservation effort involving North Seymour Island occurred on January 4, 2019, when the Directorate of Galapagos National Park (DPNG) and Island Conservation reintroduced 1,436 Galápagos land iguanas (Conolophus subcristatus) to Santiago Island. These iguanas had been absent from Santiago for 180 years, since Abel du Petit-Thouars's 1838 sighting, after Charles Darwin's 1835 sighting. The reintroduction aimed to restore Santiago's ecological health and provide the iguana species an opportunity to thrive. The iguanas were sourced from North Seymour Island, where they had been introduced in the 1930s and had since flourished.

Unlike its predecessor, it received mostly negative reviews. Rotten Tomatoes gives it a score of 13% based on 16 reviews, with an average rating of 3.5/10. Audiences polled by CinemaScore gave the film an average grade of "B" on an A+ to F scale. The film was released in the United Kingdom on March 22, 1991, and reached number 2 in the country's box office that weekend.

As of the census of 2010, there were 767 people, 280 households, and 213 families living in the village. The population density was 723.6 inhabitants per square mile (279.4/km2). There were 303 housing units at an average density of 285.8 per square mile (110.3/km2). The racial makeup of the village was 98.3% White, 0.5% African American, 0.1% Asian, 0.3% from other races, and 0.8% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 2.0% of the population.

Born in Swansea, Jones began his career at his home town team Swansea Town, signing professional with the club in September 1959. A skilful winger, he soon established himself in the squad, winning the Welsh Cup in 1961, and was transferred to Plymouth Argyle in 1964 for £45,000; which was a club record fee for Plymouth and a British record fee for a winger at the time. He spent three years at the club, reaching the Football League Cup semi-finals during the 1964–65 season, before returning to South Wales in March 1967, joining Cardiff City for £25,000.

Argonaut Insurance was founded in California in 1948 as Argonaut Insurance Exchange, specializing in workers' compensation for the California construction industry. Teledyne acquired Argonaut in 1969.

In the mid-1960s, Jackson Browne began pitching his songs to artists and publishing houses. In January 1967, after signing with Elektra Records' publishing division, Nina Music, Browne recorded demos of his material at Jaycina Studios in New York City. These recordings were compiled by Nina Music into a limited-edition, two-LP publishing demo, of which only 100 copies were pressed. The purpose of this demo was to showcase Browne's songwriting talent to other artists. Among the songs included was an early, more upbeat version of "These Days," titled "I've Been Out Walking." Browne wrote "These Days" around the age of 16.

Jean Bédard is a union representative and perennial candidate for the Marxist-Leninist Party. He has run in at least one federal election. He has also run in at least one provincial election. *Le Soleil* reported being unable to obtain an interview with Bédard during the 2003 election.
Sergio Llull joined Real Madrid in 2007, becoming central to their success. In 2010, Real Madrid extended his contract through the 2013/14 season.

Dennis Harvey, writing for *Variety*, noted that while the film is well-produced, it inevitably remains dialogue-driven. He felt that director Leon and adapter Paris Qualles successfully opened up the play to avoid stage-to-screen claustrophobia. However, Harvey considered Mervyn Warren's score a bit more earnest and old-fashioned than ideal for this refreshed take on the dramatic golden oldie.

The idea behind Il Divo came when Simon Cowell, inspired after listening to the Three Tenors, decided to form a multinational quartet of talented young singers to recreate the quality of Luciano Pavarotti, José Carreras, and Placido Domingo. Cowell searched worldwide for young singers who were willing to embark on the project from January 21, 2001, to December 16, 2003, when Miller was signed. Prior to Il Divo, each member enjoyed moderate success individually.

For a number of years, Edgar M. Gregory lived in Deposit, New York, where he and his partners established the Deposit Bridge Company in 1836. He later moved to Cincinnati, Ohio, where he was involved in mercantile and manufacturing industries. While in Cincinnati, Gregory aided fugitive slaves in their flight to Canada. By 1842, he was treasurer on the executive committee of the American Home Missionary Society. In 1846, he served as the corresponding secretary of the Cincinnati Young Men's Auxiliary of the American Bible Society. Gregory then moved to Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, where he became a preacher and established a mission.

According to the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops (USCCB), the bill "would force the Church to alter its structure in violation of its own religious principles." One of the sponsors of the Bill, Andrew J. McDonald, stated that the effort was an "attempt to create a forum for a group of concerned Catholic constituents to discuss their legislative proposals regarding parish corporate finances." Bishop William E. Lori, at that time Bishop of Bridgeport, described it as "unconstitutional," as a violation of the First Amendment to the United States Constitution. Connecticut Attorney General Richard Blumenthal opened an investigation to "determine its intent and its possible violation of the constitution."

Gedi appears to be a corruption of Ghritaghadya winch is found in two inscriptions : one near Rav, dated 1271 (Samvat 1323), where it is mentioned as a large district under Maldev, a Viceroy of Arjundev king of Anhilwad Patan; and the other in the inscription on the Malan well). This name would seem to mean the land, ‹See RfD› ghadya or ‹See RfD› padya, of butter, ‹See RfD› ghrit or ‹See RfD› ghee. The legend is that a merchant of Anhilwad in want of clarified butter, ‹See RfD› ghee stole it by magic from Malav's warehouse, and afterwards offered the value. This Malav refused as the stolen butter had been replaced by means of Malav's magic ring. On being assured that butter had been filched, he took the money, and with it built the temple, the well, and the pond.

The Tenpō calendar is a Japanese lunisolar calendar adopted in 1844, at the end of the Tenpō era. It divides solar terms using the *Teiki-hō* (定気法) method. This replaced the older *Heiki-hō* (平気法) method. In the Tenpō calendar, the lunar month starts on the day of the new moon. A leap month is added when it is necessary to ensure that the solstices and equinoxes fall within specific months. The specific lunar month designated as the leap month varies. The Winter Solstice must always be in the eleventh month, the Spring Equinox in the second month, the Summer Solstice in the fifth month, and the Autumn Equinox in the eighth month. The time of day in Japan is used to determine the dates of solar terms and lunar phases.

The cause of the 1944 explosion in Aarhus was never definitively determined. German authorities proclaimed the explosion was communist sabotage, a claim widely contested, attributing the cause to self-combustion or mishandling of ammunition due to the high work tempo demanded by the Germans. The investigation was never resumed. A memorial service was held in Aarhus Cathedral for the 12 unidentified victims. A monument was erected in Vestre Cemetery for Latvians who died in exile in Denmark after the Second World War. The city council donated 1000 kr. to each of the victim's families.

Beverly "Bonnie" Foit-Albert was a Buffalo native. She earned a Bachelor of Architecture from Cornell University in 1961 and a Master of Architecture from the University at Buffalo (UB) in 1975. Three years later, she received a Ph.D. in Human Sciences from California's Saybrook Institute through a UB program.

Emperor Menelik II's declining health, beginning around 1906, led to increasing rivalries within the government. Empress Taytu asserted her authority by undermining the crown council of ministers. The Shewan faction, which was running the daily governance, grew frustrated with Empress Taytu's decisions, particularly after she maneuvered her own candidates into positions of power. Welde Giyorgis, who commanded an army of thirty thousand men, did not commit himself to the Empress or the Shewan establishment.

As of 2008 the oldest ethnic Korean church is the Korean Christian Church of Houston. In September 2007 Chul Chung, the senior pastor, returned to South Korea after he resigned. In 2008 there was discord among factions in the church and around 100 people were expelled from the church. The members who were suspended or expelled instead attended services at the Korean Senior Center.

In 1973 and 1974, the 28th Surgical Hospital (Mobile) (Army) (MASH) helped phase-in new designs for operating rooms and patient facilities, replacing the previous canvas tents. Subsequently, all other configurations of army deployable hospitals were inactivated or reconfigured to the CSH configuration. The 212th Mobile Army Surgical Hospital was the last to convert.

In 1816, the citizens of San Cristóbal de Alvarado sought the designation of Town from King Ferdinand VII of Spain, who granted the title of Villa via Royal Decree on September 8, 1816. This title cost 92,800 maravedíes. Two years later, on September 5, 1818, Ferdinand VII authorized the establishment of the first Honorable Ayuntamiento (city council), presided over by Tomás del Hondal.

On February 14, 2020, Bailey signed a one-year, $7 million contract with the Minnesota Twins. He made his debut with the Twins on July 24, 2020. He did not earn any victories with the team. On August 2, 2020, the Twins designated Bailey for assignment. He was released on August 7, 2020.

Scientists have studied the properties of cirrus using several different methods. Lidar (laser-based radar) gives highly accurate information on the cloud's altitude, length, and width. Balloon-carried hygrometers measure the humidity of the cirrus cloud but are not accurate enough to measure the depth of the cloud. Radar units give information on the altitudes and thicknesses of cirrus clouds. Another data source is satellite measurements from the Stratospheric Aerosol and Gas Experiment program. These satellites measure where infrared radiation is absorbed in the atmosphere, and if it is absorbed at cirrus altitudes, then it is assumed that there are cirrus clouds in that location. NASA's Moderate-Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer gives information on the cirrus cloud cover by measuring reflected infrared radiation of various specific frequencies during the day. During the night, it determines cirrus cover by detecting the Earth's infrared emissions. The cloud reflects this radiation back to the ground, thus enabling satellites to see the "shadow" it casts into space. Visual observations from aircraft or the ground provide additional information about cirrus clouds. Particle Analysis by Laser Mass Spectrometry (PALMS) is used to identify the type of nucleation seeds that spawned the ice crystals in a cirrus cloud.

The Cizeta-Moroder name comes from the Italian pronunciation of designer Claudio Zampolli's initials (CZ) (Ci-Zeta). Zampolli worked as a test and development engineer at Lamborghini before starting his own business of selling and maintaining high-performance sports cars. The V16T was conceived out of his desire to have his initials on a sports car. He made a partnership venture with his long time customer Giorgo Moroder, an Oscar winning music composer, who regularly came to his shop to have his Lamborghini Countach serviced after learning that the two shared similar interests in automobiles. Moroder was a 50% stakeholder in the new joint venture. Zampolli selected a team of former Lamborghini employees to develop the car which included Oliviero Pedrazzi as the chief engineer and engine designer and Achille Bevini along with Ianose Bronzatti as in-charge of the suspension and the chassis. Giancarlo Guerra, a former craftsman of Scaglietti body works who was infamous for coach-making the body of the Ferrari 250 GTO along with devising economical ways to make the chassis of the Lamborghini Countach when he worked at Lamborghini, was tasked to build the body of the car for the initial production run.

In 2014, Mohammad Bazar CD block had 1 rural hospital, 4 primary health centres, and 1 medical facility run by another department/PSU of the state government. These facilities contained a total of 64 beds and 7 doctors, excluding private bodies. There were 25 family welfare subcentres. In that year, 1,652 patients were treated indoors and 89,826 outdoors.

From the 2000 census, 219,800 residents of Coffrane were Roman Catholic. 34.6% of the population belonged to the Swiss Reformed Church. There were 11,523 members of an Orthodox church. 5.40% of the population belonged to another Christian church. 985 individuals identified as Islamic. 1,212 residents belonged to no church, were agnostic, or atheist, representing 3.74% of the population. 1,109 individuals did not answer the question about religion, accounting for 3.42% of the population.

Wildberg is a spa town ("Luftkurort") and Schaferlaufstadt located in the state of Baden-Württemberg. The township sits on the eastern edge of the Calw district, bordering the Böblingen district to the east. Wildberg's municipal area lies in the transition zone between the Northern Black Forest and the Black Forest foothills. The central and western parts of Wildberg, Effringen, and Schönbronn, including the Nagold Valley, belong to the Enz-Nagold Plateau. West of Wildberg lies Lower Muschelkalk. The elevation within the municipal area ranges from 355.59 m to 637.54 m above sea level.

Wilhelm Exner, born in 1840, demonstrated an early aptitude for technology, nurtured at the Imperial and Royal Polytechnic Institute in Vienna, which he entered in 1857 after skipping a year of Realschule. In 1862, he attended the World Exposition in London, sent by the Lower Austrian Trade Association, beginning his long engagement with international technical innovation. He was appointed Professor of Engineering in 1869.

David Zurawik of The Baltimore Sun said the "most impressive accomplishment" of the film is the complete impartiality in the way Shepard portrays the Boy Scouts as an institution. Zurawik went on to note that even though Shepard reveals the inconsistency and sanctimoniousness between what the scouts claim are their stated values and its anti-gay policy, the film "still celebrates the idea of scouting and the incredible role it plays in [boys and young mens lives] in the United States". Film Threat praised Shepard for his portrayal of "Steven Cozza as just a normal kid who had the good fortune to be raised by a couple of great parents...it's unfathomable that a young man could take on such a sensitive cause, in a truly insensitive age bracket...and be accepted for it". Donald Liebenson of the Chicago Tribune remarked that the film is not an all out tirade against the Boy Scouts, but instead, it explores "the irony that the values Cozza took from scouting are what inspired him to take his stand".

Both Sawamura's own family and that of his wife were active in the film industry. Sawamura's younger brother and sister were the actors Daisuke Katō and Sadako Sawamura. He married Tomoko Makino, the daughter of Shozo Makino, a film director and the head of Makino Productions. His brothers in law were thus the film directors Sadatsugu Matsuda (1906–2003), Masahiro Makino (1908–1993), and Shinzō Makino, as well as the producer Mitsuo Makino. Masahiro married the actress Yukiko Todoroki and their son—and thus Sawamura's nephew—Masayuki Makino, is the head of the Okinawa Actor's School. Sawamura fathered the actors Masahiko Tsugawa and Hiroyuki Nagato, both of whom married famous actresses, Yukiji Asaoka and Yōko Minamida respectively.

Belmont, traditionally known as the "Little Italy" of the Bronx, is a diverse neighborhood with a significant Hispanic or Latino population, comprising 56.5% as of the 2010 census. While no ethnic group forms a majority, Mexican and Albanian families have moved to Belmont in recent decades. Other represented groups include White, Black, American Indian, and Asian residents. Belmont's diversity reflects its history of evolving ethnic communities. The Belmont Stakes is not held in Belmont, Bronx.

Thomas was born in Pennsylvania on October 6, 1958 and graduated from the United States Military Academy in 1980. Thomas was a member of the 75th Ranger Regiment. He led a Ranger Rifle platoon from A Company, 2nd Ranger Battalion during the Invasion of Grenada in 1983, that was dropped from an MC-130 onto a landing strip in Grenada. After completion of Infantry Officer Advanced Course in early 1986, he was assigned as Assistant S-3, Plans/Liaison Officer with 75th Ranger Regiment at Fort Benning, Georgia until 1987. Thomas was then assigned as a company commander with 3rd Ranger Battalion. In 1989, during the Invasion of Panama, he led his Ranger Rifle Company in another combat jump.

Negotiations that led to the signing of the treaty began in May 1982. In November 1983, the Soviet Union "discontinued" communication with the US, which had deployed intermediate-range missiles in Europe. In January 1985, US Secretary of State George Shultz and Soviet Foreign Minister Andrey Gromyko negotiated a three-part plan including strategic weapons, intermediate missiles, and missile defense. It received a lot of attention at the Reykjavik Summit between Ronald Reagan and Mikhail Gorbachev and ultimately led to the signing of the Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty in December 1987. Talk of a comprehensive strategic arms reduction continued and the START Treaty was officially signed by US President George H. W. Bush and Soviet General Secretary Gorbachev on 31 July 1991.

Franklin's main interest was the relationship between a patient's environment and mental illness. She developed planned environmental therapy to treat this relationship. She theorised that focusing on the well-functioning parts of a patient’s personality could help them to behave better in a social environment. Planned environmental therapy is seen as a forerunner of the therapeutic community movement.

Smith College has 285 professors across 41 academic departments and programs, maintaining a student-faculty ratio of 9:1. Smith began an engineering program and graduated the first all-women class of engineers from a U.S. college. The Picker Engineering Program offers the S.B. degree.

On June 27, 2011, head coach Bret Bielema announced Russell Wilson's commitment to Wisconsin as a graduate student. In his debut for the Badgers against UNLV, Wilson passed for 255 yards and two touchdowns, adding a 46-yard rushing touchdown. He finished the season with the Griese-Brees Quarterback of the Year award for his performance in the Big Ten.

"Market Forces" originally broadcast on March 22, 2008, on the Kids WB! block for the CW Network, at 10:00 a.m. It received generally positive reviews from television critics. Eric Goldman of IGN gave the episode an 8.5 out of 10 and wrote "Sometimes change can be fun, and this episode was a great example of that." Goldman enjoyed the design of Shocker which he felt stayed true to his original design and was "intrigued" by changing his secret identity. He praised the usage of CGI, the portrayal of Peter's life, and the "cool" ending.

Local lore associated the wedge tomb with a sea-green cow Glas Gaibhnenn, abducted from Spain by the smith Gavida. From this magical cow's udders used to flow all the rivers on the mountain-side (the "source of the seven streams" lies to the southwest), was tricked by a hag, who, in place of a milk-pail, milked her into a sieve. The cow either died of grief or deserted that locality for ever. Another version has Lon Mac Liomtha as her owner. The herd was supposedly housed at the wedge tomb of Slievenaglasha.

The National Center for Foreign Commerce (CENCOEX) made U.S. dollars available to importers at several rates. The most favorable was the official CENCOEX rate, initially set at 6.30 bolivars to one U.S. dollar, primarily benefiting importers of food and medicine. A dual exchange rate system, SICAD I, offered a less favorable rate of approximately 12 bolivars per dollar for less essential goods. A third rate, SICAD II, existed at around 50 bolivars per dollar. However, these auctions were sporadic and ceased in August 2015 due to the economic crisis. A parallel, or black market, exchange rate also existed, reaching nearly 900 bolivars to the U.S. dollar in late 2015. Publication of the parallel exchange rate within Venezuela was prohibited starting in 2008, though it was tracked and published outside of the country.

The giant thicket rat is losing habitat due to the clearance of forest for timber, firewood and conversion to agricultural land. There is almost no data on how common the species is and, although there is no data on trends, it is thought that the population is decreasing. Much of the giant thicket rat's range lies within Mount Kenya National Park but it is a priority to establish other protected areas for this species. The giant thicket rat is classified as endangered because its range is less than 2,000 km2 (770 sq mi) and is in a single locality where the habitat is being degraded. As this species is so little known the International Union for Conservation of Nature and Natural Resources recommends that it needs further study to evaluate its status and ensure its conservation.

The S.E.5a was an improved version of the original S.E.5, of which only 77 were completed before production shifted to the more advanced variant. The initial models of the S.E.5a differed from late production examples of the S.E.5 primarily in the engine installed: a geared 200 hp Hispano-Suiza 8b, often turning a large clockwise-rotation four-bladed propeller, replacing the 150 hp H.S. 8A model. In total, 5,265 S.E.5 aircraft were constructed. Austin Motors produced the largest number with 1,650, while the Royal Aircraft Factory produced 200.

Eastvale experiences a warm Mediterranean climate (Köppen climate classification Csa), characterized by mild winters and hot summers. Most of the rainfall occurs during winter and early spring. Winter low temperatures can get cold enough for frost, with days typically pleasant around 65°F, occasionally warming into the 70s. Summertime is hot, with highs averaging in the low 90s, and daytime temperatures often exceeding 100°F during the hottest months.

Prior to FIFA's planned Women's Club World Cup, other international club competitions existed. Less than a month before Japan hosted the FIFA Club World Cup, the country staged the International Women's Club Championship, the first annual international competition contested by women's champion clubs. Organized in Japan, the inaugural edition in November 2012 featured four teams: Lyon from Europe, Canberra United from Australia, and INAC Kobe Leonessa and NTV Beleza from Japan.

Traditionally, the Dongzhi Festival is a time for families to get together. One activity that occurs during these get-togethers (especially in the southern parts of China and in Chinese communities overseas) is the making and eating of tangyuan (湯圓) or balls of glutinous rice, which symbolize reunion. Tangyuan are made of glutinous rice flour and are sometimes coloured pink or green. Each family member receives at least one large tangyuan in addition to several small ones. The flour balls are cooked in a sweet soup or savory broth with both the ball and the soup/broth served in one bowl. It is also often served with jiuniang, a mildly alcoholic, unfiltered rice wine containing whole grains of glutinous rice (and often also sweet osmanthus flowers),.

On June 10, 2012, the Local Coordination Committees reported 53 deaths, including 26 in Homs. The Syrian Observatory for Human Rights reported at least 12 people killed in Homs province. The number of soldiers killed on this day is not mentioned in the provided context.

The National Roller Hockey League (NRHL) began its inaugural season in 2015 with four teams: the Alkali Revive, Alkali Surge, Canfield Alkali Chiefs, and Detroit Bordercats, each playing a 12-game regular season. The Canfield Alkali Chiefs had an undefeated regular season. In the playoffs, the Detroit Bordercats needed three games to eliminate the Alkali Revive and then swept the Alkali Surge in a best-of-three series to win the Commissioners Cup Finals. The playoffs MVP was not mentioned in the provided context.

*First Australians* is a documentary series produced by Rachel Perkins and Darren Dale of Blackfella Films. Perkins, an Arrernte and Kalkadoon woman and daughter of Aboriginal activist Charles Perkins, also directed the first episode, which she co-wrote with Louis Nowra. The series was broadcast on SBS Television.

The calendar is a lunisolar calendar which adopted Teiki-hō, where solar terms are equally divided by solar longitude, in place of Heiki-hō, in which they are equally divided by time. The lunar month starts on a day with a new moon. A leap month is added when there are three lunar months between a lunar month which includes a solstice/equinox and the following lunar month which includes a solstice/equinox. In that case the leap month is the lunar month which does not include any chūki 中気 (one of the twelve solar terms that are used to determine the months of the year). The months which include a solstice/equinox are fixed as the second, fifth, eighth and eleventh months respectively. The time of the day used in the calendar to determine the dates of solar terms and lunar phases is that observed at Kyoto.

According to the 2000 census, 25,992 residents of Aire-la-Ville were Roman Catholic, while 7.0% of the population belonged to the Swiss Reformed Church. The census recorded 464 members of an Orthodox church. Individuals belonging to another Christian church numbered 497, representing 1.53% of the population. There were 5 Jewish individuals and 985 Islamic individuals. The census also counted 41 Buddhists. Those who belonged to no church, were agnostic or atheist, comprised 3.74% of the population.

Curaçao and Sint Maarten became two new "landen" (literally: countries) within the Kingdom of the Netherlands, along the lines of Aruba and the Netherlands. Their planned new joint currency is the Caribbean guilder, which was expected to be brought in by 2012 but has been delayed. Aruba's right to secede from the Kingdom was not extended to Curaçao and Sint Maarten.

Butt's sophomore season in 2014 was impacted by a torn ACL he suffered in February of that year (Context 7), causing him to miss the first two games. He made his season debut in Week 3 against Miami University (Context 4), catching three passes for a season-high 59 receiving yards, including a 29-yard touchdown pass from Devin Gardner, in a 34-10 victory (Context 4, 5). On November 29, 2014, Butt caught a season-high four passes for 35 yards and a touchdown during Michigan's loss to #7 Ohio State (Context 4). He appeared in nine games, starting five, and recorded 21 receptions for 211 receiving yards and two touchdowns (Context 3). After Michigan finished the season with a 5-7 record, head coach Brady Hoke was relieved of his duties (Context 3).

Hviid was born 1 September 1916, in Moscow. His father worked as a wholesale merchant. Hviid's father moved the family business from Moscow due to the Russian Revolution, and fled to Denmark. The family moved again to Riga in 1921, but later returned to Denmark in 1934, due to the changing political situation in Latvia.

The rodded medium (plasma medium) is also known as the wire mesh, and wire grid. It is a square lattice of thin parallel wires The initial research pertaining to this medium was conducted by J. Brown, K.E. Golden, and W. Rotman.

Captain Charles Carnegie sailed *Baring* from Portsmouth on 24 September 1809, bound for Madras and Bengal. *Baring* reached Madeira on 12 October. She arrived at Diamond Harbour on 1 March 1810. Homeward bound, *Baring* passed Saugor on 20 April and arrived at Long Reach on 19 September.

Charles was named to the national team invited to compete at the 1992 Olympics, held in Barcelona, Spain. The USA team won their first three games, but then played the Unified Team and fell, 79–73. The USA team then faced Cuba for the bronze medal. The game was tied at halftime, and Cuba had a small lead midway through the second half, but the USA went on a run to retake the lead, and finished with an 88–74 victory and the bronze medal. Charles averaged 6.2 points per game.

Development of the AGS-40 "Balkan" began in the mid-1980s, initiated by a military order for a new automatic grenade launcher with improved range. The project stalled, but was resurrected in the early 1990s under the codename "Balkan." Development proceeded slowly for nearly two decades. State tests were underway by February 2013, with completion planned by the end of that year. The first serial batch of the AGS-40 was delivered in December 2023. The AGS-40 can be installed on various vehicles and integrated with the Arbalet-DM remote weapon station.

Pereira began the 2014–15 season with Manchester United, winning silverware in the Manchester Senior Cup final on 7 August 2014, where United defeated Manchester City 4–1. He made his senior debut on 26 August 2014, as a half-time substitute for Saidy Janko in a 4–0 away defeat to Milton Keynes Dons in the League Cup. On 15 March 2015, Pereira made his Premier League debut against Tottenham Hotspur, replacing Juan Mata in the 77th minute of a 3–0 win for United.

Founded in 1879 by Algernon Sydney Sullivan and William Nelson Cromwell, Sullivan & Cromwell advised John Pierpont Morgan during the creation of Edison General Electric (1882) and later guided key players in the formation of U.S. Steel (1901). Cromwell developed the concept of a holding company, persuading New Jersey to include it in state law and enabling companies incorporating there to avoid antitrust laws. The firm also worked with less-successful businesses during the volatile decades before the establishment of modern federal bankruptcy laws; it pioneered efforts to reorganize insolvent companies through what became known as the "Cromwell plan." Cromwell was called "the physician of Wall Street" for his ability to rescue failing companies.

In the United States, the FAA regulations on drones only pertain to the physical safety of airspace and everything under it. Privacy regulation is not within the realm of the FAA; that is for state and local governments to decide. Drone regulations vary by country, by state, by region, and, in some cases, further, by city. In addition, many preserves, and all national parks, such as the Golden Gate National Recreation Area in California, do not allow drone use. Drone operators can download an app developed by the FAA called B4UFLY, which compiles the local laws and do not fly zones and makes them easily accessible, so an operator may clearly know where it is legal to operate their drone.

The Ural Mountains extend about 2,500 km (1,600 mi) from the Kara Sea to the Kazakh Steppe along the border of Kazakhstan. Vaygach Island and the island of Novaya Zemlya form a further continuation of the chain on the north. Geographically this range marks the northern part of the border between Europe and Asia. Its highest peak is Mount Narodnaya, approximately 1,895 m (6,217 ft) in elevation. Transverse faults divide the mountain chain into seven major units, each of which has its own typical pattern of mountain ridges. From north to south, these are the Pay-Khoy, Zapolyarnyy, Pripolyarnyy, Polyarnyy, Severnyy, Sredniy, Yuzhnny Ural and Mugodzhary. The average altitudes of the Urals are around 1000–1300 meters, the highest point being Narodnaya peak in the Pripolyarnyy Ural which reaches a height of 1894 meters.

Set in November 1830, 'Captain Swing' depicts the plight of impoverished farm labourers in a Sussex village, inspired by the mythical figure of Captain Swing. The play centres on Matthew Hardeness, who is committed to non-violent action but struggles to control the increasingly volatile mob protesting against their desperate working conditions. The working class are in a state of poverty. Matthew Dinely faces a dilemma as he attempts to maintain order while the legendary Captain Swing, whose name is used on threatening letters to landowners, incites more radical action. The identity of Captain Swing is unknown, but the character represents an adversary to Matthew Dinely.

LTC William S. Key, newly discharged from the US Army following World War I was appointed Captain of Field Artillery in the Oklahoma Army National Guard. He was directed to organize a light artillery battery at Wewoka, Oklahoma. A 75mm battery was federally recognized as Battery A, 1st Oklahoma Field Artillery on July 28, 1920. Battery "A" became known as the "White Horse Battery" in recognition of the white horses used to tow the artillery pieces. On 18 July 1921, the 1st Oklahoma Field Artillery Regiment, consisting of a Regimental Headquarters and two firing battalions, incorporating the White Horse Battery and other subsequently established artillery units, was federally recognized.

Neel Kamal (1947) was a moderate success, according to some sources. The Hindustan Times reported that the film "did not achieve much success". The film's exact earnings are not specified in the provided context.

Owari province was one of the foremost production centres of enamel in the country. During the Edo period the Andō family operated a pipe shop called "Murata-ya". Andō Jubei (Jusaburo) (1876-1953) was born in Nagoya as the fourth child; he had three elder sisters. His mother died in May 1877 following an illness, and his father followed in September 1877. Orphaned at less than one year old, he was raised according to his father’s will by staff employers. His older sister married Andō Juzaemon, whose born name was Matsukichi. Together with his brother-in-law they made the cloisonné company a success. In 1893, Andō Juzaemon went to the World's Columbian Exposition in Chicago. It was his first time to travel overseas, and he used the opportunity to study the market. In 1901 Andō Jubei went to the Glasgow International Exhibition, which was his first overseas travel, and he stayed for two years in a British home to study the market.

Following several collaborative screenwriting projects, Lambert and Varkle set out to produce a feature film. Beginning in 2016, they outlined several projects that would support a close character study of an isolated individual. Inspired by headlines of mass shooters brainwashed by online hate groups, they opted to tell an "angry young man" story. The filmmakers researched online communities of self-described "red pillers" and "incels" in order to make their character realistic and relatable.

In 1974 Pavšič considered a theory according to which nature is exactly symmetric with respect to space inversion, provided that one postulates the existence of mirror particles and mirror interactions among them. The idea of mirror particles was introduced in 1956 by Lee and Yang in their paper on parity non conservation, and was in 1966 further elaborated by Yu. Kobzarev, L.B. Okun and I.Ya. Pomeranchuk within the context of a CP invariant theory. Nowadays, the so-called exact parity models are considered in many works as an explanation of dark matter.

The year 2011 marked a significant period for Sanjeev Rajput, highlighted by his gold medal win at the ISSF World Cup in Changwon, South Korea. This victory, where he broke his own record for the third time, secured him a qualification for the 2012 London Olympics. In the same year, he competed in the men's 50-metre rifle three positions at the Royal Artillery Barracks during the 2012 Summer Olympics. Later, Rajput finished 8th in his pet event at the ISSF World Cup finals in Poland. He also won 5 gold and 1 silver medal at the 34th National Games in Ranchi, Bihar.

In 1911, Vanderbilt was the champion of the South. Michigan was the only team to score against them, defeating them 3-0. The coach of that Michigan team is not mentioned in the provided context. Edwin Pope described the Vanderbilt backfield as "lightning-swift," and it included Lewie Hardage, Wilson Ingram, Ray Morrison, and captain Bob McRory. A Nashville newspaper voted it the best in the South.

Mutukula is located in extreme southern Kyotera District at the international border between Uganda and Tanzania. The town is approximately 46 kilometres (29 mi), south of Kyotera, where the district headquarters are located. This is about 91 kilometres (57 mi), by road, south-west of Masaka, the nearest large city. This location is approximately 224 kilometres (139 mi), by road, south-west of Kampala, the capital and largest city of Uganda. The coordinates of the town are 1°00'00.0"S, 31°25'00.0"E Latitude:-1.0000; Longitude:31.4167).

During World War II the Clarksdale Victory was a cargo supply ship. She took supplies to support the troops at the Battle of Okinawa. She was at Okinawa from April 26 to May 8, 1945. On April 27, 1945 a shell landed only 15 yards from the Clarksdale Victory. The shell exploded and fragments landed on her deck. One of Clarksdale Victory's lifeboats was damaged by the fragments. On the same day near by, the SS Canada Victory was hit by a kamikaze plane; the explosion in a cargo hold blew out the side of the ship, and she sank in seven minutes.

In October 2017, Banijay Group (the current parent company of Yellow Bird as a result of acquiring the company's former parent Zodiak Media in February 2016) launched a British counterpart to Yellow Bird named Yellow Bird UK. Yellow Bird UK will focus on developing and creating original ideas with international appeal and a Nordic noir look and feel for British audiences, working with all broadcasters and platforms. Based in central London alongside fellow sister companies BlackLight and Fearless Minds, it will have close ties to the Swedish Yellow Bird.

RADA is based in the Bloomsbury area of Central London. The main RADA building where classes and rehearsals take place is on Gower Street (with a second entrance on Malet Street), with a second premise nearby in Chenies Street where RADA Studios is located. The Goodge Street and Euston Square underground stations are both within walking distance.

Planners knew the original Tampa Museum of Art building, which opened in 1979, was too small. For 30 years, the museum's demolition was a controversial topic. Several plans for expansion or relocation were proposed by the museum and the city.

Krensky was born in Chicago, Illinois in 1951 and attended New Trier High School (East). He received a B.A. in biology, Summa Cum Laude, and M.D. from the University of Pennsylvania. He was a Resident (medicine) and Fellow in Pediatrics at Children’s Hospital Boston and a research fellow at Dana Farber Cancer Institute, Boston. After one year on the faculty at Harvard and Boston Children’s Hospital, he moved to Stanford in 1984. He was on the faculty at Stanford for 23 years and developed the Children’s Health Initiative, a $500 million investment in research, education and clinical care, at Stanford and Lucile Packard Children's Hospital.

Graham continued to maintain his innocence and attempted to secure an appeal that would grant him a release from prison. The South Dakota Supreme Court heard oral arguments regarding his 2010 conviction on 19 March 2012 in Vermillion, South Dakota. Graham's attorney, John Murphy, argued that the government should not have had the authority to transfer his case from federal to state jurisdiction following his extradition to the US, the South Dakota Supreme Court ruled that the state was within its rights to prosecute Graham, there was sufficient evidence to convict Graham, and his life-sentence imprisonment without parole was commensurate with the crime committed. The South Dakota Supreme Court thus dismissed the John Graham Appeal. Graham is currently incarcerated at the South Dakota State Penitentiary in Sioux Falls, South Dakota.

De Grave is best known for his topographical drawings of the Southern Netherlands, including Maastricht and some in France during the years 1667-1668, among which drawings of the military camps of Stadholder William III during the campaigns against the French in 1674-1676. He later refocussed on imaginary and Italianate landscapes, often with a strong accent on perspective. He became a member of the Haarlem Guild of St. Luke in 1659. His relationship to Timotheus de Graaf (1647-1724), a teacher of Jacob Appel has not been established. His topographical work is often confused with that of Valentijn Klotz (and his brother(?) Barnardus Klotz), with whom he made several travels together. He drew mostly in black or brown pen with grey wash. A few paintings of his, mostly with imaginary landscapes, are also known.

Cortese was born in Milan to a family from Stresa, Piedmont. In 1940, she met conductor Victor de Sabata, who was 31 years older and married with children. She left high school to follow him to Rome, and there she graduated from the Academy of Dramatic Art. They separated in 1948. Cortese married Richard Basehart, her co-star in The House on Telegraph Hill, in 1951, and had one son with him, the actor Jackie Basehart; they divorced in 1960. She never remarried. Jackie Basehart died in Milan in 2015, predeceasing Cortese.

At Dartmouth, Devens participated on three varsity teams: field hockey, ice hockey and lacrosse. She was named a captain of all three teams. She was considered by many teammates to be the best female athlete Dartmouth ever had. She was nicknamed "The Devil", for her ability to outlast other athletes in exercise routines. In her sophomore year, she was the co-winner of the Class of '76 Award at Dartmouth. As of the 2009-10 Dartmouth Big Green women's ice hockey season, Devens ranked 23rd in all-time scoring among Big Green women's ice hockey players.

Vincent Chukwuemeka Ike, born on April 28, 1931, in Ndikelionwu, Anambra State, Nigeria, to Charles and Dinah Ike, received his early education in his hometown before attending Ife-Mbaise and later, from 1945 to 1950, Government College Umuahia. At Umuahia, he began writing for the school magazine, *The Umuahian*, influenced by teachers like Saburi Biobaku. Notable contemporaries at the school included Chinua Achebe, Christopher Okigbo, and Ken Saro-Wiwa. After completing his secondary education, Ike attended University College, Ibadan (now the University of Ibadan), where he was invited by Chinua Achebe to join the magazine club. He earned a B.A. from the University of London in 1955. In 1959, he married Adebimpe Olurinsola Abimbolu. Ike's first novel, *Toads for Supper*, was published in 1965. He obtained his Master's degree from Stanford University in 1966. After the Nigerian Civil War, he served briefly as head of the University of Nigeria, Nsukka. In 1971, he became the chief executive of the West African Examinations Council (WAEC). In 2008, he became the Igwe (king) of Ndikelionwu. His only son, Prince Osita Ike, died in 2016.

The Critérium du Dauphiné is an eight-day stage race traditionally held in the Dauphiné region of southeastern France, encompassing portions of the Alps. The race has often started with an opening prologue on the first day, sometimes a fearsome uphill time trial. Stages are usually held in the lower hilly regions of Rhône-Alpes during the middle days, while the second half of the Dauphiné addresses mountainous terrain. An individual time trial is often included.

Starship is a second stage or long-duration spacecraft, forming the upper part of the rocket. The spacecraft is 50 m (160 ft) tall and has a dry mass of less than 100 t (220,000 lb). Starship's payload volume is about 1,000 m3 (35,000 cu ft), larger than the International Space Station's pressurized volume by 80 m3 (2,800 cu ft), and can be even bigger with an extended 22 m (72 ft)-tall volume.: 2 By refueling the Starship spacecraft in orbit using tanker spacecraft, Starship will be able to transport larger payloads and more astronauts to other Earth orbits, the Moon, and Mars.: 5

Johnson was married to stylist Keri Johnson whom he officially divorced in 2010. He began dating singer and actress Jessica Simpson in January 2010. They announced their engagement that November. Many Hmong immigrated to Michigan after the end of the Vietnam War in the 1970s. The Hmong had moved to Detroit in order to obtain employment and so members of the same families could live in the same area. Hmong people had migrated to Detroit from various places in the United States. As of that year, Metro Detroit's Hmong population is smaller than the major Hmong populations in California, Minnesota, and Wisconsin.

In the First Round Proper of the 1950-51 FA Cup, clubs from the Football League Third Division North and South joined the competition, along with non-league clubs who had successfully navigated the qualifying rounds. Two clubs from the Football League Third Division, Doncaster Rovers and Notts County, received a bye to the Third Round due to their strong finish in the previous season. In addition, Shrewsbury Town were given a bye to the Third Round for qualifying to the fifth round in the last season. Of the four new clubs admitted to the Football League this season, only Mansfield Town entered in the First Round Proper. Shrewsbury Town protested their initial requirement to start in the qualifying rounds. To ensure the correct number of matches, non-league club Blyth Spartans received a bye to the First Round Proper. The matches of the First Round Proper were played on Saturday, 25 November 1950.

The series, originally titled *Vida*, was first announced as being in development on July 27, 2017, during the Starz executive session at the Television Critics Association (TCA) press tour. PMS5 is an algorithm used in planted motif search (PMS). It extends the algorithm FULLPRUNE to solve the q-PMS problem. Given a set of strings S, PMS5 computes B<sub>d</sub>(x, y, z), the intersection of the d-neighborhoods of three l-mers x, y, and z within S. Here, S is the alphabet of interest, and L refers to the length of the l-mers.

*Washing Machine* received largely positive reviews from music critics, with many considering it a return to form for Sonic Youth. Stephen Thomas Erlewine of AllMusic described the album as their "most adventurous, challenging, and best record since *Daydream Nation*," noting a return to the fearless exploration of their SST Records era, but within tighter song structures. Erlewine further opined that the music "demands attention, never taking predictable paths or easy choices," suggesting that *Washing Machine* showcased Sonic Youth still had things to say. Peter Margasak of *CMJ New Music Monthly* highlighted "The Diamond Sea" as the album's centerpiece, a track whose length Thurston Moore joked was unsurprising, stating he wished "every song would be 20 minutes long."

The mountain is located on the border between Piedmont and Aosta Valley, on the watershed dividing Valle dell'Orco from Valsavarenche, the latter tributary of the Dora Baltea. A 3.112 m high saddle divides Punta bes from Punta Leynir, while the Orco/Valsavarenche ridge continues towards the Colle del Nivolet. The Punta Bes, due to its location, offers a good view on the nearby massif of Gran Paradiso.

Cardiff University's current coat of arms was granted by the College of Arms in 1988 following the merger of University College Cardiff and the University of Wales Institute of Science and Technology. The coat of arms incorporates features from the heraldry of both former institutions. The three chevrons are derived from the arms of the de Clare lords of Glamorgan. The open book signifies learning; on it are the crescent and annulet, marks of cadence that indicate that University College Cardiff was the second of the University of Wales' institutions, and that the University of Wales Institute of Science and Technology was the fifth.

The Helmand River, the longest in Afghanistan at 1,150 km, originates in the Hindu Kush and ends in Hamun-i-Helmand in the Sistan and Baluchistan province of Iran. One of the river's primary arms crosses through Lashkargah, lending the city a riverside atmosphere and providing a pleasant setting for picnics. The river is deep enough to allow for various water sports, including swimming and boating, with boats available for rent. Mirwais Nika Park was recently built on the river's bank. On the opposite side of the city lies a large thicket inhabited by diverse species of trees, birds, mammals, and reptiles.

Alpine meadow-rue has a circumboreal distribution, occurring in northern Europe and Asia, Alaska, northern Canada, and Greenland, as well as mountain ranges further south. Its natural habitats include tundra, open birch woodland, stream and river banks, lake shores, alpine meadows, and boggy areas. It is occasionally found on fens among and on the fringes of coniferous forests.

Aitken's Gap, later known as Buttlejorrk, developed due to its location on the route to the goldfields. The *Argus* reported on 21 October 1852 that bushrangers robbed a gentleman at Aitken's Gap. Three men were involved in the robbery, which occurred at 10 a.m. As a result of its location, a scene of bustle and uproar developed at Aitken's Gap. William Kelly arrived at Aitken's Gap in 1852, travelling by bullock cart. He observed diggers returning from the diggings with their gold, which contributed to the scene of bustle and uproar.

Nyuju Stumpy Brown was born 1 July 1924 on the Canning Stock Route in Ngapawarlu, Western Australia. She grew up in the Great Sandy Desert. She was also known as Kumanjayi Brown. Giosue Giuppone began his sporting career in bicycling competitions. He later transitioned to motorcycle racing in the early 1900s, most notably racing for Peugeot. As part of the 'Peugeot automobiles' factory team, he participated in track racing, driving large-capacity, ultra-lightweight racers that typically weighed around 50kg with unlimited engine capacity, at times as much as two litres. *The Forging of a Rebel*, an autobiographical trilogy by Arturo Barea, was originally written in Spanish. Ilsa Barea, Arturo's wife, translated the work into English. The first edition was published in English by Faber & Faber in three parts between 1941 and 1946: *The Forge*, *The Track*, and *The Clash*. The trilogy garnered high praise from critics, including George Orwell, who dryly noted that Barea was "one of the most valuable of the literary acquisitions that England has made as a result of Fascist persecution." The trilogy was an immediate international success and was translated into nine languages during the forties. The Spanish text did not appear until 1951, when it was published in Buenos Aires by an Argentinian publishing house.

The date of construction of Lisbon's first pillory is unknown, but was probably in or before 1179 following the granting of a charter to Lisbon by Afonso I of Portugal. The earliest written records date for that structure date to late 14th century. Later records place the pillory at various places in the city; but all traces of this and many other structures were destroyed in the Lisbon earthquake of 1755.

The New Zealand General Service Medal 2002 (NZGSM 2002) was established by royal warrant to recognize service since 2000. The NZGSM 2002 (Iraq 2015) was authorized by regulation on 28 July 2016. Individuals eligible for award of the medal will have served in the operational area since 7 December 2014 as part of or with New Zealand Government deployment for general regional security operations. These eligible individuals will have been members of the New Zealand Defence Force, New Zealand police employees, employees or contractors of a government department who are New Zealand citizens or residents, and any others who the Minister of Defence, on the advice of the Chief of Defence Force, determines is eligible for the medal.
